## lattice-system

A Lean 4 + Mathlib formalization project targeting general lattice models.
This project subsumes and generalizes the earlier
[ising-model](https://github.com/phasetr/ising-model) project, progressively
covering classical spin systems, quantum spin systems, Hubbard, BCS,
CAR algebras, and eventually lattice QCD.

## Design axis: graphs, not lattices

Despite the name, the **primary combinatorial abstraction in this
library is a graph `(╬Ť, E_╬Ť)`** ÔÇö finite for finite-volume work and
infinite for the thermodynamic-limit / algebraic-formulation work
that is a major long-term goal ÔÇö not "a lattice". Concrete lattices
(the 1D chain, square / cubic grids, infinite chains, ÔäĄ^d, ÔÇŽ) appear
only as specific instances such as `SimpleGraph.pathGraph`,
`SimpleGraph.cycleGraph`, products of these, or their infinite
analogues. This convention follows the standard mathematical-physics
literature on many-body systems on graphs (Lieb's theorem on
bipartite lattices, the MarshallÔÇôLiebÔÇôMattis theorem, Miyao 2021
┬ž3, ÔÇŽ) and aligns the project with mathlib's `SimpleGraph`
foundations.

Finite-volume work uses `╬Ť : Type*` together with `[Fintype ╬Ť]`
when needed (e.g. for traces, partition functions, finite sums of
local terms); infinite-volume work drops the `Fintype` assumption
and uses graphs over types like `ÔäĄ` or `ÔäĄ^d` instead.

The bridge from a `SimpleGraph` to the pairwise coupling
`J : ╬Ť Ôćĺ ╬Ť Ôćĺ Ôäé` consumed by `heisenbergHamiltonian` (and similar
operators) lives in `LatticeSystem.Lattice.Graph` (`couplingOf`).
Existing chain coupling families (`openChainCoupling`,
`periodicChainCoupling`) are characterised as
`couplingOf (pathGraph _) _` and `couplingOf (cycleGraph _) _`
respectively.

## Scope

| Area | Stage | Typical references |
|---|---|---|
| Classical spin systems | Inherited from ising-model | Friedli-Velenik, Glimm-Jaffe |
| Quantum spin systems | Current focus | Tasaki, Nielsen-Chuang (cross-check) |
| Hubbard / BCS | Medium term | Tasaki 1998, Bru-Pedra |
| CAR-algebraic formulation | Medium-long term | Araki-Moriya, Bru-Pedra |
| Thermodynamic limit (infinite graphs) | Long term, **major project goal** | Simon, Friedli-Velenik, Bratteli-Robinson |
| Lattice QCD | Longest term | Aarts, Davies |

## Refactoring conventions and review criteria

A **single-source-of-truth document** for refactoring conventions
applied as the review checklist on every pull request:
[Refactoring conventions and review criteria](refactoring-conventions.html).
Topics: test methods (decide / bridge / small-exhaustive / shim /
`#guard_msgs`), module-split criteria, generic / dedup conventions,
deprecation window policy, naming / docstring rules, linter
exceptions, public-doc synchronisation.

The companion page [Deprecation window](deprecations.html) tracks
currently-deprecated public declarations, the `since` date,
recommended replacement, and earliest-removal window.

unique-up-to-positive-scalar strictly positive PerronÔÇôFrobenius eigenvector ÔÇö equivalent to the matrix-level Tasaki (2.5.4) ground-state expansion `╬ú_¤â c_¤â \|╬Ę╠â^¤âÔčę` with `c_¤â > 0`. Complex-form bridge: complex sector matrix Hermiticity + real-Ôćö-complex eigenvector correspondence (#857, #858, #861). Generic spin `S`, arbitrary bipartite-antiferromagnetic Heisenberg coupling supported on a connected bipartite graph, with the intermediate-existence hypothesis. The next step is the lift from the magnetization sector to the FULL Hilbert space ÔÇö comparing ground-state energies across magnetization sectors. | Done |
| P1n (Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, final statement) | **Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3 (LiebÔÇôMattis, general spin-S, `\|A\| Ôëá \|┬ČA\|`) ÔÇö final statement.** The current `Prop` definition `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` is the structural, `h_intermediate`-free statement: it closes the conclusion from the physical hypotheses `(1 ÔëĄ N, 1 ÔëĄ \|A\|, 1 ÔëĄ \|┬ČA\|)` rather than the older vacuous-at-`N=1` intermediate-support hypothesis. The canonical proof witnesses are `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_bipartiteToy` (PR #3891, toy coupling) and `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_of_bipartiteCompletePositive` (PR #3893, general bipartite `J` positive on the complete bipartite graph). These declarations are the structurally repaired replacements for the older `h_intermediate` capstones removed in PR #3917. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 | Truly-unconditional closure at all `N Ôëą 1` (PR #3891 toy, PR #3893 general) |
| P1o (Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, MattisÔÇôNishimori anisotropic) | **Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4 (MattisÔÇôNishimori)**: ground-state uniqueness + `┼ť┬│_tot \|╬Ž_GSÔčę = 0` for the anisotropic XXZ + single-ion Hamiltonian (2.5.14) on a connected bipartite lattice with `\|A\| = \|B\|`, under (i) `Ôłĺ1 < ╬╗ ÔëĄ 1, D Ôëą 0` or (ii) `╬╗ Ôëą 1, D ÔëĄ 0`. The model is only U(1)-invariant (not SU(2)), so the proof uses even/odd-sector PerronÔÇôFrobenius (at most double degeneracy) + a deformation argument from the SU(2) point `(╬╗,D)=(1,0)` rather than MLM directly. bridge giving per-block submatrix-full intersection finrank equality, (h.2) #3841 strengthens involution decomposition to finrank equality, (h.3) #3842 combines (h.1)+(h.2) into `finrank Ôäé (eig M ╬╝) = ÔłĹ_p finrank Ôäé (eig M.submatrix_p ╬╝)` for parity-block-diagonal `M` commuting with `P`, (h.4) #3844 submatrix-based ÔëĄ 2 wrappers. **(i) chain: Hermitian spectral chain** (#3846ÔÇô#3855): (i.1) #3846 submatrix Hermiticity, (i.2) #3847 submatrix eigenvalue realness (`╬╝.im = 0`), (i.3) #3848 submatrix minimum eigenvalue exists, (i.4) #3849 Hermitian eigenspace `= ÔŐą` below min, (i.5) #3850 block-diag eigenspace `= ÔŐą` below joint per-block min, (i.6) #3851 full eig `ÔëĄ 2` at min(per-block mins) given per-block `ÔëĄ 1` at min, (i.7) #3854 bare `─Ą'` specialisation, (i.8) #3855 hermitianMinEigenvalue` hypothesis explicit). **Target balanced-sector PF discharge** (Issue #3739): off-diagonal anisotropic/Heisenberg agreement, real sector matrices, the Marshall-dressed shifted anisotropic sector matrix, structural irreducibility, and CollatzÔÇôWielandt PF/minimum identification now provide the balanced-sector `finrank Ôäé ÔëĄ 1` input at the spin-1/2 target point and in the general spin-S target sector; the PF-free target wrappers no longer take an explicit `h_balanced_sector_pf` callback. **Strict-gap, SU(2)-uniqueness, and MLM endpoint wrappers** now replace the explicit balanced/full equality input by the standard strict gap over all non-balanced non-empty magnetization sectors, derive that strict gap from SU(2)-endpoint global uniqueness, and then construct the SU(2) uniqueness input from the general Theorem 2.3 MLM/Casimir/PF endpoint. **D-boundary extension**: the spin-1/2 parity-block route has a dedicated `D Ôëą 0` version because single-ion `┬▒2` moves are impossible for `Fin (1+1)`. The general spin-S route now also has a bond-only parity reachability replacement for the single-ion branch, giving general `D Ôëą 0` parity-block irreducibility and the MLM/Casimir target wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_D_nonneg_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_D_nonneg_general`. **╬╗=1 boundary**: at spin `1/2`, `(S^3)^2 = (1/4)I`, so `singleIonAnisotropyS D 1` is a scalar shift and `anisotropicHeisenbergS J 1 D 1` reduces to the Heisenberg Hamiltonian plus `D|╬Ť|/4`; the SU(2) Theorem 2.3/MLM uniqueness transfers to target uniqueness and zero magnetization at `╬╗ = 1`. For general spin-S, the SU(2)-point wrappers cover `╬╗ = 1`, `D = 0`, and for `2 ÔëĄ N` the ion-only parity reachability route gives the `╬╗ = 1`, `D > 0` endpoint wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_lambda_one_D_pos_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_lambda_one_D_pos_general`. **Case (ii) bridges**: the case-(ii) path-region lemmas show the deformation path stays in `╬╗ Ôëą 1`, `D ÔëĄ 0`; the first case-(ii) target wrappers reuse the balanced-sector PF bridge while leaving balanced-sector/full-ground equality plus full `finrank <= 2` explicit; the strict-gap wrappers replace the direct balanced-sector/full-ground equality input by the strict sector gap; the no-full-finrank wrappers then use sector projection and Hermitian below-min exclusion to remove the full `finrank <= 2` input once strict gap is supplied; the crossing-callback bridge compresses the remaining strict-gap derivation to a single target crossing contradiction callback; the path-callback bridge reduces that target callback to a contradiction for crossings along the case-(ii) path; the crossing-set bridge reduces the path callback to contradiction for non-empty `perMCrossingSet M Ôłę Icc 0 1`; the first-crossing bridge reduces crossing-set non-emptiness to contradiction at `sInf (perMCrossingSet M Ôłę Icc 0 1)`; the argmin-first-crossing bridge reduces the target contradiction to the sector whose first crossing minimises those `sInf` values; the first-crossing finrank bridge proves that argmin callback from a `finrank <= 2` bound at the selected first crossing; the path-global finrank bridge moves this to every `t Ôłł Icc 0 1`; the block-path finrank bridge reduces it to full-ground-energy parity-block submatrix simplicity; and the block-PF/min bridge reduces that to pathwise bare parity-block PF simplicity plus PF/min identification; and the parity-gauge sign layer introduces the case-(ii) block gauge needed to flip exactly the ┬▒2 parity-bond and single-ion moves; and the local sign layer proves strict shifted-entry positivity for transverse, parity-bond, and single-ion elementary moves; and the block reachability, block non-negativity, step-support, raw support-classification, and boundary move-set bridges reduce parity-block irreducibility to conditional irreducibility under the bipartite support-zero assumption on `J`. | The public general spin-S `2 ÔëĄ N` parameter-region wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_general` package the general spin-S surface. The spin-`1/2` wrappers `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf` and `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf` now package the exact `N = 1` surface: `-1 < ╬╗ < 1`, `D Ôëą 0`; `╬╗ = 1`, using the scalar-shift boundary; and strict case (ii) `1 < ╬╗`, `D ÔëĄ 0`. |

with `#ÔćĹ=#Ôćô+1`, `#ÔćĹ+#Ôćô=Ne`), the real form `tJEffReMatrix = (tJEffMatrix).re`, its sector restriction `tJEffReMatrixOnSector = submatrix Subtype.val`, and `tJEffReMatrixOnSector_isSymm` or an adjacent antiparallel spin exchange `tJSpinSwap`), with `tJSpinSwap_count` (the swap is `s Ôłś Equiv.swap i j`, a site bijection) and `tJStep_preserves_sector` (a step keeps `#ÔćĹ`/`#Ôćô`, so combining (`tJEffMatrix_re_neg_of_step`), for `¤ä,J > 0` every `TJStep s s'` produces `M_{s',s}.re < 0` (a hop `ÔëĄ Ôłĺ¤ä`, an exchange `ÔëĄ ÔłĺJ/2`), i.e. `B_{s',s} = ÔłĺM_{s',s} > 0`. **The adjacent-swap bridge is in place** (`TJAdjacentSwap.lean`): an `AdjacentSwapStep` (exchange the values at an adjacent pair of distinct sites, `= s Ôłś Equiv.swap a b`) is always a `TJStep` (`adjacentSwapStep_to_TJStep` ÔÇö a hop if one site is empty, an exchange if the two are opposite spins), so `AdjacentSwapReachable` lifts to `TJStep`-reachability (`tjReachable_of_adjacentSwapReachable`); the sector connectivity then reduces to the model-agnostic combinatorial fact that two `Fin 3`-configs with equal value-counts are adjacent-swap reachable. **The reachability basics are in place** (`TJSwapReachableBasics.lean`): `adjacentSwapReachable_swap` (the value-swapped `s Ôłś Equiv.swap a b` is reachable), `adjacentSwapReachable_count` (reachability preserves every value-count, so it stays in-sector), and `exists_needed_value_right_of_same_counts` (equal counts + prefix agreement Ôč╣ the needed value `s' p` sits right of `p` ÔÇö the bubble target). **The sector connectivity is now proven** (`TJSwapReachable.lean`): `bubble_reachable` (move a value leftward to a target by NN swaps, prefix-preserving, induction on the gap) + `reach_of_agree_aux` (selection sort: bubble the needed value into the leftmost disagreement, recurse) assemble into **`adjacentSwapReachable_of_same_counts`** ÔÇö any two `Fin 3`-configs with equal value-counts are adjacent-swap reachable, hence (via the bridge) `TJStep`-reachable within a sector. **The shifted PF matrix is set up** (`TJSectorShifted.lean`): `tJSectorShifted = c┬Ě1 Ôłĺ M` with `tJSectorShifted_isSymm`, `tJSectorShifted_nonneg` (entrywise `Ôëą 0` for `c` above the diagonal), `tJStep_ne` (a step changes the config), and `tJSectorShifted_pos_of_step` (a `TJStep` between (`#Ôłů+#ÔćĹ+#Ôćô=N+1`) + `tJSector_same_counts` (distinct sector states share all value-counts) feed the connectivity, and `tJSectorShifted_pow_pos_of_reachable` lifts a `TJStep`-reachable chain to a Ne)` witness (the `┼ť┬│=┬Ż` filling with `ÔćĹ`'s left of `Ôćô`'s) supplies A.18's `[Nonempty]`, and **`tJEffReMatrixOnSector_perronFrobenius`** applies Theorem A.18 to `M = tJEffReMatrixOnSector` ÔÇö The highest weight `╬ę` generates the `Ne+1` LI tower `(┼ťÔü╗)^k ╬ę` (`highestWeight_spinMultiplet_general`); each member is again a ground state (`┼ťÔü╗` commutes `─Ą_tJ`/`N╠é`, preserves hard-core), so `LinearIndependent.of_comp` + `fintype_card_le_finrank` give the bound; rests only on A.17. **E5 ladder injectivity (`TJLadderInjective.lean`) is COMPLETE**: the SU(2) norm identity `ÔÇľ┼ťÔü╗vÔÇľ┬▓=ÔÇľ┼ťÔü║vÔÇľ┬▓+2szÔÇľvÔÇľ┬▓` (`fermionTotalSpin_ladder_norm`, from `[┼ťÔü║,┼ťÔü╗]=2┼ť┬│`+`(┼ťÔü╗)ß┤┤=┼ťÔü║`) gives `┼ťÔü╗` injective on weight `sz>0` and `┼ťÔü║` on `sz<0` ÔÇö no rep theory ÔÇö embedding each `┼ť┬│`-weight space of `G` into `┼ť┬│=┬▒┬Ż`, the key to the upper bound `finrankÔëĄNe+1`; fully axiom-free. **E5b weight-finrank step (`TJGroundWeightFinrank.lean`) is COMPLETE**: `tJ_ground_weight_finrank_le_succ` ÔÇö for `szÔëą0`, `finrank (GÔŐô┼ť┬│=sz+1) ÔëĄ finrank (GÔŐô┼ť┬│=sz)` (`┼ťÔü╗` injects the `sz+1` weight space into the `sz` one, preserving `G` via `fermionTotalSpinMinus_mulVec_mem_groundSubmodule` and lowering the weight by one); iterating to `┬Ż` (and up via `┼ťÔü║`) bounds every weight space by the `┼ť┬│=┬Ż` block ÔëĄ1. **The `┼ťÔü║` mirror (`TJGroundWeightFinrankRaise.lean`) is COMPLETE**: `tJ_ground_weight_finrank_le_of_spinZ_neg` ÔÇö for `sz<0`, `finrank (GÔŐô┼ť┬│=sz) ÔëĄ finrank (GÔŐô┼ť┬│=sz+1)` (`┼ťÔü║` injects `sz` into `sz+1`), so negative-weight blocks are bounded by `┼ť┬│=┬Ż` from below too. **Every weight block ÔëĄ1 (`TJGroundWeightOne.lean`) is COMPLETE**: `tJ_ground_weight_finrank_le_one_pos/_neg` ÔÇö `finrank (GÔŐô┼ť┬│=┬Ż+k) ÔëĄ 1` and `finrank (GÔŐô┼ť┬│=Ôłĺ┬ŻÔłĺk) ÔëĄ 1` (`k:ÔäĽ`), by iterating the two steps to the `┼ť┬│=┬Ż` block (ÔëĄ1, E3a); the half-integers exhaust the `┼ť┬│` spectrum at odd filling. **The `┼ť┬│` weight decomposition (`TJGroundWeightDirectSum.lean`) is COMPLETE**: `fermionTotalSpinZ_mulVec_mem_groundSubmodule` (`┼ť┬│` preserves `G`), `fermionTotalSpinZ_iSup_eigenspace_eq_top` (`┼ť┬│` diagonal on the computational basis, so its eigenspaces span `ÔŐĄ`), and `tJ_groundSubmodule_eq_iSup_inf_eigenspace` ÔÇö `G = ÔĘć ╬╝, G ÔŐô eigenspace ┼ť┬│ ╬╝` (`Submodule.eq_iSup_inf_genEigenspace` for the invariant `G`); **The finite `┼ť┬│` weight reindexing (`TJGroundWeightReindex.lean`) is COMPLETE**: `tJ_groundSubmodule_inf_eigenspace_eq_bot` (off-weight blocks vanish ÔÇö `G ÔŐô eigenspace ┼ť┬│ ╬╝ = ÔŐą` for `╬╝` outside `{a Ôłĺ Ne/2 : a Ôłł Fin (Ne+1)}`) and `tJ_groundSubmodule_eq_iSup_weight` ÔÇö `G = ÔĘć a : Fin (Ne+1), G ÔŐô eigenspace ┼ť┬│ (a Ôłĺ Ne/2)` (the all-`Ôäé` supremum collapses to the `Ne+1` occurring half-integer weights); **The degeneracy upper bound (`TJGroundDegeneracyUpper.lean`) is COMPLETE**: `tJ_groundSubmodule_finrank_le` ÔÇö `finrank G ÔëĄ Ne+1` (the `Ne+1` half-integer weight blocks are independent (`eigenspaces_iSupIndep`), hence an internal direct sum of `G` (`DirectSum.coeLinearMap` injective + `finrank_directSum`), and each block is ÔëĄ1 (#4309), so `finrank G = ÔłĹ finrank (block) ÔëĄ Ne+1`); paired with the SU(2)-tower lower bound (`tJ_groundSubmodule_finrank_ge`, #4305) this pins `finrank G = Ne+1`. **Proposition 11.24 is now PROVED** (`proposition_11_24` in `TJProposition1124.lean`, E6 capstone): `IsMaximalSpinMultipletSubmodule N (groundSubmoduleAtFilling ÔÇŽ) Ne` ÔÇö `finrank G = Ne+1` (le_antisymm of #4305/#4312) and every ground state is an `(┼ť_tot)┬▓` eigenvector at `(Ne/2)(Ne/2+1)` (the `Ne+1` LI tower states `(┼ťÔü╗)^k ╬ę` span `G`, each maximal-spin via `highestWeight_spinMultiplet_general`). **This discharges the former `axiom proposition_11_24`**; the entire d=1 ferromagnetic t-J ferromagnetism result rests only on Theorem A.17 (**now discharged** ┬žA.3.2). Then the total-spin identification (`S_tot = Ne/2`) via the highest weight raised this way, the SU(2)-tower lower bound (paired with this `┼ť┬│=┬Ż` block bound for the upper bound), and the capstone replacing the axiom. The discharge lemmas add no new t-J axioms; the only inherited dependency is the A.17 sector reduction `tJHamiltonian_eigenstate_spin_zero_or_half` (which rests on Theorem A.17 (`exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate`, **now discharged**) axiom), every other layer being fully axiom-free.**); **generic fixed-filling hard-core ground subspace (`GroundSubspaceAtFilling.lean`)**: `fillingHardcoreStates`/`groundEnergyAtFilling`/`groundSubmoduleAtFilling` (`H`-eigenspace at the ground energy ÔŐô `N╠é=Ne` ÔŐô `hubbardHardcoreSubspace`), shared by Prop 11.24 and Thm 11.26; axiom-free; **┬ž11.5.3 d=1 decorated Hubbard model + Lemma 11.25 (documented axiom) + **Theorem 11.26 (PROVED, `MetallicFerroModel.lean`, Issue #4314)**: the duplicated-internal-site lattice `╬Ť=EÔł¬(I├Ś{1,2})` in `Fin(3K+3)` (`decExternalSite`/`decInternalSite1/2`); localized states `decAlpha`/`decBeta1`/`decBeta2` (eqs. (11.5.7)ÔÇô(11.5.9)) + fermion ops `decA/BCreation/Annihilation` (eq. (11.5.10)); `decHopping` (`t ╬ú b╠éÔÇáb╠é Ôłĺ s ╬ú_{ÔčĘp,qÔčęÔłłE,¤â}├óÔÇá_p├ó_q`, eq. (11.5.14)) + `decInteraction` (`U ╬ú n╠éÔćĹn╠éÔćô`, eq. (11.5.13)) + `decHubbardHamiltonian`; **`axiom lemma_11_25`** (`t,UÔćĹÔł×` Hubbard Ôëí `JÔćĹÔł×` t-J at `¤ä=(1+4╬Ż┬▓)s`, rendered as spin-structure transfer: Hubbard ground subspace is the maximal-spin `Ne+1`-multiplet **iff** the t-J one is, in the limits; **discharge in progress, Issue #4314**: with Prop 11.24 proved, the first step `TJHalfFillingKinetic.lean` proves the t-J kinetic term vanishes at half-filling `Ne=K+1` ÔÇö `tJ_kinetic_sandwich_mulVec_tJConfigOf_eq_zero_of_full`, `P╠éhc K P╠éhc |╬Ž_sÔčę=0` for fully-occupied `s`, so `─Ą_tJ|half` reduces to the ferromagnetic Heisenberg model; PR2 `TJHalfFillingExchange.lean` completes the reduction `tJHamiltonian_mulVec_tJConfigOf_eq_of_full`: `─Ą_tJ|╬Ž_sÔčę = J┬ĚtJExchange|╬Ž_sÔčę` for fully-occupied `s`; PR3a `TJAllUpSpinDot.lean` computes `fermionSpinDot_mulVec_allUpState` `┼ť_i┬Ě┼ť_j|ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę=┬╝|ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę` (iÔëáj), so the all-up state has exchange energy 0; PR3b `TJAllUpGround.lean` then proves `tJHamiltonian_mulVec_allUpState_eq_zero` `─Ą_tJ|ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę=0` at half-filling ÔÇö the maximal-spin `|ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę` is a zero-energy eigenstate; PR3c-prep `TJSingletAnnihilation.lean` adds the singlet annihilator `╬ö_xy=─ë_{yÔćô}─ë_{xÔćĹ}Ôłĺ─ë_{yÔćĹ}─ë_{xÔćô}` (`╬ö_xy|ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę=0`), towards the bond identity; PR3c `TJExchangeBondPSD.lean` proves the CAR identity `tJSingletAnnihilation_conjTranspose_mul_self` `╬ö_xyÔÇá╬ö_xy = n╠é_{xÔćĹ}n╠é_{yÔćô}+n╠é_{xÔćô}n╠é_{yÔćĹ}Ôłĺ┼ťÔü║_x┼ťÔü╗_yÔłĺ┼ťÔü╗_x┼ťÔü║_y` (xÔëáy) + cross-site commutes `TJCrossSiteSpinCommute.lean`, the content behind the half-bond identity; PR3d `TJExchangeBondHalf.lean` proves `tJExchangeBond_eq_half_singletNormSq` `n╠é_xn╠é_y/4Ôłĺ┼ť_x┬Ě┼ť_y=┬Ż╬ö_xyÔÇá╬ö_xy` (xÔëáy) and `tJExchangeBond_posSemidef` (the bond is positive-semidefinite) ÔÇö the per-bond PSD input; PR3e `TJExchangePSD.lean` proves `tJExchange_posSemidef` `(tJExchange N G).PosSemidef` (graph sum of per-bond PSD terms via `Finset.sum_induction`) ÔÇö the operator nonnegativity behind the half-filling ground energy 0; PR3f `TJHalfFillingReduction.lean` extends to the whole sector ÔÇö `tJFillingSector_full` (Ne=N+1 Ôč╣ fully occupied), `tJ_kinetic_sandwich_mulVec_eq_zero_of_filling`, and `tJHamiltonian_mulVec_eq_smul_tJExchange_of_filling` (`─Ą_tJ v=J┬ĚtJExchange v` for hard-core N╠é=N+1 v); PR3g `TJAllUpProperties.lean` (`N╠é|ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę=(N+1)|ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę`, hardcore, Ôëá0) + `TJHalfFillingGroundEnergy.lean` `tJ_groundEnergyAtFilling_eq_zero` (`groundEnergyAtFilling ─Ą_tJ (N+1)=0`: all-up at 0, PSD Ôçĺ rayleighÔëą0); axiom-free); PR3h `TJHalfFillingDegeneracyLower.lean` `tJ_halfFilling_groundSubmodule_finrank_ge` (`N+2 ÔëĄ finrank` of the half-filling ground subspace: the all-up state is a highest-weight ground state, so the SU(2) lowering tower yields `N+2` LI ground states `(┼ťÔü╗)^k|ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę`; mirrors #4305; axiom-free); PR3i `TJHalfFillingBondAction.lean` `tJExchangeBond_mulVec_tJConfigOf_full` (the exchange bond is a half spin-swap on the filled sector: `(┬╝ n╠é_x n╠é_y Ôłĺ ┼ť_x┬Ě┼ť_y)|╬Ž_sÔčę=┬Ż(|╬Ž_sÔčęÔłĺ|╬Ž_{tJSpinSwap s x y}Ôčę)` via the sign-free swap over the 4 spin cases; ground states of `tJExchange` have spin-swap-invariant bond amplitudes ÔÇö toward the degeneracy upper bound; axiom-free); PR3i-2 `TJHalfFillingBondGround.lean` `tJ_ground_bond_mulVec_eq_zero` (a half-filling ground state is annihilated by every adjacent bond: `bond_xy v=0` via the PSD sum `ÔčĘv,tJExchange vÔčę=0` Ôçĺ each `ÔčĘv,bond_xy vÔčę=0` Ôçĺ `╬ö_xy v=0`; axiom-free); PR3i-3a `TJHalfFillingAmplitude.lean` `tJ_ground_amplitude_swap_invariant` (half-filling ground amplitudes are spin-swap invariant: `v(tJConfigOf s)=v(tJConfigOf(tJSpinSwap s x y))` for adjacent bonds, via filling-basis expansion + the bond half-swap action + `bond_xy v=0`; `tJFillingSwap` sector permutation; axiom-free); PR3i-3b `TJHalfFillingUpCount.lean` `tJ_ground_amplitude_eq_of_same_upCount` (half-filling ground amplitudes depend only on the up-count: equal up-counts Ôč╣ equal value-counts Ôč╣ adjacent-swap reachable [`adjacentSwapReachable_of_same_counts`] Ôč╣ amplitudes equal by reachability induction over the per-bond swap invariance; axiom-free); PR3i-3c `TJHalfFillingDegeneracyUpper.lean` `tJ_halfFilling_groundSubmodule_finrank_le` (**upper bound `finrank G ÔëĄ N+2`**: ground states determined by amplitudes constant on the N+2 up-count classes Ôč╣ injective `G Ôć¬ (Fin(N+2)ÔćĺÔäé)`; with the lower bound #4326 Ôč╣ `finrank G = N+2`; axiom-free); **PR3-cap `TJHalfFillingMaximalSpin.lean` `tJ_halfFilling_isMaximalSpinMultiplet`** (the half-filling t-J ground subspace is the maximal-spin `(N+2)`-fold multiplet: `IsMaximalSpinMultipletSubmodule N G (N+1)`, assembling `finrank=N+2` with the all-up SU(2) tower spanning `G`; boundary case of Theorem 11.26's t-J side; axiom-free, no A.17); **PR-unify `TJMaximalSpinUnified.lean` `tJ_isMaximalSpinMultiplet_of_le`** (the d=1 t-J ground subspace is the maximal-spin multiplet for ALL odd `Ne ÔëĄ K+1`: metallic `Ne<K+1` via `proposition_11_24` + half-filling `Ne=K+1`; the half-filling chain generalized to drop `0<N` so `K=0` is covered; t-J input to Theorem 11.26, rests on A.17 via Prop 11.24); **PR-final: `theorem_11_26` is now a PROVED theorem** (`MetallicFerroModel.lean`, Issue #4314): discharged from `lemma_11_25` (documented strong-coupling-equivalence axiom) + `tJ_isMaximalSpinMultiplet_of_le`; `#print axioms theorem_11_26` = `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound, lemma_11_25]` (A.17's `exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate` now discharged ┬žA.3.2). Tasaki ┬ž11.5.3 metallic-ferromagnetism Theorem 11.26 complete + **`theorem_11_26` (PROVED, Issue #4314)** (d=1, `NeÔëĄK+1=|E|` odd (Tasaki's `NÔëĄL`) Ôçĺ `IsMaximalSpinMultipletSubmodule (3K+2) (groundSubmoduleAtFilling (decHubbardHamiltonian ÔÇŽ) Ne) Ne` for large `t,U` ÔÇö ground `S_tot=Ne/2`, `Ne+1`-fold; metallic when `Ne<K+1`); discharged from `lemma_11_25` (documented axiom) + `tJ_isMaximalSpinMultiplet_of_le`, so the model and Theorem 11.26 are proved modulo `lemma_11_25` (A.17 now discharged); Theorem 11.27 still a documented axiom); **generic single-particle-state operators (`SpinfulVectorOperator.lean`)**: `spinfulCreationFromVector`/`spinfulAnnihilationFromVector` (`─ł_¤â(¤ć)=╬ú_x ¤ć(x)─ë_{x,¤â}`), shared helper; axiom-free; **┬ž11.5.4 TanakaÔÇôTasaki model + Theorem 11.27 (AXIOMATIZED, `TanakaTasakiModel.lean`, Issue #4198) ÔÇö ┬ž11.5/Chapter 11 capstone**: the heavily-decorated lattice `╬Ť=E├Ś{1,2,3}Ôł¬I├Ś{1,2}` (externals triplicated, internals duplicated), d=1 in `Fin(5K+5)` (`ttExtSite`/`ttIntSite`); special states `ttAlpha`/`ttBeta`/`ttDeltaP`/`ttDeltaI` (eqs. (11.5.19)ÔÇô(11.5.22), `├ó` with `1/ÔłÜ(3+4╬Ż┬▓)`) + ops via `spinfulCreationFromVector`; `ttHopping` (`╬ú_{ÔčĘp,qÔčę}(Ôłĺs ├óÔÇá├óÔłĺt b╠éÔÇáb╠é)+uÔéü╬úb╠éÔÇáb╠é+uÔéé╬úd╠éÔÇád╠é`, eq. (11.5.24)) + `ttInteraction` + `ttHamiltonian` (finite); genuine `uÔéé,UÔćĹÔł×` limit objects `ttDKernel` (`d╠é╬Ž=0`, via Theorem A.12) + `ttEffectiveHamiltonian` (`uÔéé,U=Ôł×` effective hopping); **`axiom theorem_11_27`** (d=1, `uÔéü>2(|s|+2|t|)`, `K+1ÔëĄNeÔëĄ2(K+1)` (Tasaki `L^dÔëĄNÔëĄ2L^d`) Ôçĺ **in the limit** every ground state in `groundSubmoduleAtFilling (ttEffectiveHamiltonian ÔÇŽ) Ne ÔŐô ttDKernel` has max spin `S_tot=Ne/2` ÔÇö taken as a genuine limit, not finite thresholds (Tasaki proves it only in the limit, warns finite `uÔéé,U` is not expected to work); weaker than the multiplet predicate, Tasaki claims only the spin; metallic when `1<Ne/L<2`); model axiom-free, `theorem_11_27` documented axiom ([63])) |

| Appendix A (Tasaki Mathematical Appendices) | The book-order content after Chapter 11; foundations for the deferred Chapter-11 proof discharges (frustration-free A.9/A.10, limit A.11/A.12, PerronÔÇôFrobenius A.17/A.18 ÔÇö the last largely already in `Math/PerronFrobenius*`/`CollatzWielandt*`). **Complete ÔÇö A.1ÔÇôA.28 all formalized in book order** (Issues [#4205](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4205) + [#4224](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4224), both closed; see the **status & axiomatization policy** note below the Roadmap): **Theorem A.1 (Lie product formula)** `e^{A+B} = lim_N (e^{A/N}e^{B/N})^N` **now proved (axiom-free)** (`Math/MatrixAnalysis/LieProduct.lean`, `lieProductFormula` ÔÇö mathlib has only the commuting case): generic `trotterProductFormula` in a complete normed `ÔäŁ`-algebra via exponential-series tail bounds (`ÔÇľe^XÔłĺ1ÔłĺXÔÇľ ÔëĄ ÔÇľXÔÇľ┬▓e^{ÔÇľXÔÇľ}` etc.) + telescoping power estimate (`ÔÇľCÔü┐ÔłĺDÔü┐ÔÇľ ÔëĄ n┬ĚM^{nÔłĺ1}┬ĚÔÇľCÔłĺDÔÇľ`) + `O(s┬▓)` product comparison + the exact `n`-th-power identity, instantiated for matrices under the scoped operator norm; **Lemmas A.4** (Hermitian `├é` is `Ôëą0` iff all eigenvalues `Ôëą0`), **A.5** (`├é,B╠éÔëą0 Ôçĺ ├é+B╠éÔëą0`), **A.6** (`B╠éÔÇáB╠éÔëą0`, and conversely every `├éÔëą0` is `─ł┬▓` for a *unique* PSD square root `─ł=ÔłÜ├é` via `cfc Real.sqrt`, existence+uniqueness) **proved** (axiom-free, `Math/PosSemidef/Basics.lean`). **Theorem A.7** (Weyl monotonicity: Hermitian `├éÔëĄB╠é` Ôçĺ `i`-th sorted eigenvalue `├é.eigenvaluesÔéÇ i ÔëĄ B╠é.eigenvaluesÔéÇ i`) **now proved (axiom-free)** via the CourantÔÇôFischer block/pigeonhole argument (`Math/MatrixAnalysis/CourantFischer.lean`: spectral Rayleigh-sum `reÔčĘx,TxÔčę=ÔłĹ╬╗ßÁóÔÇľÔčĘbßÁó,xÔčęÔÇľ┬▓` + block bounds + the top-`(i+1)`/bottom-`(cardÔłĺi)` eigenspaces meeting by finrank pigeonhole; `EigenvalueMonotone.lean`). **Corollary A.8** (`├éÔëĄB╠é Ôçĺ Tr e^├é ÔëĄ Tr e^B╠é`, eq A.2.31) **now proved (axiom-free)** (`MatrixAnalysis/TraceExpMonotone.lean`: spectral mapping `Tr e^├é = ╬ú e^{a_j}` via `Matrix.exp_conj` + `exp_diagonal` + `trace_mul_cycle`, then A.7 Weyl + `Real.exp` monotone). **Lemmas A.9/A.10** (frustration-free Hamiltonian `─Ą=╬ú─ąÔ▒╝`, `─ąÔ▒╝Ôëą╬ÁÔ▒╝`: A.9 simultaneous eigenstate Ôçĺ ground state at `╬ú╬ÁÔ▒╝`; A.10 converse) **proved** (axiom-free, `FrustrationFree.lean`). **Lemma A.11** (`├éÔëą0 Ôłž ÔčĘ╬Ž|├é|╬ŽÔčę=0 Ôçĺ ├é╬Ž=0`; `├é=B╠éÔÇáB╠é Ôçĺ B╠é╬Ž=0`; + angular-momentum corollary `─┤┬▓╬Ž=0 Ôçĺ ─┤ÔüŻßÁůÔüż╬Ž=0`) **proved** (axiom-free, `PosSemidef/Kernel.lean`, over the existing `RayleighPosSemidefKernel`). **Theorem A.12** (`─Ą_v=─ĄÔéÇ+vV╠é`, `V╠éÔëą0`: `vÔćĹÔł×` finite-energy eigenstate families converging to nonzero `╬Ž` satisfy the effective Schr├Âdinger eq `P╠éÔéÇ─ĄÔéÇ╬Ž=E╬Ž` on `HÔéÇ=ker V╠é` ÔÇö documented axiom, `EffectiveLimit.lean`, weak-form + Tendsto, non-vacuous finite-energyÔçĺeffective direction). **Appendix A.2 complete.** **┬žA.3 angular momentum**: **Lemma A.14** (su(2) ladder) ÔÇö operator identity `─┤Ôü╗─┤Ôü║=─┤┬▓Ôłĺ─┤┬│(─┤┬│+1)` (eq A.3.7) + ladder non-vanishing (`ÔłĺJÔëĄM<J`, `╬ŽÔëá0` Ôçĺ `─┤Ôü║╬ŽÔëá0`) + raising **membership** (`─┤Ôü║╬ŽÔłłH_{J,M+1}`, via `[─┤┬│,─┤Ôü║]=─┤Ôü║` + `[─┤┬▓,─┤Ôü║]=0`) + **lowering** direction (`─┤Ôü║─┤Ôü╗=─┤┬▓Ôłĺ─┤┬│(─┤┬│Ôłĺ1)` eq A.3.8, `─┤Ôü╗╬ŽÔëá0` for `ÔłĺJ<MÔëĄJ`, `─┤Ôü╗╬ŽÔłłH_{J,MÔłĺ1}`) **proved** ÔÇö A.14 fully done (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Ladder.lean`). **Lemma A.15 (spin bound, part 1)**: norm identities `ÔÇľ─┤┬▒╬ŽÔÇľ┬▓={J(J+1)ÔłĺM(M┬▒1)}ÔÇľ╬ŽÔÇľ┬▓` (eq A.3.9, Hermitian ─┤^╬▒) `angRaise/angLower_normSq` + `angMom_abs_le_J` (`ÔłĺJÔëĄMÔëĄJ`, i.e. `JÔłĺM,J+MÔëą0`) **proved** (axiom-free). **Lemma A.15 (integrality)** + **Theorem A.13 (`J = n/2`)**: ladder-termination `raiseIter` (iterated `─┤Ôü║`) with `raiseIter_eigenspace`/`raiseIter_ne_zero` gives `JÔłĺM Ôłł ÔäĄÔëą0` (`angMom_sub_mem_nat`); the gauge-reflected `(─┤┬╣,Ôłĺ─┤┬▓,Ôłĺ─┤┬│)` gives `J+M Ôłł ÔäĄÔëą0`, so `2J=(JÔłĺM)+(J+M)ÔłłÔäĄÔëą0`, i.e. `J=n/2` (`angMom_J_eq_half_nat`) **proved** (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Quantization.lean`). **Theorem A.16 (SU(2)-multiplet degeneracy)**: for SU(2)-invariant `─Ą` (`[─Ą,─┤ßÁů]=0`), a joint energy eigenstate in `H_{J,M0}` yields nonzero same-energy companions in every `H_{J,JÔłĺk}` (`kÔëĄ2J`) ÔÇö `ham_su2_multiplet` (energy `E` is Ôëą`(2J+1)`-fold degenerate), proved via raise-to-top + reflected-lowering ladder + `ham_mulVec_raiseIter` (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Multiplet.lean`). **Theorem A.17 (spin-0/1-2 sector suffices)**: every energy eigenvalue of an SU(2)-invariant `─Ą` has an eigenstate with `─┤┬│=0` or `─┤┬│=1/2` ÔÇö `ham_eigenstate_spin_zero_or_half` (corollary of A.16+A.13; the lone simultaneous-diagonalization step `exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate` is **now proved (axiom-free)** via the generic common eigenvector of two commuting Hermitian operators on an invariant subspace (`Math/CommutingHermitianEigenvector.lean`: `exists_common_eigenvector_of_isHermitian_commute`), the Casimir `─┤┬▓` being PSD and commuting with `─┤┬│`/`─Ą`, `AngularMomentum/SpinHalfSector.lean`). **Theorem A.18 (PerronÔÇôFrobenius, real symmetric)**: a real symmetric `M` with `(c┬Ě1ÔłĺM)` irreducible (off-diag `ÔëĄ0` + connectivity) has a nondegenerate lowest eigenvalue with a strictly positive eigenvector ÔÇö `perronFrobenius_real_symmetric` (reuses the project's CollatzÔÇôWielandt PF + eigenspace simplicity; the lowest-eigenvalue identification is the variational `|w|`-argument eq A.4.1), proved (axiom-free, `PerronFrobeniusSymmetric.lean`). **Theorems A.19/A.20 (polar + SVD)**: `matrix_polar_decomposition` (`A = W C`, `W` unitary, `C` PSD) and `matrix_singular_value_decomposition` (`A = U D VÔÇá`, `U,V` unitary, `D = diagonal d` with `d Ôëą 0`) ÔÇö **now proved (axiom-free)**: SVD built from the spectral theorem of `Aß┤┤A` (`eigenvectorUnitary` `V`, eigenvalues `╬╗_i Ôëą 0`, `d_i = ÔłÜ╬╗_i`), with the normalised images `u_i = d_iÔü╗┬╣┬ĚA v_i` extended to an orthonormal basis (`exists_orthonormalBasis_extension_of_card_eq`) giving `U`, and `A V = U D` columnwise; polar follows as `W = U Vß┤┤`, `C = V D Vß┤┤` (`MatrixAnalysis/Decomposition.lean`). **Theorems A.21/A.22 (Wigner)**: `wignerAutomorphism_unitary` (linear ÔłŚ-automorphism of `M(H)` Ôč╣ `╬ô(├é)=├ŤÔÇá├é├Ť`) + `wignerAutomorphism_antiunitary` (antilinear Ôč╣ `├ŤÔÇá├é╠ä├Ť`) + `wignerProjection` (rank-1-projection map preserving `Tr[P PÔÇ▓]` Ôč╣ (anti)unitary `V╠é`) ÔÇö documented axioms over `Matrix D D Ôäé` (`WignerTheorem.lean`, Issue #4224). **Definition A.23 + Theorem A.24 (states / BanachÔÇôAlaoglu)**: `IsState` (weak-ÔłŚ-continuous `¤ć : WeakDual Ôäé A` with `¤ć 1 = 1`, `0 ÔëĄ ¤ć(aÔÇáa)`) over an abstract unital C*-algebra + `stateSpace_isCompact` (state space weak-ÔłŚ compact, documented axiom) ÔÇö `CStarAlgebra/State.lean`. **Definitions A.25/A.27 + Theorem A.26 (ground states)**: dynamics modelled by a derivation `╬┤=[─Ą,┬Ě]`; `IsGroundState` (`0 ÔëĄ ¤ë(aÔÇá┬Ě╬┤a)`) + `HasNonzeroGap` (`╬│>0`, `¤ë(aÔÇá╬┤a) Ôëą ╬│¤ë(aÔÇáa)` on `¤ë(a)=0`) + `groundState_variational` (ground state Ôč║ `¤ë(─Ą_L)` least over states agreeing outside ╬Ť_L, documented axiom) ÔÇö `CStarAlgebra/GroundState.lean`. **Theorem A.28 (GNS construction)**: `gns_construction` ÔÇö every state `¤ü` on a C*-algebra has a GNS triple `(H_¤ü, ¤Ç_¤ü, ╬ę_¤ü)` (`ÔłŚ`-representation `¤Ç : A ÔćĺÔőćÔéÉ[Ôäé] (HÔćĺL H)` + cyclic `╬ę`) with `¤ü(├é)=ÔčĘ╬ę,¤Ç(├é)╬ęÔčę` and `{¤Ç(├é)╬ę}` dense; documented axiom (mathlib has the GNS machinery), `CStarAlgebra/GNS.lean`. **┬žA.6ÔÇô┬žA.7 (Wigner / states / ground states / GNS) complete Ôćĺ Appendix A complete.** Prove-first where mathlib supports, axiomatize-first for the heavy analytic results, strict book order (see the status & axiomatization-policy note below). |

### Appendix A: status and axiomatization policy

Tasaki's **Appendix A is fully formalized in book order (A.1ÔÇôA.28)**, and the
entire Tasaki text up to and including Chapter 11 (┬ž11.5) plus this appendix is
now covered. The appendix splits into two kinds of items:

- **Proved (axiom-free)** ÔÇö the linear-algebra and angular-momentum core that
  `mathlib` supports directly: **A.1** (the Lie product / Trotter formula, proved
  from scratch in a complete normed `ÔäŁ`-algebra), **A.4ÔÇôA.6**
  (positive-semidefinite basics + unique
  PSD square root), **A.7ÔÇôA.8** (Weyl eigenvalue monotonicity via CourantÔÇôFischer,
  and the trace-exp monotonicity through the spectral mapping for `exp`),
  **A.9ÔÇôA.11** (frustration-free Hamiltonians, energy-form
  kernels), **A.13ÔÇôA.16** (angular-momentum quantization `J = n/2`, the ladder,
  and SU(2)-multiplet degeneracy), **A.17** (spin-0/half sector via the common
  eigenvector of commuting Hermitians), **A.18** (PerronÔÇôFrobenius for a real
  symmetric matrix, via the project's CollatzÔÇôWielandt development and the
  variational `|w|`-argument), and **A.19ÔÇôA.20** (polar + singular-value
  decompositions from the spectral theorem).
- **Documented axioms (faithful statements, deferred proofs)** ÔÇö **A.12**
  (strong-coupling effective Hamiltonian limit) and the
  operator-algebraic **A.21ÔÇôA.28** (Wigner's
  theorem, states, BanachÔÇôAlaoglu, ground states of infinite systems, the GNS
  construction).

**Value judgment / policy.** The documented axioms above are kept as *faithful,
book-order statements* ÔÇö they record exactly what Tasaki proves ÔÇö but they are
**not active proof targets** of this project. The heavy functional-analytic and
operator-algebraic results (states on the quasi-local C\*-algebra, weak-ÔłŚ
compactness, ground states, GNS, Wigner) belong to a dedicated operator-algebra /
functional-analysis development; such a development may well be carried out
**separately** (for instance contributed to `mathlib`), and these axioms simply
**wait for that implementation**. Accordingly the project's policy is to
**axiomatize only the appendix results that Tasaki's formalized main development
actually uses**, to **prove** the remaining ones where `mathlib` provides the
tools, and otherwise to leave a faithful axiom in place rather than invest in a
large bespoke operator-algebra build whose natural home is elsewhere. The
`#print axioms` of every theorem in the repository makes the precise dependency
on these documented axioms auditable.

## Formalized theorems

All items below are formally proved with **zero `sorry`**. Full
mathematical statements and proof sketches are in
[`tex/proof-guide.tex`](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/blob/main/tex/proof-guide.tex).

### Single-site Pauli operators

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬ž2.1 eq. (2.1.8), p. 15. Cross-checked with Nielsen-Chuang
┬ž2.1.3 Figure 2.2 (pp. 65-66, definitions), Ex. 2.19 (p. 70,
Hermiticity), Ex. 2.41 (p. 78, `(¤â^╬▒)┬▓ = I` and anticommutation),
Ex. 2.40 (p. 77, commutator, whence the cyclic products).

|---|---|---|

| `pauliX/Y/Z_mul_self` | `(¤â^╬▒)┬▓ = I` | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX_mul_pauliY` etc. | `¤â^x ¤â^y = i┬Ě¤â^z` (cyclic) | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX_anticomm_pauliY` etc. | `¤â^╬▒ ¤â^╬▓ + ¤â^╬▓ ¤â^╬▒ = 0` (╬▒ Ôëá ╬▓) | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |

### ZÔéé ├Ś ZÔéé representation (Tasaki ┬ž2.1 eqs. (2.1.27)-(2.1.34))

The ZÔéé ├Ś ZÔéé structure is proved across files:
- S = 1/2 (projective, eq. (2.1.31)): `spinHalfRot*_pi_sq = -1`, anticommutation, products.
- S = 1 (genuine, eq. (2.1.27)): `spinOnePiRot*_sq = 1`, commutation.

See `Quantum/Z2Z2.lean` for the unified documentation.

### 3D rotation matrices `R^(╬▒)_¤Ç` (Tasaki ┬ž2.1 eq. (2.1.28))

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬ž2.1 eqs. (2.1.27)-(2.1.28), p. 18 and Problem 2.1.f.

| Lean name | Statement | File |

| `rot3D{1,2,3}Pi` | 3├Ś3 real orthogonal ¤Ç-rotation matrices | `Quantum/Rotation3D.lean` |
| `rot3D{1,2,3}Pi_sq` | `(R^(╬▒)_¤Ç)┬▓ = 1` | `Quantum/Rotation3D.lean` |
| `rot3D{1,2,3}Pi_mul_rot3D{2,3,1}Pi` | `R^(╬▒)_¤Ç ┬Ě R^(╬▓)_¤Ç = R^(╬│)_¤Ç` (cyclic, Problem 2.1.f) | `Quantum/Rotation3D.lean` |
| `rot3D{1,2,3}Pi_comm_*` | distinct-axis `R^(╬▒)_¤Ç` and `R^(╬▓)_¤Ç` commute | `Quantum/Rotation3D.lean` |

### Polynomial-basis decomposition for S = 1 (Tasaki ┬ž2.1 Problem 2.1.a, S = 1)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum
Many-Body Systems*, ┬ž2.1 Problem 2.1.a, p. 15 + solution S.1, p. 493.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `spinOneProj{Plus,Zero,Minus}` | the three diagonal projectors `\|¤ł^¤âÔčęÔčĘ¤ł^¤â\|` (¤â Ôłł {+1, 0, -1}) | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneProj{Plus,Zero,Minus}_eq_polynomial` | each diagonal projector equals a polynomial in `┼ť^(3)` (Lagrange interpolation) | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit{01,02,10,12,20,21}` | the six off-diagonal matrix units `\|¤ł^¤äÔčęÔčĘ¤ł^¤â\|` (¤ä Ôëá ¤â) | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit{01,12}_eq_polynomial` | `(1/ÔłÜ2) ┼ť^- ┬Ě P_¤â` for the two single-step lowering units | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit{10,21}_eq_polynomial` | `(1/ÔłÜ2) ┼ť^+ ┬Ě P_¤â` for the two single-step raising units | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit02_eq_polynomial` | `(1/2) (┼ť^-)┬▓ ┬Ě P_+` for the double-step lowering unit | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit20_eq_polynomial` | `(1/2) (┼ť^+)┬▓ ┬Ě P_-` for the double-step raising unit | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOne_decomposition` | every 3├Ś3 complex matrix is a linear combination of the 9 matrix units (entry-wise); combined with the polynomial expressions above this gives Tasaki Problem 2.1.a for `S = 1` | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |

### Spin-`S` operators (general S Ôëą 0, parameterised by `N = 2S : ÔäĽ`)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body Systems*, ┬ž2.1 Problem 2.1.a (p. 15) and solution S.1 (p. 493).

Generic spin-`S` operators live on `Matrix (Fin (N + 1)) (Fin (N + 1)) Ôäé`, with `N = 2S : ÔäĽ` (so `N = 1` Ôçö `S = 1/2`, `N = 2` Ôçö `S = 1`). Tracking issue #458 (Tasaki ┬ž2.1 P1d''' general-S generalisation).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `spinSOp3` | `┼ť^(3) := diag(N/2, N/2 - 1, ÔÇŽ, -N/2)` for `N : ÔäĽ` | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus` / `spinSOpMinus` | raising/lowering operators with matrix entries `ÔłÜ(k┬Ě(N Ôłĺ k + 1))` (resp. `ÔłÜ((N Ôłĺ k)(k + 1))`) on the immediate sub/super-diagonal | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp1` / `spinSOp2` | `┼ť^(1) := (┼ť^+ + ┼ť^-) / 2`, `┼ť^(2) := (┼ť^+ Ôłĺ ┼ť^-) / (2 i)` | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_apply_top` / `_bottom` | `┼ť^+` annihilates `\|NÔčę` (highest weight); `┼ť^-` annihilates `\|0Ôčę` (lowest weight) ÔÇö the ladder boundaries | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_commutator_spinSOp{Plus,Minus}` | **Cartan relations** `[┼ť^{(3)}, ┼ť^+] = ┼ť^+` and `[┼ť^{(3)}, ┼ť^-] = -┼ť^-`: `┼ť^┬▒` shift the magnetic quantum number by `┬▒1`. Proved entry-wise via `Matrix.diagonal_mul` / `mul_diagonal` (╬▓-2 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Algebra.lean` |
| `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_conjTranspose` / `spinSOp{1,2,3}_isHermitian` | adjointness `(┼ť^+)ß┤┤ = ┼ť^-`, `(┼ť^-)ß┤┤ = ┼ť^+`, and Hermiticity of `┼ť^{(╬▒)}` for `╬▒ Ôłł {1, 2, 3}` (╬▓-3 of Issue #458). The ladder adjointness follows from the matrix-entry symmetry; `┼ť^{(1)}` and `┼ť^{(2)}` use the `1/2` and `1/(2i)` self-conjugacy facts | `Quantum/SpinS/Hermitian.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj` / `spinSOp3_sub_smul_mul_diagProj` / `_self_mul_diagProj` | the **diagonal projector** `P_k := diag(╬┤_{i,k})` and the eigenvalue-action lemma `(┼ť^{(3)} Ôłĺ ╬╗_j ÔÇó 1) ┬Ě P_k = (╬╗_k Ôłĺ ╬╗_j) ┬Ě P_k`, with the annihilation specialisation at `j = k` (╬▓-4 of Issue #458). Foundation for the Lagrange-interpolation polynomial decomposition: each `P_k` will be expressed as `ÔłĆ_{j Ôëá k} (┼ť^{(3)} Ôłĺ ╬╗_j ÔÇó 1) / (╬╗_k Ôłĺ ╬╗_j)` in a follow-up PR | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProj.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_mul_diagProj` / `diagProj_mul_spinSOp3` / `spinSOp3_commute_diagProj` | the **eigenvalue equation** `┼ť^{(3)} ┬Ě P_k = ╬╗_k ┬Ě P_k` (where `╬╗_k = (N : Ôäé)/2 Ôłĺ k`), with the symmetric `P_k ┬Ě ┼ť^{(3)} = ╬╗_k ┬Ě P_k` and the resulting commutativity. Both are diagonal-times-diagonal computations (╬▓-5 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Lagrange.lean` |
| `mul_diagProj_apply` / `diagProj_mul_apply` / `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_mul_diagProj_apply` | **Column/row selection** by the diagonal projector: `(A ┬Ě P_k)[i, j] = A[i, k]` if `j = k` else `0` (and dually for `P_k ┬Ě A`). Specialised to `┼ť^┬▒` produces off-diagonal matrix units (╬▓-6 of Issue #458) ÔÇö the building blocks of the polynomial decomposition theorem | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderProj.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_succ_mul_spinSOpMinus` | **Ladder recursion**: `┼ť^+ ┬Ě P_{k+1} ┬Ě ┼ť^- = (k + 1)(N Ôłĺ k) ┬Ě P_k`. The triple product collapses to a non-zero scalar multiple of `P_k`. Iterating from the lowest-weight projector `P_N` (itself a polynomial in `┼ť^{(3)}`) yields every diagonal projector as a polynomial in `{1╠é, ┼ť^{(╬▒)}}` (╬▓-7 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderRecursion.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_first` / `spinSOpMinus_mul_diagProj_last` | **Ladder boundaries**: `┼ť^+ ┬Ě P_0 = 0` (top of ladder) and `┼ť^- ┬Ě P_N = 0` (bottom). The first/last columns of `┼ť^┬▒` vanish, so multiplication by `P_{0/N}` (which selects that column) gives `0`. Terminate the recursion at the highest- and lowest-weight projectors (╬▓-8 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderBoundary.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_isHermitian` / `sum_spinSDiagProj_eq_one` | **Hermiticity** of each `P_k`, and the **resolution of the identity** `╬ú_k P_k = 1`. The latter is the cornerstone for the matrix-unit decomposition: combined with the off-diagonal matrix units (╬▓-6, ╬▓-7), gives every matrix in `M_{N+1}(Ôäé)` (╬▓-9 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProjProperties.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_succ_eq_single` / `spinSOpMinus_mul_diagProj_eq_single` | **Off-diagonal matrix-unit decomposition**: `┼ť^+ ┬Ě P_{i+1} = ÔłÜ((i+1)(NÔłĺi)) ┬Ě E_{i, i+1}` and `┼ť^- ┬Ě P_i = ÔłÜ((NÔłĺi)(i+1)) ┬Ě E_{i+1, i}`, where `E_{a, b} := Matrix.single a b 1` is the (a, b) matrix unit (╬▓-10 of Issue #458). These are the simplest off-diagonal matrix units expressible via `┼ť^┬▒` ladder action on diagonal projectors | `Quantum/SpinS/OffDiagUnit.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_sq_eq_diagonal` | `(┼ť^{(3)})┬▓ = diag((N/2 Ôłĺ i)┬▓)`. Step toward the **Casimir identity** `(┼ť^{(1)})┬▓ + (┼ť^{(2)})┬▓ + (┼ť^{(3)})┬▓ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬Ě 1` for general spin (╬▓-11 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Op3Square.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_spinSOpMinus_eq_diagonal` | `┼ť^+ ┬Ě ┼ť^- = diag((i + 1)(N Ôłĺ i))`. The product is diagonal because `┼ť^+[i, l] ┬Ě ┼ť^-[l, j]` is non-zero only when `l = i + 1 = j + 1`, forcing `i = j`. Combined with the analogous `┼ť^- ┬Ě ┼ť^+` and `(┼ť^{(3)})┬▓`, this assembles the Casimir identity (╬▓-12 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PlusMinusDiag.lean` |
| `spinSOpMinus_mul_spinSOpPlus_eq_diagonal` | `┼ť^- ┬Ě ┼ť^+ = diag(i ┬Ě (N Ôłĺ i + 1))`. Symmetric to ╬▓-12 with `┼ť^┬▒` swapped (╬▓-13 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/MinusPlusDiag.lean` |
| `spinSOp1_sq_add_spinSOp2_sq` / `spinSOp_total_squared` | **Casimir identity** for general spin-`S`: `(┼ť^{(1)})┬▓ + (┼ť^{(2)})┬▓ + (┼ť^{(3)})┬▓ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬Ě 1`, equivalent to `S(S+1) ┬Ě 1` for `S = N/2`. Proves the irreducible spin-`S` representation has Casimir eigenvalue `S(S+1)` (Schur's lemma). The intermediate identity `(┼ť^{(1)})┬▓ + (┼ť^{(2)})┬▓ = (1/2)(┼ť^+ ┬Ě ┼ť^- + ┼ť^- ┬Ě ┼ť^+)` is proved using `module` (╬▓-14 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Casimir.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_mulVec_basis` | spin-`S` eigenstate equation `┼ť^{(3)} ┬Ě \|kÔčę = (N/2 Ôłĺ k) ┬Ě \|kÔčę`, where `\|kÔčę := Pi.single k 1` is the `k`-th unit basis vector of `(Fin (N + 1) Ôćĺ Ôäé)` (╬▓-15 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Eigenstates.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mulVec_basis` / `spinSOpMinus_mulVec_basis` | **Ladder action on basis vectors**: `┼ť^+ ┬Ě \|kÔčę = ÔłÜ(k(N Ôłĺ k + 1)) ┬Ě \|k Ôłĺ 1Ôčę` for `k Ôëą 1`, and `┼ť^- ┬Ě \|kÔčę = ÔłÜ((N Ôłĺ k)(k + 1)) ┬Ě \|k + 1Ôčę` for `k ÔëĄ N Ôłĺ 1`. The standard SU(2) ladder relations on the magnetic-quantum-number basis (╬▓-16 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderStates.lean` |
| `spinSOp_total_squared_mulVec_basis` | **Casimir eigenvalue on basis**: `(┼ť)┬▓ ┬Ě \|kÔčę = (N(N+2)/4) ┬Ě \|kÔčę`. Direct consequence of `(┼ť)┬▓ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬Ě 1` (╬▓-14) ÔÇö every basis vector has the universal Casimir eigenvalue, reflecting that the spin-`S` representation is a single irreducible (Schur) (╬▓-17 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirEigenvalue.lean` |
| `spinSOp{1,2,3,Plus,Minus}_commute_total_squared` | **Casimir invariance**: each `┼ť^{(╬▒)}` and `┼ť^┬▒` commutes with the Casimir `(┼ť)┬▓ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬Ě 1`. Direct consequence of the scalar nature of the Casimir (╬▓-18 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirInvariance.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_commutator_spinSOpMinus` | **Third Cartan relation**: `[┼ť^+, ┼ť^-] = 2 ┬Ě ┼ť^{(3)}`. Combines ╬▓-12 (`┼ť^+ ┬Ě ┼ť^- = diag((i + 1)(N Ôłĺ i))`) and ╬▓-13 (`┼ť^- ┬Ě ┼ť^+ = diag(i (N Ôłĺ i + 1))`); the difference `(i+1)(N-i) Ôłĺ i(N-i+1) = N Ôłĺ 2i = 2(N/2 Ôłĺ i)` matches `2 ┬Ě ┼ť^{(3)}` (╬▓-19 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Cartan3.lean` |
| `spinSOp1_commutator_spinSOp2` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[┼ť^{(1)}, ┼ť^{(2)}] = i ┬Ě ┼ť^{(3)}`. Derived from the Cartan relations by algebraic manipulation through `┼ť^{(1)} = (┼ť^+ + ┼ť^-)/2` and `┼ť^{(2)} = (┼ť^+ Ôłĺ ┼ť^-)/(2i)`: `(P+Q)(P-Q) Ôłĺ (P-Q)(P+Q) = -2[P, Q] = -4 ┼ť^{(3)}`, then scalar simplification `-1/I = I` (╬▓-20 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator.lean` |
| `spinSOp2_commutator_spinSOp3` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[┼ť^{(2)}, ┼ť^{(3)}] = i ┬Ě ┼ť^{(1)}`. Derived from Cartan ╬▓-2 (`[┼ť^{(3)}, ┼ť^┬▒] = ┬▒┼ť^┬▒`) via `┼ť^{(2)} = (┼ť^+ Ôłĺ ┼ť^-)/(2i)`: the commutator reduces to `(1/(2i)) (-┼ť^+ Ôłĺ ┼ť^-) = (-1/(2i)) ┬Ě 2 ┼ť^{(1)} = i ┬Ě ┼ť^{(1)}` (╬▓-21 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator23.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_commutator_spinSOp1` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[┼ť^{(3)}, ┼ť^{(1)}] = i ┬Ě ┼ť^{(2)}`. Derived from Cartan ╬▓-2 via `┼ť^{(1)} = (┼ť^+ + ┼ť^-)/2`: the commutator reduces to `(1/2) (┼ť^+ Ôłĺ ┼ť^-) = (1/2) ┬Ě (2i) ┬Ě ┼ť^{(2)} = i ┬Ě ┼ť^{(2)}`. Together with ╬▓-20 and ╬▓-21 this completes the standard SU(2) commutator algebra (Tasaki eq. (2.1.1)) for spin-`S` operators (╬▓-22 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator31.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_mul_self` / `spinSDiagProj_mul_of_ne` | **Idempotence and orthogonality** of the diagonal projectors `P_k = |kÔčęÔčĘk|`: `P_k ┬Ě P_k = P_k` and `P_i ┬Ě P_j = 0` for `i Ôëá j`. Combined with ╬▓-9 (`ÔłĹ_k P_k = 1`) this gives the spectral decomposition of the identity for `┼ť^{(3)}` (╬▓-23 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProjOrtho.lean` |
| `aeval_diagonal` | **Polynomial evaluation at a diagonal matrix**: `aeval (Matrix.diagonal v) p = Matrix.diagonal (fun i => p.eval (v i))`. Foundational lemma for the Lagrange-interpolation step (╬▓-25+) ÔÇö lets us pull a polynomial in `┼ť^{(3)}` (a diagonal matrix) through to its scalar action on each diagonal entry. Proof by `Polynomial.induction_on'` on monomials and addition (╬▓-24 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/AevalDiagonal.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_eq_lagrange_aeval` | **Lagrange-interpolation formula for `P_k`**: `P_k = aeval (┼ť^{(3)}) (Lagrange.basis Finset.univ (spinSOp3Eigen N) k)`, equivalently `P_k = ÔłĆ_{j Ôëá k} (┼ť^{(3)} Ôłĺ ╬╗_j ÔÇó 1)/(╬╗_k Ôłĺ ╬╗_j)` with `╬╗_j = (N : Ôäé)/2 Ôłĺ j.val`. Combines ╬▓-24 (`aeval` of diagonal) with mathlib's `Lagrange.eval_basis_self` / `eval_basis_of_ne`. Each diagonal projector is therefore a **polynomial in `┼ť^{(3)}`**, which combined with ╬▓-9 (`ÔłĹ P_k = 1`) gives an explicit polynomial decomposition of `1╠é` (╬▓-25 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LagrangeFormula.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_eq_one_add_I_smul_two` / `spinSOpMinus_eq_one_sub_I_smul_two` | **Inversion of the Cartesian definition**: `┼ť^+ = ┼ť^{(1)} + i ┬Ě ┼ť^{(2)}` and `┼ť^- = ┼ť^{(1)} Ôłĺ i ┬Ě ┼ť^{(2)}`. The defining identities `┼ť^{(1)} = (1/2)(┼ť^+ + ┼ť^-)`, `┼ť^{(2)} = (1/(2i))(┼ť^+ Ôłĺ ┼ť^-)` invert to express the ladder operators as **linear combinations** of the Hermitian Cartesian spin operators (╬▓-26 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PMAsOneTwo.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_mem_adjoin_spinSOp3` / `spinSDiagProj_mem_adjoin` | **Diagonal projectors live in the algebra generated by the spin operators**: `P_k Ôłł Algebra.adjoin Ôäé {┼ť^{(3)}}` (and a fortiori `P_k Ôłł Algebra.adjoin Ôäé {┼ť^{(1)}, ┼ť^{(2)}, ┼ť^{(3)}}`). Promotes the Lagrange-interpolation formula (╬▓-25) from "polynomial-evaluation" form to "subalgebra-membership" form via `Algebra.adjoin_singleton_eq_range_aeval` (╬▓-27 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/ProjMemAdjoin.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mem_adjoin` / `spinSOpMinus_mem_adjoin` | **Ladder operators live in `Algebra.adjoin Ôäé {┼ť^{(╬▒)}}`**: `┼ť^+, ┼ť^- Ôłł Algebra.adjoin Ôäé {┼ť^{(1)}, ┼ť^{(2)}, ┼ť^{(3)}}`. Direct consequence of ╬▓-26 (`┼ť^┬▒ = ┼ť^{(1)} ┬▒ i ┬Ě ┼ť^{(2)}`) and the fact that subalgebras are closed under `Ôäé`-linear combinations (╬▓-28 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PMMemAdjoin.lean` |
| `single_succ_mem_adjoin` / `single_succ_swap_mem_adjoin` | **Immediate-neighbor matrix units live in `Algebra.adjoin Ôäé {┼ť^{(╬▒)}}`**: `E_{i, i+1}, E_{i+1, i} Ôłł Algebra.adjoin Ôäé {┼ť^{(1)}, ┼ť^{(2)}, ┼ť^{(3)}}`. Combines ╬▓-10 (`┼ť^+ ┬Ě P_{i+1} = ÔłÜ((i+1)(N-i)) ┬Ě E_{i, i+1}`) with ╬▓-27 (`P_k Ôłł adjoin`) and ╬▓-28 (`┼ť^┬▒ Ôłł adjoin`); the ladder coefficient is non-zero on the valid range and the subalgebra is closed under multiplication and scalar `smul`. Step toward expressing every matrix unit as a polynomial in `{┼ť^{(╬▒)}}` (╬▓-29 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/NeighborUnitAdjoin.lean` |
| `single_offset_succ_mem_adjoin` / `single_offset_succ_swap_mem_adjoin` | **Arbitrary stride-(k+1) matrix units live in `Algebra.adjoin Ôäé {┼ť^{(╬▒)}}`**: `E_{i, i+(k+1)}, E_{i+(k+1), i} Ôłł Algebra.adjoin Ôäé {┼ť^{(╬▒)}}` for any `k`. Induction on `k`: base case is ╬▓-29; inductive step chains via `Matrix.single_mul_single_same` (`E_{i,j} ┬Ě E_{j,k} = E_{i,k}`) (╬▓-30 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/OffsetUnitAdjoin.lean` |
| `matrix_single_mem_adjoin` | **Every matrix unit `E_{i,j}` lives in `Algebra.adjoin Ôäé {┼ť^{(╬▒)}}`**. Three-case split: `i = j` reduces to ╬▓-27 via `Matrix.diagonal_single` (`E_{i,i} = P_i`); `i.val < j.val` is ╬▓-30 upper; `j.val < i.val` is ╬▓-30 lower. Last building block before the spanning theorem (╬▓-31 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllUnitsAdjoin.lean` |
| `matrix_mem_adjoin` / **`spinS_adjoin_eq_top`** | **­čÄ» Tasaki ┬ž2.1 Problem 2.1.a (P1d''') general-`S`**: every operator on the `(2S+1)`-dimensional spin-`S` Hilbert space `Ôäé^{N+1}` is a polynomial in `{1╠é, ┼ť^{(1)}, ┼ť^{(2)}, ┼ť^{(3)}}`. Equivalently, `Algebra.adjoin Ôäé {┼ť^{(1)}, ┼ť^{(2)}, ┼ť^{(3)}} = ÔŐĄ`. Proof: every matrix unit `E_{i,j} Ôłł adjoin` (╬▓-31), so by the entry-wise decomposition `M = ÔłĹ_{i,j} M_{i,j} ÔÇó E_{i,j}` (`Matrix.matrix_eq_sum_single`) and the linearity of the subalgebra, every matrix is in the adjoin (╬▓-32 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/SpanningTheorem.lean` |
| `ManyBodyOpS` / `onSiteS` / `spinSSiteOp{1,2,3,Plus,Minus}` | **Multi-site spin-`S` operator space** indexed by configurations `¤â : ╬Ť Ôćĺ Fin (N + 1)`, with the site-embedded operator `onSiteS i A` acting as `A` on site `i` and as the identity elsewhere; site-specialised `┼ť_i^{(╬▒)}, ┼ť_i^┬▒`. Hermiticity preservation `onSiteS_isHermitian` lifts from single-site to multi-site (Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Phase B-╬▓ ╬▓-3a, Issue #412) | `Quantum/SpinS/MultiSite.lean` |

### Basis states and raising/lowering (Tasaki ┬ž2.1)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬ž2.1 eqs. (2.1.2), (2.1.3), (2.1.6), p. 14 for the `S = 1`
case (`¤â Ôłł {-1, 0, +1}`).

| Lean name | Statement | File |

| `spinOnePiRot{1,2,3}_sq` | `(├╗_╬▒)┬▓ = 1` for integer S (Tasaki eq. (2.1.31) integer case) | `Quantum/SpinOneBasis.lean` |

(2.2.15)): `(1/4¤Ç) ÔłźÔéÇ^{2¤Ç} d¤ć ÔłźÔéÇ^¤Ç d╬Ş sin ╬Ş ┬Ě ├Ť^(3)_¤ć ├Ť^(2)_╬Ş ¤ü (├Ť^(3)_¤ć ├Ť^(2)_╬Ş)ÔÇá = (1/2) P_singlet` where `¤ü = \|ÔćĹÔéüÔćôÔééÔčęÔčĘÔćĹÔéüÔćôÔéé\|`. ### Time-reversal map for `S = 1/2` (Tasaki ┬ž2.3)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬ž2.3 eqs. (2.3.4)ÔÇô(2.3.8), pp. 26ÔÇô27.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `timeReversalSpinHalf` | `╬ś╠é(v) = (-conj(v 1), conj(v 0))` (Tasaki eq. (2.3.6), `S = 1/2`); the antilinear unitary `├╗_2 ┬Ě K╠é` of ┬ž2.3 | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_spinHalfUp` / `_spinHalfDown` | `╬ś╠é|¤ł^ÔćĹÔčę = |¤ł^ÔćôÔčę` and `╬ś╠é|¤ł^ÔćôÔčę = -|¤ł^ÔćĹÔčę` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_add` | additivity `╬ś╠é(v + w) = ╬ś╠é(v) + ╬ś╠é(w)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_smul` | **antilinearity** in the scalar: `╬ś╠é(c ÔÇó v) = (conj c) ÔÇó ╬ś╠é(v)` (Tasaki ┬ž2.3 antilinearity warning, pp. 26ÔÇô27) | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_sq` | **Kramers degeneracy at `S = 1/2`** (Tasaki eq. (2.3.8), half-odd-integer): `╬ś╠é┬▓ = -1╠é` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_spinHalfOp{1,2,3}_mulVec` | spin sign flip (Tasaki eq. (2.3.14)): `╬ś╠é(┼ť^(╬▒) ┬Ě v) = (-┼ť^(╬▒)) ┬Ě (╬ś╠é v)` for `╬▒ = 1, 2, 3` ÔÇö equivariance form of `╬ś╠é ┼ť^(╬▒) ╬ś╠éÔü╗┬╣ = -┼ť^(╬▒)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `complexConjugationSpinHalf` | the antilinear complex-conjugation map `K╠é` of Tasaki ┬ž2.3 eq. (2.3.4) at `S = 1/2`: `K╠é(v) i := conj(v i)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `complexConjugationSpinHalf_sq` / `_add` / `_smul` | `K╠é` is involutive (`K╠é┬▓ = id`), additive, and antilinear in the scalar | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_eq_spinHalfRot2_pi_mulVec` | the **factorisation identity** of Tasaki ┬ž2.3: `╬ś╠é = ├╗_2 ┬Ě K╠é` where `├╗_2 = spinHalfRot2 ¤Ç` is the ¤Ç rotation about the `2`-axis | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `flipConfig` / `flipConfig_apply` / `flipConfig_involutive` | the spin-flip on a many-body configuration `¤â : ╬Ť Ôćĺ Fin 2`: `flipConfig ¤â x := 1 - ¤â x`; involutive | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSign` / `timeReversalSign_zero` / `_one` / `_mul_flip` | per-site sign factor `╬Á(0) = 1`, `╬Á(1) = -1` for the multi-spin time-reversal, with `╬Á(s) ┬Ě ╬Á(1 - s) = -1` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti` | multi-spin time-reversal map (Tasaki ┬ž2.3 lattice extension, `S = 1/2`): `(╬ś╠é_tot v) ¤ä := (ÔłĆ_x ╬Á(¤ä x)) ┬Ě conj(v (flip ¤ä))` for finite `╬Ť` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSign_prod_conj` / `timeReversalSign_prod_mul_flip` | the product `ÔłĆ_x ╬Á(¤ä x)` is real (conjugation invariant); the cross product equals `(-1)^|╬Ť|` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_sq` | **Multi-spin Kramers degeneracy at `S = 1/2`** (Tasaki ┬ž2.3 half-odd-integer extension): `╬ś╠é_tot┬▓ = (-1)^|╬Ť| ┬Ě 1╠é` ÔÇö `+1╠é` when `|╬Ť|` is even, `-1╠é` when odd | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_basisVec` | action of `╬ś╠é_tot` on a many-body basis state: `╬ś╠é_tot |╬Ę_¤âÔčę = (ÔłĆ_x ╬Á(flip ¤â x)) ┬Ě |╬Ę_{flip ¤â}Ôčę` ÔÇö natural many-body generalisation of `╬ś╠é|ÔćĹÔčę = |ÔćôÔčę` and `╬ś╠é|ÔćôÔčę = -|ÔćĹÔčę` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_pauliZ_mulVec` | multi-site sign-flip equivariance for `¤â^z` (Tasaki ┬ž2.3 (2.3.14) lifted to many-body): `╬ś╠é_tot ((onSite x ¤â^z) v) = (-(onSite x ¤â^z))(╬ś╠é_tot v)`. Diagonal-action case; `¤â^x`, `¤â^y` deferred | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `siteFlipAt` / `siteFlipAt_self` / `siteFlipAt_of_ne` / `flipConfig_siteFlipAt_comm` / `siteFlipAt_involutive` | per-site flip helpers: `siteFlipAt ¤ä x` flips slot `x` only; commutes with `flipConfig`; involutive. The combinatorial primitive underlying off-diagonal `¤â^x_x` / `¤â^y_x` action (deferred) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `onSite_pauliX_mulVec_basisVec` | basis-state action of the off-diagonal site Pauli: `(onSite x ¤â^x).mulVec |╬Ę_¤âÔčę = |╬Ę_{siteFlipAt ¤â x}Ôčę` (the spin at site `x` is swapped) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `pauliX_eq_indicator` / `onSite_pauliX_mulVec_apply` | closed-form `pauliX a b = if b = 1 - a then 1 else 0`, lifted to `((onSite x ¤â^x).mulVec v) ¤ä = v (siteFlipAt ¤ä x)` for any state `v` (general extension of the basis-state action) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSign_prod_siteFlipAt` | `ÔłĆ_y ╬Á((siteFlipAt ¤ä x) y) = -(ÔłĆ_y ╬Á(¤ä y))` ÔÇö the per-site flip swaps `╬Á(¤ä x)` with `╬Á(1 - ¤ä x) = -╬Á(¤ä x)`, flipping the total sign | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_pauliX_mulVec` | multi-site sign-flip equivariance for `¤â^x` (Tasaki ┬ž2.3 (2.3.14) at ╬▒ = 1): `╬ś╠é_tot ((onSite x ¤â^x) v) = (-(onSite x ¤â^x))(╬ś╠é_tot v)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_pauliY_mulVec` | multi-site sign-flip equivariance for `¤â^y` (Tasaki ┬ž2.3 (2.3.14) at ╬▒ = 2): `╬ś╠é_tot ((onSite x ¤â^y) v) = (-(onSite x ¤â^y))(╬ś╠é_tot v)`. The proof handles the per-site `┬▒i` factor via `conj(pauliY_sign(1 - s)) = pauliY_sign(s)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_add` / `_smul` / `_real_smul` | multi-spin `╬ś╠é_tot` is additive, antilinear in the scalar (`╬ś╠é_tot(c ÔÇó v) = conj(c) ÔÇó ╬ś╠é_tot v`), and real-linear (special case of antilinearity at real `r`) ÔÇö foundational for lifting Pauli-axis equivariance to bilinear / Heisenberg-type Hamiltonian forms | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_spinHalfOp{1,2,3}_mulVec` | Tasaki ┬ž2.3 (2.3.14) for spin-1/2 ops `┼ť^(╬▒) = ¤â^(╬▒) / 2`: `╬ś╠é_tot ((onSite x ┼ť^(╬▒)) v) = (-(onSite x ┼ť^(╬▒)))(╬ś╠é_tot v)` for ╬▒ = 1, 2, 3 ÔÇö direct corollaries of the Pauli versions by scalar (1/2) multiplication | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_spinHalfDot_mulVec` | **Time-reversal invariance of the bilinear `┼ť_x ┬Ě ┼ť_y`** (Tasaki ┬ž2.3): `╬ś╠é_tot ((┼ť_x ┬Ě ┼ť_y) v) = (┼ť_x ┬Ě ┼ť_y)(╬ś╠é_tot v)` ÔÇö two equivariance `-1` factors cancel; sums per-axis | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec` | **Time-reversal invariance of the Heisenberg Hamiltonian** (Tasaki ┬ž2.3): for real coupling `J` (`conj(J(x,y)) = J(x,y)`), `╬ś╠é_tot ((H J) v) = (H J)(╬ś╠é_tot v)`. Combines per-bond invariance + ╬ś╠é_tot antilinearity (J reality) + additivity (over double-sum) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling_conj` / `periodicChainCoupling_conj` | every entry of `openChainCoupling N J` (resp. `periodicChainCoupling N J`) is real (under complex conjugation), since `J : ÔäŁ` makes `(-(J : Ôäé))` real-valued | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_openChainHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_periodicChainHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_squareLatticeHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_squareTorusHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_cubicLatticeHeisenberg_mulVec` | concrete time-reversal invariance: the open / periodic chain, the 2D open square / torus, and the 3D cubic Heisenberg Hamiltonians all commute with `╬ś╠é_tot` for any real coupling `J : ÔäŁ`. Backed by `*Coupling_conj` reality lemmas in `HeisenbergChain.lean` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_basisVec_upDown` / `_basisVec_basisSwap_upDown` | `╬ś╠é_tot |ÔćĹÔćôÔčę = -|ÔćôÔćĹÔčę` and `╬ś╠é_tot |ÔćôÔćĹÔčę = -|ÔćĹÔćôÔčę` on `Fin 2` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_singlet` | the two-site spin singlet `|ÔćĹÔćôÔčę - |ÔćôÔćĹÔčę` is **time-reversal invariant** (Tasaki ┬ž2.3 / ┬žA.3): being the SU(2) `S = 0` representation, it survives `╬ś╠é_tot` unchanged | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_triplet_zero` | the triplet `m = 0` state `|ÔćĹÔćôÔčę + |ÔćôÔćĹÔčę` is **anti-invariant** under `╬ś╠é_tot`: `╬ś╠é_tot (|ÔćĹÔćôÔčę + |ÔćôÔćĹÔčę) = -(|ÔćĹÔćôÔčę + |ÔćôÔćĹÔčę)` (the symmetric combination picks up a minus sign from the per-basis-vector flip) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |

### Multi-body operator space (abstract lattice)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬ž2.2, pp. 21-26 (tensor-product Hilbert space and site-local
operators). The lattice `╬Ť` is an arbitrary finite set with decidable
equality; specializing to `╬Ť = Fin N` recovers an `N`-site chain.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `ManyBodyOp ╬Ť` | `Matrix (╬Ť Ôćĺ Fin 2) (╬Ť Ôćĺ Fin 2) Ôäé` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite i A` | site-embedded operator at `i : ╬Ť` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_isHermitian` | `A.IsHermitian Ôćĺ (onSite i A).IsHermitian` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_add`, `onSite_sub`, `onSite_zero`, `onSite_smul`, `onSite_one` | linearity of the site embedding and `onSite i 1 = 1` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_mul_onSite_of_ne` | distinct-site commutation (Tasaki (2.2.6), `x Ôëá y`, S = 1/2) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `basisVec` / `onSite_mulVec_basisVec` | tensor-product basis states and their action under site operators (Tasaki (2.2.1)/(2.2.4)) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_mul_onSite_same` | same-site product `onSite i A ┬Ě onSite i B = onSite i (A ┬Ě B)` (Tasaki (2.2.6), `x = y`) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_commutator_same` | same-site commutator `[onSite i A, onSite i B] = onSite i [A, B]` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.mul_of_commute` | commuting Hermitians multiply Hermitian (generic; relocated to the matrix-analysis layer in PR #4342) | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |

### Generic matrix-analysis helpers (`Math/MatrixAnalysis/`)

Topic-organized generic linear-algebra facts extracted from the physics files where they had been
re-proved as private helpers (Issue [#4339](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4339)).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `Matrix.isHermitian_sum` | a finite sum of Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.mul_of_commute` | the product of two commuting Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_isHermitian` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_sq_of_sq_one` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting involutions is an involution | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_mul_self_of_idempotent` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting idempotents is idempotent | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_mulVec_eq_self` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of matrices each fixing `¤ł` also fixes `¤ł` | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.trace_im` | the trace of a Hermitian matrix is real | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |
| `Matrix.trace_mul_star_of_isHermitian` | `Tr(A┬ĚB)` of two Hermitian matrices is conjugation-invariant | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |

(The two trace helpers above are also listed in the Gibbs-state section where they are consumed; the
canonical definitions live in `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean`.)

These are consumed by the JordanÔÇôWigner string / Hubbard hard-core projection layers (PR #4342),
replacing the per-file private copies in `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean`,
`Fermion/JordanWigner/Operators.lean`, and `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean`.

### Total spin operator (Tasaki ┬ž2.2 eq. (2.2.7), (2.2.8))

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬ž2.2 eqs. (2.2.7) and (2.2.8), p. 22.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `totalSpinHalfOp1/2/3 ╬Ť` | `┼ť_tot^(╬▒) := ╬ú_{x Ôłł ╬Ť} onSite x ┼ť^(╬▒)` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp1/2/3_isHermitian` | `┼ť_tot^(╬▒)` is Hermitian | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `spinHalfOp_onSite_comm_of_ne` | S = 1/2 named wrapper of `onSite_mul_onSite_of_ne` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `spinHalfOp{1,2,3}_onSite_commutator_spinHalfOp{2,3,1}_onSite` | same-site commutator `[┼ť_x^(╬▒), ┼ť_x^(╬▓)] = i ┬Ě ┼ť_x^(╬│)` (Tasaki (2.2.6), `x = y`) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpPlus`, `totalSpinHalfOpMinus` | `┼ť^┬▒_tot := ╬ú_{x Ôłł ╬Ť} onSite x ┼ť^┬▒` (Tasaki (2.2.8)) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpPlus_eq_add`, `totalSpinHalfOpMinus_eq_sub` | `┼ť^┬▒_tot = ┼ť^(1)_tot ┬▒ i ┬Ě ┼ť^(2)_tot` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |

| `totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{2,3,1}` | `[┼ť_tot^(╬▒), ┼ť_tot^(╬▓)] = i ┬Ě ┼ť_tot^(╬│)` (total spin commutation) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp3_commutator_totalSpinHalfOpPlus/Minus` | `[┼ť_tot^(3), ┼ť^┬▒_tot] = ┬▒┼ť^┬▒_tot` (Cartan ladder relations) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared` / `_isHermitian` | Casimir operator `(┼ť_tot)┬▓ := ╬ú_╬▒ (┼ť_tot^(╬▒))┬▓` and its Hermiticity | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
