| `periodicChainHeisenbergGibbsState_pow_trace` | `Tr(¤ü_╬▓^n) = Z(n╬▓) / Z(╬▓)^n` for the periodic-chain Hamiltonian | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_eq` | for `N = 1` (the 2-site open chain on `Fin 2`), `H_open = -2J ┬Ě spinHalfDot 0 1` (explicit one-bond reduction; Tasaki ┬ž2.4 simplest concrete instance) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `H_open(N=1) ┬Ě |ÔćĹÔćĹÔčę = -(J/2) ┬Ě |ÔćĹÔćĹÔčę` ÔÇö `|ÔćĹÔćĹÔčę` lies in the `S = 1` triplet sector and is an exact eigenvector with eigenvalue `-J/2` (this is the ferromagnetic ground state for `J < 0`) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_singlet` | `H_open(N=1) ┬Ě (|ÔćĹÔćôÔčę - |ÔćôÔćĹÔčę) = (3J/2) ┬Ě (|ÔćĹÔćôÔčę - |ÔćôÔćĹÔčę)` ÔÇö singlet eigenvalue, the antiferromagnetic ground state for `J > 0` (Tasaki ┬ž2.5 concrete instance) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_all_down` | `H_open(N=1) ┬Ě |ÔćôÔćôÔčę = -(J/2) ┬Ě |ÔćôÔćôÔčę` ÔÇö all-down state has the same eigenvalue as all-up (both are `S = 1` triplet states) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_triplet_zero` | `H_open(N=1) ┬Ě (|ÔćĹÔćôÔčę + |ÔćôÔćĹÔčę) = -(J/2) ┬Ě (|ÔćĹÔćôÔčę + |ÔćôÔćĹÔčę)` ÔÇö triplet `m = 0` state, completing the 3-fold degenerate triplet representation `S = 1` with eigenvalue `-J/2` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_three_site_eq` | for `N = 2` (the 3-site open chain on `Fin 3`, 2 bonds), `H_open = -2J ┬Ě (spinHalfDot 0 1 + spinHalfDot 1 2)` ÔÇö explicit two-bond reduction | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_three_site_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `H_open(N=2) ┬Ě |ÔćĹÔćĹÔćĹÔčę = -J ┬Ě |ÔćĹÔćĹÔćĹÔčę` ÔÇö confirming the linear scaling `E(|ÔćĹ..ÔćĹÔčę) = -N┬ĚJ/2` (here `N = 2` bonds, `J = 1` per bond) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling_sum_eq` | for any `N : ÔäĽ`, `╬ú_{x,y Ôłł Fin (N+1)} openChainCoupling N J x y = -(2N ┬Ě J : Ôäé)` (the bond-counting lemma: each of the `N` unordered nearest-neighbour bonds is counted in both orientations) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_const` | for any `N : ÔäĽ`, `J : ÔäŁ`, and constant `s : Fin 2`, `H_open ┬Ě |s..sÔčę = -(N┬ĚJ/2 : Ôäé) ┬Ě |s..sÔčę` ÔÇö both `s = 0` (all-up) and `s = 1` (all-down) share the same eigenvalue by SU(2) symmetry | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `s = 0` specialisation of the above (Tasaki ┬ž2.4 (2.4.5)/(2.4.1) ferromagnetic ground-state energy `E_GS = -|B|┬ĚS┬▓` for `S = 1/2`, `|B| = N` bonds) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_all_down` | `s = 1` specialisation: same eigenvalue `-(N┬ĚJ/2)` for the all-down state by SU(2) symmetry | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpMinus_pow_basisVec_all_up` | for any `N : ÔäĽ`, `J : ÔäŁ`, `k : ÔäĽ`, `H_open ┬Ě ((┼ťtot^-)^k ┬Ě |ÔćĹ..ÔćĹÔčę) = -(N┬ĚJ/2 : Ôäé) ┬Ě ((┼ťtot^-)^k ┬Ě |ÔćĹ..ÔćĹÔčę)` ÔÇö the unnormalised Tasaki ┬ž2.4 (2.4.9) ferromagnetic ground states `|╬Ž_MÔčę` made explicit on the chain (combines PRs #82 + #98) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpPlus_pow_basisVec_all_down` | dual ladder from `|Ôćô..ÔćôÔčę`, same eigenvalue `-(N┬ĚJ/2)` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | `H_open` preserves every magnetisation subspace `H_M` (chain specialisation of PR #91) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | `H_periodic` preserves every magnetisation subspace `H_M` (chain specialisation of PR #91) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |

### Perron-Frobenius theorem (`Math/PerronFrobenius.lean`, `Math/PerronFrobeniusPrimitive.lean`, `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean`, `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean`)

Perron-Frobenius theorem for nonneg irreducible/primitive matrices (Issue #405, closed).
The sorry in `exists_pos_eigenvec_max` is eliminated via the Collatz-Wielandt port (PRs AÔÇôC).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `Matrix.IsPrimitive.of_irreducible_pos_diagonal` | irreducible nonneg + positive diagonal Ôćĺ primitive (Seneta ┬ž1.1, Prop. 1.3, p. 17) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusPrimitive.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.collatzWielandtFn` | CW function `min_{i\|x_i>0} (Ax)_i/x_i` (Seneta ┬ž1.2, p. 27) | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.le_any_ratio` | `CW(x) ÔëĄ (Ax)_i/x_i` for `x_i > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.le_mulVec` | fundamental inequality `CW(x)┬Ěx ÔëĄ Ax` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.smul_eq` | scale invariance `CW(cx) = CW(x)` for `c > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.upperSemicontinuousOn` | CW is upper-semicontinuous on stdSimplex | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.exists_maximizer` | CW attains its max on stdSimplex (EVT for USC, Seneta ┬ž1.2, p. 28) | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.eq_eigenvalue` | `CW(v) = r` when `Av = r┬Ěv`, `v > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.lt_of_all_ratios_gt` | all ratios `> c` Ôč╣ `CW(x) > c` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.pos_of_nonneg_eigenvec` | irreducible nonneg + `Av = ╬╝v`, `v Ôëą 0`, `v Ôëá 0` Ôč╣ `v > 0` (standard propagation argument) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.exists_positive_eigenvector_of_primitive` | primitive nonneg Ôč╣ Ôłâ `r > 0`, `v > 0` with `Av = rv` (Seneta ┬ž1.2, pp. 27ÔÇô28) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.exists_positive_eigenvector_of_irreducible` | irreducible nonneg Ôč╣ Ôłâ `r > 0`, `v > 0` with `Av = rv` (Seneta ┬ž1.2, pp. 27ÔÇô28) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `exists_nonneg_eigenvec_max` | (**sorry**, retained for docs) symmetric nonneg max eigenvalue has nonneg eigenvector | `Math/PerronFrobenius.lean` |
| `exists_pos_eigenvec_max` | (**sorry-free**) irreducible nonneg Hermitian Ôč╣ max eigenvalue has strictly positive eigenvector | `Math/PerronFrobenius.lean` |
| `pos_eigenvec_unique` | strictly positive eigenvector unique up to positive scalar | `Math/PerronFrobenius.lean` |
| `PerronFrobenius.eigenvec_proportional_of_pos_eigenvec` | **geometric simplicity of the Perron eigenvalue**: irreducible nonneg with a strictly positive eigenvector `v` at `╬╝` Ôč╣ every real eigenvector at `╬╝` is `s ÔÇó v` for some scalar `s` (the `╬╝`-eigenspace is 1-dimensional). Perturbation trick: `v + t ÔÇó u > 0` for small `t > 0` is an eigenvector at `╬╝`, hence `ÔłŁ v` by `pos_eigenvec_unique`, forcing `u ÔłŁ v`. Used to show a Perron ground state is a joint eigenvector of any commuting operator (e.g. the total Casimir) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusSimple.lean` |

References: E. Seneta, *Non-negative Matrices and Markov Chains* (3rd ed.), Springer 2006, ┬ž1.2 (pp. 27ÔÇô28);
or4nge19/MCMC: `MCMC/PF/LinearAlgebra/Matrix/PerronFrobenius/`.

### Spin-`S` MarshallÔÇôLiebÔÇôMattis on the magnetization sector (Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.2 generic S, sector form)

> **Route correction (2026-05-26).** The earlier "saturated-ladder-iterate"
> route toward `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` was found **unsound**: it reduced the
> theorem to a leaf hypothesis (lowerable attach-sum dominance) that is **false**
> ÔÇö the ferromagnetic ladder iterate `(┼ťÔü╗_tot)^M|all-upÔčę` is *not* the
> Marshall-positive antiferromagnetic ground state (concrete counterexample:
> spin-1/2, `V={a,b}`, `A={a}`, `B={b}`, `M=0Ôćĺ1`). The 40 conditional-wrapper
> modules of that route were removed; their detailed rows below were deleted with
> them. The **sound** route keeps the per-sector PerronÔÇôFrobenius ground state
> (`tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_sector_existence`, sorry-free) and chains sectors by
> SU(2) invariance `[─Ą, ┼ťÔü╗_tot] = 0`; some rows below still reference helper
> lemmas from the removed route pending re-documentation. See
> `.self-local/docs/tasaki-2-5-pf-route-design.md` and Issue #3542.

Generic-spin (`N = 2S`) version of Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.2 applied to the
**magnetization-`M` sector** of the un-dressed antiferromagnetic
Heisenberg Hamiltonian on a bipartite graph. The sector subtype
`magConfigS V N M := { ¤â : V Ôćĺ Fin (N + 1) // magSumS ¤â = M }` is the
natural index type since the dressed Heisenberg matrix is irreducible
on each sector. The core sector-matrix API lives in
`Quantum/SpinS/DressedMatrixOnMagSector.lean`; the trailing
eigenvalue-uniqueness + complex-form MLM Theorem 2.2 statements
were moved to the companion
`Quantum/SpinS/DressedMatrixOnMagSectorEigenvalueUnique.lean` in
refactor #30 (PR #2829). Tracked in Issue #412.

| Lean name | Statement |
|---|---|
| `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_succ` | **Sound PF adjacent-sector ladder link (lowering)**: if a magnetization-`M` sector vector `magSectorEmbedding ╬Ž` is a Heisenberg eigenvector at energy `╬╝` and a total-Casimir eigenvector at `╬│` away from the sector's lowering-kernel value `(card┬ĚN/2ÔłĺM)(card┬ĚN/2ÔłĺMÔłĺ1)`, then `┼ťÔü╗_tot ┬Ě magSectorEmbedding ╬Ž` is a Heisenberg eigenvector at the **same** `╬╝` (via `[─Ą, ┼ťÔü╗_tot]=0`), is non-zero (total-Casimir non-vanishing criterion), and lies in the next magnetization sector. This is the energy-preserving step that chains the per-sector PerronÔÇôFrobenius ground states (`tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_sector_existence`) across the admissible range; it uses **no** Marshall positivity of the lowered vector (the false hypothesis of the removed ferromagnetic-ladder route). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23PFLadderLink.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_pred` / `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_pred_of_mem_predictedGS` | **Sound PF adjacent-sector ladder link (raising)** and its predicted-GS specialisation: the raising companions of `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_succ(_of_mem_predictedGS)`. `┼ťÔü║_tot` maps a sector-`K` Heisenberg eigenvector (total-Casimir away from the raising-kernel value `(card┬ĚN/2ÔłĺK)(card┬ĚN/2ÔłĺK+1)`) to a non-zero same-energy eigenvector in the previous sector; predicted-GS membership in sector `M+1` strictly above the left endpoint discharges the Casimir hypotheses via `tasaki23_predictedCasimirValue_ne_raising_kernel_value_of_mem_of_left_lt`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23PFLadderLink.lean`) |
| `sublatticeMagSubspaceS` / `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS` (with `mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS_iff`, `basisVecS_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS`, `sublatticeMagSubspaceS_disjoint`) | **Sublattice magnetization grading**: the `┼ť_A^(3)`-eigenspaces `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M` and the diagonal eigenvalue `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS A ¤â = ÔłĹ_{xÔłłA}(N/2Ôłĺ¤â_x)`. Submodule API (zero/add/smul/neg/sub/finite-sum membership), disjointness of distinct eigenvalues, and basis-state membership. Sublattice analogue of `magSubspaceS`/`magEigenvalueS`; scaffold (Issue #3658, PR 5) for the sublattice highest-weight argument bounding `(┼ť_A)┬▓`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagnetization.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_groundState_sublattice_casimir_re_le` | **Sublattice Casimir bounds for the toy ground state**: the per-sector Marshall-positive toy GS is a joint Casimir eigenvector (#3657) whose `(┼ť_A)┬▓`/`(┼ť_┬ČA)┬▓` eigenvalues `╬│_A, ╬│_B` obey `╬│_A.re ÔëĄ s_A(s_A+1)`, `╬│_B.re ÔëĄ s_B(s_B+1)` (apply the spectral max bound #3672 to the non-zero Marshall-dressed embedding). Discharges two hypotheses of the toy total-Casimir pin #3676 (Issue #3674, PR 3). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToySublatticeBounds.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_extremal_sector_magnetization_re_eq_predicted` | **Extremal sector realizes the predicted total spin**: for the left-endpoint sector `M = min(|A|,|┬ČA|)┬ĚN`, the magnetization eigenvalue `|V|┬ĚN/2 Ôłĺ M` has real part `= tasaki23PredictedTotalSpin A N = |s_A Ôłĺ s_B|`. Supplies the extremal-sector hypothesis `hM` of the toy total-Casimir pin #3676 (Issue #3674, PR 4). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ExtremalSector.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_groundState_casimir_eq_predicted_of_energy_le` | **Toy ground state is the predicted-Casimir witness (modulo the energy bound)**: in the extremal sector `M = min(|A|,|┬ČA|)┬ĚN`, the Marshall-positive toy GS with toy energy `╬╝ ÔëĄ predicted Ôłĺ s_A(s_A+1) Ôłĺ s_B(s_B+1)` is a `(┼ť_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector at exactly `tasaki23PredictedCasimirValue A N`. Capstone assembling the joint eigenvector (#3657), sublattice bounds (#3677), toy energy formula (#3673), extremal sector (#3678), the two-sided pin (#3676), and Hermitian eigenvalue realness ÔÇö exactly the witness form required by #3656. The single remaining obligation is the reference energy bound `h╬╝` (Issue #3674, PR 5). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyWitness.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_sector_groundEnergy_le_of_witness` | **Toy ground energy is minimal in its sector**: the Marshall-positive toy ground state (energy `╬╝`) has `╬╝ ÔëĄ E` for every sector eigenvector `¤ć Ôëá 0` of the toy Heisenberg sector matrix at real energy `E`. Specialises `heisenbergHamiltonianSReMatrixOnMagSector_eigenvalue_ge_of_marshallPositive` to `J = bipartiteCoupling A`; reduces the reference energy bound `h╬╝` (and hence the whole route, via #3679) to exhibiting one sector eigenvector at the predicted ground energy `predicted Ôłĺ s_A(s_A+1) Ôłĺ s_B(s_B+1)` (Issue #3674, PR 6). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyGroundEnergyBound.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_total_casimir_re_eq_predicted_of_bounds` (with `tasaki23PredictedTotalSpin_nonneg`) | **Toy total-Casimir pin (extremal sector)**: in the extremal magnetization sector `M.re = |s_AÔłĺs_B|`, a non-zero `(┼ť_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│_tot` with sublattice Casimir max bounds `╬│_A ÔëĄ s_A(s_A+1)`, `╬│_B ÔëĄ s_B(s_B+1)` and reference toy energy bound `(╬│_totÔłĺ╬│_AÔłĺ╬│_B).re ÔëĄ predicted Ôłĺ s_A(s_A+1) Ôłĺ s_B(s_B+1)` has `╬│_tot.re = predicted`. A two-sided squeeze (magnetization lower bound #3675 + sublattice upper bounds #3672 + energy formula #3673), isolating the extremal-sector and reference-energy obligations as hypotheses; no ClebschÔÇôGordan (Issue #3674, PR 2). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyCasimirPin.lean`) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_ge_of_mem_magSubspaceS` (with `exists_highestWeight_eigenvector_ge`) | **Total-Casimir magnetization lower bound** `(┼ť_tot)┬▓ Ôëą m(m+1)`: a non-zero `(┼ť_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│` in `magSubspaceS V N M` with `0 ÔëĄ M.re` has `M.re┬Ě(M.re+1) ÔëĄ ╬│.re`. The total spin dominates its `z`-projection: raising with `┼ťÔü║_tot` only increases the magnetization, so the highest weight `M'` reached has `M' Ôëą M`, giving `╬│ = M'(M'+1) Ôëą M(M+1)`. Lower-bound companion of `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_le_sMax`; in the extremal sector `m = s_AÔłĺs_B` it pins the toy GS total Casimir to predicted, bypassing the ClebschÔÇôGordan triangle inequality (Issue #3674, PR 1). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirSpectralLowerBound.lean`) |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_mulVec_of_joint_casimir_eigenvector` | **Toy energy on a joint Casimir eigenvector**: a simultaneous eigenvector of `(┼ť_tot)┬▓`, `(┼ť_A)┬▓`, `(┼ť_┬ČA)┬▓` at `(╬│_tot, ╬│_A, ╬│_B)` is a `─Ą_toy`-eigenvector at `╬│_tot Ôłĺ ╬│_A Ôłĺ ╬│_B` (from `─Ą_toy = (┼ť_tot)┬▓Ôłĺ(┼ť_A)┬▓Ôłĺ(┼ť_┬ČA)┬▓`). The energy formula underlying the variational identification of the toy ground state's predicted total Casimir (Issue #3658, PR 4 step 1). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/ToyHamiltonianJointEnergy.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_eigenvalue_re_le_sA` | **Sublattice Casimir spectral max bound** `(┼ť_A)┬▓ ÔëĄ s_A(s_A+1)`: every eigenvalue `╬│` of `(┼ť_A)┬▓` (with a non-zero eigenvector) has `╬│.re ÔëĄ s_A(s_A+1)`, `s_A = |A|┬ĚN/2`. Assembles the sublattice weight-component extraction (#3670), highest-weight existence (#3671), and the highest-weight bound (#3668). Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_le_sMax`; the sublattice analytic ingredient of the Issue #3658 witness construction (Issue #3658, PR 12). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirSpectralBound.lean`) |
| `sublatticeExists_highestWeight_eigenvector` (with `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS_ne_sMax_add_one`) | **Sublattice highest-weight existence**: from a non-zero `(┼ť_A)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│` in `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A (s_AÔłĺk)`, repeatedly raising with `┼ť_A^+` (which terminates once the sublattice magnetization exceeds `s_A`, since the subspace at `s_A+1` is `ÔŐą`) produces a non-zero highest-weight `(┼ť_A)┬▓`-eigenvector at the same `╬│`. | `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_eigenvec_exists_weight_component` (with `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_sublatticeMagProjFn_eq`, `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS`) | **Sublattice weight-component extraction**: since `(┼ť_A)┬▓` commutes with `┼ť_A^(3)` it preserves each sublattice magnetization subspace and commutes with the projector; hence a non-zero `(┼ť_A)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│` has a non-zero sublattice-weight component `sublatticeMagProjFn A (s_AÔłĺk) v` that is again a `(┼ť_A)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│` in a single sublattice magnetization subspace. | `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_highestWeight_eigenvalue_re_le` (with `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS_im_zero`) | **Sublattice highest-weight Casimir bound**: a non-zero highest-weight `(┼ť_A)┬▓`-eigenvector (`┼ť_A^+ w = 0`) at `╬│` in `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M` satisfies `╬│.re ÔëĄ s_A(s_A+1)`, `s_A = |A|┬ĚN/2`. Its Casimir value is `M(M+1)` (#3663), `M` is an attained basis eigenvalue (#3667 ÔŐą-lemma), so `|M| ÔëĄ s_A` (#3660). Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSOp3_apply_diag/off_diag` and `magSubspaceS_eq_bot_of_not_in_spectrum`; lets the highest-weight argument conclude an attained sublattice magnetization is an actual basis eigenvalue (so `|M| ÔëĄ s_A`) (Issue #3658, PR 7). Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSOpPlus_mulVec_mem_magSubspaceS_of_mem`; drives the termination of the highest-weight raising for `(┼ť_A)┬▓` (Issue #3658, PR 6). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagShift.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_of_sublatticeSpinSOpPlus_eq_zero` | **Sublattice highest-weight Casimir relation**: if `┼ť_A^(3) w = m_A┬Ěw` and `┼ť_A^+ w = 0`, then `(┼ť_A)┬▓ w = m_A(m_A+1)┬Ěw`. From the sublattice Cartan identity `┼ť_A^- ┼ť_A^+ = (┼ť_A^1)┬▓+(┼ť_A^2)┬▓Ôłĺ┼ť_A^(3)` (so `(┼ť_A)┬▓ = ┼ť_A^-┼ť_A^+ + ┼ť_A^(3) + (┼ť_A^(3))┬▓`); on a highest-weight `┼ť_A^(3)`-eigenvector the `┼ť_A^-┼ť_A^+` term vanishes. Sublattice analogue of the total highest-weight relation; scaffold (Issue #3658, PR 4) for the sublattice Casimir spectral max bound. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeHighestWeight.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_of_sublatticeSpinSOpMinus_eq_zero` | **Sublattice lowest-weight Casimir relation**: if `┼ť_A^(3) w = m_A┬Ěw` and `┼ť_A^- w = 0`, then `(┼ť_A)┬▓ w = m_A(m_AÔłĺ1)┬Ěw`. From the other sublattice Cartan identity `┼ť_A^+ ┼ť_A^- = (┼ť_A^1)┬▓+(┼ť_A^2)┬▓+┼ť_A^(3)` (so `(┼ť_A)┬▓ = ┼ť_A^+┼ť_A^- Ôłĺ ┼ť_A^(3) + (┼ť_A^(3))┬▓`); on a lowest-weight vector the `┼ť_A^+┼ť_A^-` term vanishes. At `m_A = Ôłĺs_A` this gives `(┼ť_A)┬▓ = s_A(s_A+1)` (max), so the all-down sublattice state realizes the maximal `(┼ť_A)┬▓` ÔÇö a building block of the minimal-total-spin state (Issue #3674). | `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_antiAligned_eq_max` / `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_complement_antiAligned_eq_max` (with `antiAlignedConfigS`) | **Anti-aligned state realizes the maximal sublattice Casimirs**: the basis config `antiAlignedConfigS A N` (all `A`-sites up `¤â=0`, all `┬ČA`-sites down `¤â=N`) satisfies `(┼ť_A)┬▓ |¤âÔéÇÔčę = s_A(s_A+1) |¤âÔéÇÔčę` and `(┼ť_┬ČA)┬▓ |¤âÔéÇÔčę = s_B(s_B+1) |¤âÔéÇÔčę` ÔÇö the `A`-block is the sublattice highest weight (#3663), the `┬ČA`-block the sublattice lowest weight (#3681). | `tasaki23_extremal_highestWeight_totalCasimir_eq_predicted` | **Extremal-sector highest weight has the predicted total Casimir**: a vector in the extremal magnetization sector `M = min(|A|,|┬ČA|)┬ĚN` annihilated by `┼ťÔü║_tot` is a `(┼ť_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector at exactly `tasaki23PredictedCasimirValue A N` (the total highest-weight relation gives `m(m+1)` with `m = |s_AÔłĺs_B|`, via #3678). With the maximal sublattice Casimirs, such a highest-weight vector is the minimal-total-spin joint eigenstate; its existence (ClebschÔÇôGordan) is the last obligation (Issue #3674, PR 9). | `jointSublatticeCasimirEigenspace_totalSpinSOpPlus_invariant` / `_totalSpinSOpMinus_invariant` | **Ladder invariance of the joint sublattice-Casimir eigenspace**: `W = jointSublatticeCasimirEigenspace A N` (`(┼ť_A)┬▓=s_A(s_A+1)` and `(┼ť_┬ČA)┬▓=s_B(s_B+1)`) is invariant under `┼ťÔü║_tot` and `┼ťÔü╗_tot` (both sublattice Casimirs commute with them). `W` is the carrier on which `(┼ť_tot)┬▓` decomposes; the rank-nullity of `┼ťÔü║_tot` across its magnetization grading yields the minimal-total-spin highest-weight state (Issue #3674, PR 10). | `jointSublatticeCasimirEigenspace_totalSpinSOp3_invariant` | **`┼ť┬│_tot`-invariance of the joint sublattice-Casimir eigenspace**: `W = jointSublatticeCasimirEigenspace A N` is invariant under `┼ť┬│_tot` (both sublattice Casimirs commute with it), so it is graded by total magnetization `W = ÔŐĽ_M (W ÔŐô magSubspaceS V N M)`. With the ladder invariance (#3684), `W` is an `su(2)`-subrepresentation ÔÇö the setting for the rank-nullity argument producing the minimal-total-spin highest-weight state (Issue #3674, PR 11). The `su(2)` ladder identity for the `A`-sublattice on the all-up state, from the sublattice Cartan `┼ť_A^+ ┼ť_A^- = (┼ť_A)┬▓ Ôłĺ (┼ť_A^(3))┬▓ + ┼ť_A^(3)`, the maximal Casimir (#3691) and the iterate's magnetization `s_A Ôłĺ k` (#3692). Since `(k+1)(\|A\|┬ĚN Ôłĺ k) Ôëá 0` for `k < \|A\|┬ĚN`, it drives the inductive non-vanishing of the iterates; sublattice analogue of ┬ž2.4's total identity (Issue #3687, PR 6). | `sublatticeLadderIterateDownS_ne_zero` | **Non-vanishing of the sublattice ladder iterates**: for `k ÔëĄ |A|┬ĚN`, `(┼ť_A^-)^k ┬Ě |¤â_ÔŐĄÔčę Ôëá 0`. Inductive (sublattice analogue of ┬ž2.4's total non-vanishing): if `v_{k+1}=0` then `┼ť_A^+ v_{k+1} = (k+1)(|A|┬ĚNÔłĺk) v_k = 0` (the ladder identity #3693), and the scalar is non-zero for `k < |A|┬ĚN`, forcing `v_k = 0` against the IH. With the distinct magnetizations (#3692), the `|A|┬ĚN+1` iterates are linearly independent (Issue #3687, PR 7). | `sublatticeSpinSOpPlus_mulVec_jointLadderIterateDownS_succA` | **Raising the `A`-index of the joint iterate**: `┼ť_A^+ ┬Ě jointIterate (k_A+1) k_B = (k_A+1)(\|A\|┬ĚN Ôłĺ k_A) ┬Ě jointIterate k_A k_B`. Generalizes the single-sublattice ladder identity (#3693) to the joint iterate via the joint maximal `A`-Casimir (#3698) and `A`-magnetization `s_A Ôłĺ k_A` (#3700). Shows `┼ť_A^+` maps the diagonal family into the lower-index family ÔÇö half of the rankÔÇônullity step (Issue #3687, PR 14). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/JointLadderRaiseA.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOpPlus_complement_mulVec_jointLadderIterateDownS_succB` | **Raising the `┬ČA`-index of the joint iterate**: `┼ť_┬ČA^+ ┬Ě jointIterate k_A (k_B+1) = (k_B+1)(\|┬ČA\|┬ĚN Ôłĺ k_B) ┬Ě jointIterate k_A k_B`. Complement companion of #3701: `┼ť_┬ČA^+` commutes past `(┼ť_A^-)^{k_A}` (cross-sublattice) and the inner single-sublattice ladder identity (#3693 at `┬ČA`) lowers the `┬ČA`-index. With #3701, `┼ťÔü║_tot = ┼ť_A^+ + ┼ť_┬ČA^+` maps the diagonal family into the lower-index family ÔÇö the rankÔÇônullity step (Issue #3687, PR 15). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/JointLadderRaiseB.lean`) |
| `jointLadderIterateDownS_ne_zero` | **Non-vanishing of the joint ladder iterates**: for `k_A ÔëĄ |A|┬ĚN`, `k_B ÔëĄ |┬ČA|┬ĚN`, `(┼ť_A^-)^{k_A} (┼ť_┬ČA^-)^{k_B} ┬Ě |¤â_ÔŐĄÔčę Ôëá 0`. Induction on `k_A` (base = `┬ČA`-iterate non-vanishing #3694; step via the `A`-raising identity #3701 with non-zero scalar for `k_A < |A|┬ĚN`). With the joint `A`-magnetization (#3700), the diagonal-family members are non-zero eigenvectors at distinct `A`-magnetizations, hence linearly independent (Issue #3687, PR 16). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/JointLadderIterateNonvanishing.lean`) |
| `jointDiagonalIterate` / `jointDiagonalIterate_linearIndependent` | **Linear independence of the diagonal joint-ladder family**: at the extremal total magnetization `s_A Ôłĺ s_B` (WLOG `|┬ČA| ÔëĄ |A|`), the diagonal family `k_A ÔćŽ jointLadderIterateDownS A N k_A (|┬ČA|┬ĚN Ôłĺ k_A)` (`k_A = 0,ÔÇŽ,|┬ČA|┬ĚN`) is linearly independent ÔÇö each member is a non-zero `┼ť_A^(3)`-eigenvector (#3703, #3700) at the distinct eigenvalue `s_A Ôłĺ k_A`. | `toy_joint_eigenvector_energy_re_ge` | **Toy minimum-energy bound `─Ą_toy Ôëą E`** (option (a) ÔÇö coupled bound #3723 + arithmetic #3716): a joint `(┼ť_tot)┬▓`/`(┼ť_A)┬▓`/`(┼ť_┬ČA)┬▓`-eigenvector `¤ł` (eigenvalues `╬│_tot, ╬│_A, ╬│_B`) in a magnetization level (with `|┬ČA| ÔëĄ |A|`) has toy energy `(╬│_tot Ôłĺ ╬│_A Ôłĺ ╬│_B).re Ôëą E = (s_AÔłĺs_B)(s_AÔłĺs_B+1) Ôłĺ s_A(s_A+1) Ôłĺ s_B(s_B+1)`. Writing the (non-negative #3724, `ÔëĄ s(s+1)` #3672) sublattice Casimir eigenvalues as `a(a+1)`, `b(b+1)` (real spins via the square root), the coupled bound (#3723) gives `╬│_tot.re Ôëą |aÔłĺb|(|aÔłĺb|+1)`, hence `(╬│_totÔłĺ╬│_AÔłĺ╬│_B).re Ôëą |aÔłĺb|(|aÔłĺb|+1) Ôłĺ a(a+1) Ôłĺ b(b+1) = f(a,b) Ôëą f(s_A,s_B) = E` (#3716). This is the interior-sector toy minimum-energy bound (pins `r_min(M) = E` with the predicted-energy witness #3715) (Issue #3542). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyMinEnergyBound.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_min_energy_arith_eq` | **Strict (equality) arithmetic core** (option (a)): if `f(a,b) = f(s_A,s_B)` on the box `0 ÔëĄ a ÔëĄ s_A`, `0 ÔëĄ b ÔëĄ s_B ÔëĄ s_A` with `s_B > 0`, then `(a,b) = (s_A,s_B)` (the minimiser of `f(a,b) = |aÔłĺb|(|aÔłĺb|+1) Ôłĺ a(a+1) Ôłĺ b(b+1)` is the corner). When the toy energy attains its minimum `E` the toy-minimum-energy chain is tight, so this pins the toy ground state's sublattice spins to the maximal `(s_A,s_B)`, hence its total Casimir to the predicted value (Issue #3542). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyMinEnergyArithEq.lean`) |
| `toy_joint_eigenvector_totalCasimir_eq_predicted` (with `tasaki23PredictedCasimirValue_eq_sub`) | **A minimal-energy joint eigenvector has the predicted total Casimir** (option (a), interior-sector culmination): for `|┬ČA| ÔëĄ |A|`, `s_B > 0`, a joint `(┼ť_tot)┬▓`/`(┼ť_A)┬▓`/`(┼ť_┬ČA)┬▓`-eigenvector `¤ł` (eigenvalues `╬│_tot, ╬│_A, ╬│_B`) whose toy energy `(╬│_tot Ôłĺ ╬│_A Ôłĺ ╬│_B).re ÔëĄ E` (the predicted minimum) satisfies `╬│_tot = tasaki23PredictedCasimirValue A N`. The toy minimum-energy bound (#3725) forces `(╬│_tot Ôłĺ ╬│_A Ôłĺ ╬│_B).re = E`; the coupled bound + equality arithmetic (#3727) force the sublattice spins to the corner `(s_A,s_B)`, whence `╬│_tot.re = (s_AÔłĺs_B)(s_AÔłĺs_B+1) = tasaki23PredictedCasimirValue`. This is the per-sector toy-ground-state predicted-Casimir witness (any admissible sector), supplying `hsource_cas` for the overlap pin (Issue #3542). | `exists_predictedEnergy_sector_eigenvector_of_mem` | **A predicted-energy real sector eigenvector in every admissible sector** (option (a)): generalising #3710 (extremal sector) to each `M Ôłł tasaki23GroundStateSectors A N` via the joint predicted eigenvector at that sector (#3715). The joint eigenvector `magSectorEmbedding ╬Ž` is a full-space `heisenbergHamiltonianS (bipartiteCoupling A)`-eigenvector at the predicted toy energy `E` (Casimir energy formula #3673); restricting to the sector and taking real/imaginary parts (real coupling) gives a non-zero real eigenvector of `heisenbergHamiltonianSReMatrixOnMagSector (bipartiteCoupling A) N M` at `E`. | `tasaki23_toy_groundState_casimir_eq_predicted_at` | **The toy ground state has the predicted total Casimir in every admissible sector** (option (a), generalising #3711 from the extremal sector): for `|┬ČA| ÔëĄ |A|`, `s_B > 0`, in each `M Ôłł tasaki23GroundStateSectors A N` there is a Marshall-positive `v > 0` whose embedding `magSectorEmbedding (sign ┬Ě v)` is a `(┼ť_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector at `tasaki23PredictedCasimirValue A N`. | `tasaki23_sector_lift_and_casimir`, `tasaki23_common_energy_step`, `tasaki23_common_groundEnergy` | **Common ground-state energy across every admissible sector (constancy half of Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3)** (option (a), TIER 4, PR #3732): for a connected bipartite antiferromagnetic coupling `J` with `|┬ČA| ÔëĄ |A|`, `s_B > 0`, there is a single energy `╬╝` such that in *every* admissible sector `M Ôłł tasaki23GroundStateSectors A N` the Marshall-positive PerronÔÇôFrobenius ground state (from `exists_marshallSign_eigenvector_ÔÇŽ`) has energy `╬╝`. | `tasaki23_eigenvalue_ge_common` | **Global minimality of the common energy ÔÇö sector minÔÇômax engine** (option (a), TIER 5, PR #3733): given the common energy `╬╝` realised by a Marshall-positive ground state in every admissible sector (`hcommon`, #3732) and a lower bound `hOutside` for the non-admissible sectors, every non-zero full-space eigenvector of `heisenbergHamiltonianS J N` at energy `╬╝'` satisfies `╬╝ ÔëĄ ╬╝'`. Proof: a non-zero eigenvector has a non-zero magnetisation-sector restriction (`eq_sum_magSectorEmbedding_magSectorRestriction`), which is a sector eigenvector at `╬╝'` (`ÔÇŽ_magSectorRestriction_of_full_eigen`); its non-zero real or imaginary part (`ÔÇŽ_re/_im_of_complex_eigenvec`) is a real sector eigenvector; the CollatzÔÇôWielandt per-sector bound (`ÔÇŽ_eigenvalue_ge_of_marshallPositive`) closes the admissible case, `hOutside` the non-admissible case. The non-admissible bound `hOutside` is the LiebÔÇôMattis energy monotonicity `E(S_tot)` beyond the band, isolated as an explicit hypothesis (Issue #3542). | `tasaki23_toy_sector_energy_ge_predicted` | **Toy sector energy lower bound ÔÇö LiebÔÇôMattis lower bound for the toy Hamiltonian** (option (a), TIER 5, PR #3734): for the bipartite toy Hamiltonian `J = bipartiteCoupling A` (`|┬ČA| ÔëĄ |A|`), in *every* magnetisation sector `M` the predicted minimum energy `(bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted A N).re` is below every dressed-sector eigenvalue `╬╝M` ÔÇö not just the admissible band. | `totalSpinSOpMinus_mulVec_normSq_eq`, `totalSpinSOpMinus_mulVec_ne_zero_of_pos_weight`, `totalSpinSOpPlus_mulVec_ne_zero_of_neg_weight` | **One total-spin ladder step is non-vanishing on a weight vector** (general-`J` `hOutside` thread, step 1, PR #3736): from `[┼ťÔü║_tot, ┼ťÔü╗_tot] = 2 ┼ť┬│_tot` (the two Casimir rearrangements) and `(┼ťÔü╗)ÔÇá = ┼ťÔü║`, a weight-`w` vector `╬Ž` (`┼ť┬│_tot ╬Ž = w ╬Ž`) satisfies `ÔÇľ┼ťÔü╗_tot ╬ŽÔÇľ┬▓ = ÔÇľ┼ťÔü║_tot ╬ŽÔÇľ┬▓ + 2 w ÔÇľ╬ŽÔÇľ┬▓`. Hence `┼ťÔü╗_tot ╬Ž Ôëá 0` when `╬Ž Ôëá 0` and `w.re > 0`, and dually `┼ťÔü║_tot ╬Ž Ôëá 0` when `w.re < 0`. This is the SU(2) non-vanishing input for the inward-ladder discharge of the non-admissible-sector lower bound: an eigenvector outside the band can be moved to the band edge by `┼ťÔłô_tot` without annihilation (Issue #3542). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42; LiebÔÇôMattis, J. Math. Phys. 3 (1962) 749 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23TotalLoweringNonvanishing.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_admissible_eigenvector_ge`, `raise_iterate_ne_zero`, `lower_iterate_ne_zero`, `tasaki23_general_hOutside` | **General-`J` non-admissible-sector lower bound via the SU(2) inward ladder** (general-`J` `hOutside` thread, steps 2ÔÇô3, PR #3737): discharges, for an ARBITRARY connected bipartite antiferromagnetic coupling `J`, the `hOutside` hypothesis of the global-minimality engine (#3733) ÔÇö every dressed-sector eigenvalue `╬╝M` in a non-admissible sector `M` is `Ôëą` the admissible common energy `╬╝`. A non-admissible eigenvector is lifted to the full Hilbert space, then moved to the admissible band edge by iterating `┼ťÔü║_tot` (if `M` is above the band) or `┼ťÔü╗_tot` (if below): each step commutes with `H` (energy preserved, `heisenbergHamiltonianS_mulVec_totalSpinSOp{Plus,Minus}_mulVec`) and is non-zero (#3736, intermediate weights have the right sign), with weight shifts (`totalSpinSOp{Plus,Minus}_mulVec_mem_magSubspaceS_of_mem`). The landed non-zero eigenvector lies in an admissible sector, where the CollatzÔÇôWielandt per-sector bound `tasaki23_admissible_eigenvector_ge` (the reused admissible core of the engine) gives `╬╝ ÔëĄ ╬╝M`. This needs only the SU(2) ladder, not reflection positivity (Issue #3542). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42; LiebÔÇôMattis, J. Math. | `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` / `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_bipartiteToy` | **Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3 truly-unconditional capstone for the bipartite toy coupling** (#3887 extension chain, Thm23-#3887.12ÔÇô.21, PR #3891): the canonical `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` API is now the structural statement, dropping the vacuous-at-`N=1` `h_intermediate` hypothesis (`ÔłÇ ¤ä, ÔłÇ x, Ôłâ z, A z Ôëá A x Ôłž (¤ä z).val < N`; unsatisfiable when `¤ä Ôëí Fin.last 1`). The toy capstone `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_bipartiteToy` proves the conclusion from `(1 ÔëĄ N, 1 ÔëĄ |A|, 1 ÔëĄ |┬ČA|)`, the genuine physical hypotheses, by chaining structural variants of the entire MLM / sector existence / Casimir / lower-bound stack: complex sector Marshall eigenvector (#3887.12), full-Hilbert lift (#3887.13), four-level Marshall-positive uniqueness chain (#3887.14), Theorem 2.2 bundled full (#3887.15), Theorem 2.3 sector existence wrapper (#3887.16), toy sector energy lower bound (#3887.17), per-sector toy ground state at predicted (#3887.19), final capstone (#3887.21). At spin-1/2 (N=1) this is the **first truly-unconditional Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3 closure**. The already-`h_intermediate`-free pieces (`tasaki23_eigenvalue_ge_common`, `tasaki23_toy_sector_groundEnergy_le_of_witness`, `exists_predictedEnergy_sector_eigenvector_of_mem`) are reused directly. | `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_of_bipartiteCompletePositive` | **Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3 truly-unconditional capstone for general bipartite J** (PR #3893, Issue #3892): the canonical general-`J` closure proves `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3 A N J c` for ANY real symmetric non-negative bipartite coupling `J` positive on the complete bipartite graph at any `N Ôëą 1`, under canonical orientation `|┬ČA| ÔëĄ |A|` with `s_B > 0`. This removes BOTH the toy-coupling restriction of PR #3891 and the vacuous-at-N=1 `h_intermediate` restriction of the older #3738 capstone. At spin-1/2 (N=1) this is the **first truly-unconditional Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3 closure for general bipartite J**. Chain: structural sector lift + Casimir (Step 1), structural adjacent-sector common-energy step (Step 2), structural common ground-state energy (Step 3, TIER 4 constancy), capstone (Step 4) combining Step 3 with PR #3891's `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_sector_existence` and the already-`h_intermediate`-free `tasaki23_general_hOutside` + `tasaki23_eigenvalue_ge_common` + `heisenbergHamiltonianSReMatrixOnMagSector_eigenvalue_ge_of_marshallPositive`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42; LiebÔÇôMattis, J. Math. Phys. 3 (1962) 749 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23StructuralGeneralFinal.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_unique_groundState_has_zero_magnetization` | **Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4 partial conclusion: unique GS has zero `┼ť┬│_tot` magnetization** (Issue #3739, PR #3896): if the anisotropic Heisenberg ground-state eigenspace has `finrank Ôäé ÔëĄ 1`, every non-zero ground state `╬Ž` satisfies `┼ť┬│_tot ╬Ž = 0`. Proof: ╬Ž, ┼ť┬│_tot ╬Ž, ╬ś ╬Ž all lie in the GS eigenspace (`H` commutes with `┼ť┬│_tot` (#3741) and with the reflection `╬ś` (#3745)). From `finrank ÔëĄ 1` + ╬Ž Ôëá 0 and `Module.finrank_le_one_iff`, `┼ť┬│_tot ╬Ž = ╬│ ╬Ž` and `╬ś ╬Ž = ╬┤ ╬Ž` for some scalars. `╬ś┬▓ = 1` Ôč╣ `╬┤┬▓ = 1` Ôč╣ `╬┤ Ôëá 0`. Apply the anticommutation `╬ś ┼ť┬│_tot = -┼ť┬│_tot ╬ś` (#3744) to ╬Ž: get `╬┤╬│ ÔÇó ╬Ž = -╬│╬┤ ÔÇó ╬Ž` Ôč╣ `2╬┤╬│ ÔÇó ╬Ž = 0` Ôč╣ `╬┤╬│ = 0` Ôč╣ `╬│ = 0` (since ╬┤ Ôëá 0). This is the second conclusion of Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4 once uniqueness is established (uniqueness itself awaits the deformation argument from the SU(2) point, separate forthcoming work). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43-44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem24ZeroMagnetizationFromUniqueness.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_at_SU2_eigenspace_eq_heisenbergHamiltonianS` / `heisenbergHamiltonianS_unique_groundState_has_zero_magnetization` | **Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4 SU(2) base case via Theorem 2.3 reduction** (Issue #3739, PR #3897): at the SU(2) point `(╬╗=1, D=0)`, the anisotropic Hamiltonian reduces to the isotropic Heisenberg Hamiltonian (via `anisotropicHeisenbergS_one_zero`), so every eigenspace coincides. The first lemma gives the direct eigenspace identification; the second specializes PR #3896 to the SU(2) point, giving `┼ť┬│_tot|╬ŽÔčę=0` for any non-zero GS of the isotropic Heisenberg Hamiltonian under the GS uniqueness hypothesis. Prepares the SU(2) base case for the deformation argument: once uniqueness is established at the SU(2) point (for symmetric `|A|=|┬ČA|` via Theorem 2.2 + SU(2) singlet structure, separate forthcoming work), the `┼ť┬│_tot|╬ŽÔčę=0` conclusion follows immediately. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43-44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem24SU2BaseCase.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_apply_eq_zero_of_magSumS_ne` | **Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4 sector-crossing matrix entries vanish** (Issue #3739, PR #3898): the matrix entry `anisotropicHeisenbergS J ╬╗ D N ¤â ¤ä = 0` whenever `magSumS ¤â Ôëá magSumS ¤ä` ÔÇö the matrix-element form of U(1) invariance `[H, ┼ť┬│_tot] = 0` (#3741). Proof: evaluate `H ┬Ě ┼ť┬│_tot = ┼ť┬│_tot ┬Ě H` at `(¤â, ¤ä)`; since `┼ť┬│_tot` is diagonal with entries `magEigenvalueS`, get `H ¤â ¤ä ┬Ě M_¤ä = M_¤â ┬Ě H ¤â ¤ä`. When `magSumS ¤â Ôëá magSumS ¤ä`, Nat-cast injectivity gives `magEigenvalueS ¤â Ôëá magEigenvalueS ¤ä`, so `H ¤â ¤ä = 0`. Key building block for sector-decomposition arguments toward Theorem 2.4 obligation (2.a) uniqueness. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43-44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSectorZero.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_magSectorProjection_eigen` | **Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4 sector projection of eigenvector is eigenvector** (Issue #3739, PR #3899): for an eigenvector `v` of the anisotropic Hamiltonian at `╬╝`, the sector projection `magSectorEmbedding (magSectorRestriction (M:=M) v)` is also an eigenvector at the same `╬╝`. Vector-level analog of PR #3898: only same-sector entries survive in the `mulVec` expansion (cross-sector entries vanish by U(1) invariance). This enables sector-by-sector spectral decomposition of any anisotropic H-eigenvector, key building block toward Theorem 2.4 obligation (2.a) uniqueness via the sector-decomposition + reflection argument. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43-44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicSectorProjectionEigenvector.lean`) |
| `linearIndependent_pair_of_magSubspaceS_distinct` | **Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4 distinct-sector pair LI** (Issue #3739, PR #3900): non-zero vectors in distinct magnetization sectors `MÔéü Ôëá MÔéé` are linearly independent. Direct consequence of `magSubspaceS_disjoint` (existing): for `a ÔÇó ╬ŽÔéü + b ÔÇó ╬ŽÔéé = 0`, get `a ÔÇó ╬ŽÔéü Ôłł magSubspaceS ╬Ť N MÔéü Ôłę magSubspaceS ╬Ť N MÔéé = ÔŐą`, so `a ÔÇó ╬ŽÔéü = 0`, giving `a = 0` (since ╬ŽÔéü Ôëá 0), and similarly `b = 0`. Key technical lemma for the sector-decomposition + reflection argument toward Theorem 2.4 obligation (2.a): non-zero vectors in three pairwise distinct sectors form a 3-LI family, contradicting obligation (1)'s `finrank ÔëĄ 2`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43-44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/MagSubspaceDistinctLI.lean`) |
| `linearIndependent_triple_of_magSubspaceS_distinct` | **Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4 distinct-triple-sector LI** (Issue #3739, PR #3901): three non-zero vectors in three pairwise distinct magnetization sectors `MÔéü Ôëá MÔéé`, `MÔéü Ôëá MÔéâ`, `MÔéé Ôëá MÔéâ` are linearly independent. Direct application of mathlib's `Module.End.eigenvectors_linearIndependent'` to `Matrix.toLin' (totalSpinSOp3 ╬Ť N)` viewed as a linear endomorphism ÔÇö `magSubspaceS = End.eigenspace` of `totalSpinSOp3`. This is the contradiction lever for the SU(2) symmetric `finrank ÔëĄ 1` argument: admissible-sector GS + non-admissible GS + reflected non-admissible GS = 3-LI family contradicting obligation (1) `finrank ÔëĄ 2`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43-44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/MagSubspaceDistinctTripleLI.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_threeLI_of_admis_and_nonadmis` | **Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4 three-LI from admissible + non-admissible eigenvectors** (Issue #3739, PR #3902): for an admissible-sector eigenvector ╬Ž (at `┼ť┬│_tot = 0`) and a non-admissible-sector eigenvector ╬Ę (at `┼ť┬│_tot = M' Ôëá 0`) at the same energy, the family `{╬Ž, ╬Ę, ╬ś ╬Ę}` is linearly independent, where `╬ś ╬Ę Ôłł magSubspaceS ╬Ť N (-M')` is the reflected non-admissible eigenvector. Combines PR #3901's triple-LI with: (i) `manyBodyReversalS_mulVec_mem_magSubspaceS_neg` (reflection shifts sector M Ôćĺ -M); (ii) `╬ś┬▓ = 1` giving `╬ś ╬Ę Ôëá 0` from `╬Ę Ôëá 0`; (iii) `M' Ôëá 0` Ôč╣ `0 Ôëá M'`, `0 Ôëá -M'`, `M' Ôëá -M'` (the latter via `2M' = 0` Ôč╣ `M' = 0`). The contradiction lever for the SU(2) symmetric `finrank ÔëĄ 1` argument: this 3-LI family combined with obligation (1) `finrank ÔëĄ 2` forces no non-admissible-sector eigenstate at the GS energy. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43-44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem24ThreeLIFromNonAdmis.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_finrank_le_two_no_admis_plus_nonadmis` | **Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4 finrank ÔëĄ 2 + admissible + non-admissible Ôćĺ False** (Issue #3739, PR #3903): if the anisotropic Hamiltonian eigenspace at `╬╝` has `finrank ÔëĄ 2` AND contains both an admissible-sector eigenvector ╬Ž (at `┼ť┬│_tot = 0`) and a non-admissible-sector eigenvector ╬Ę (at `┼ť┬│_tot = M' Ôëá 0`), then `False`. Proof: PR #3902 gives `{╬Ž, ╬Ę, ╬ś ╬Ę}` linearly independent; `╬ś ╬Ę` is also at energy ╬╝ via ╬ś commutes with H (#3745); the 3-LI family lifts to the eigenspace `ÔćąE` via `LinearIndependent.of_comp E.subtype`; `LinearIndependent.fintype_card_le_finrank` gives `3 ÔëĄ finrank Ôäé ÔćąE`, contradicting `finrank ÔëĄ 2` via `omega`. This is the direct contradiction in the SU(2) symmetric `finrank ÔëĄ 1` argument. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43-44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem24FinrankLeTwoContradiction.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigvec_nonadmis_projection_zero` | **Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4 eigvec non-admissible projection = 0** (Issue #3739, PR #3904): under `finrank ÔëĄ 2` at `╬╝` + admissible eigvec exists, any eigvec ╬Ę at ╬╝ has zero `magSectorEmbedding (magSectorRestriction (M := M) ╬Ę)` for every non-admissible sector index `M` (with `2M Ôëá |╬Ť|┬ĚN`). Proof: the projection is an eigvec at ╬╝ via PR #3899 and lies in `magSubspaceS ╬Ť N M'` with `M' = |╬Ť|┬ĚN/2 - M Ôëá 0` (non-admissible ┼ť┬│_tot eigenvalue). If non-zero, PR #3903 derives False; hence the projection is `0`. Penultimate step toward the SU(2) symmetric `finrank ÔëĄ 1`: combined with `eq_sum_magSectorEmbedding_magSectorRestriction`, the eigenspace at ╬╝ is contained in the admissible sector. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43-44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem24EigvecNonadmisProjectionZero.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigvec_in_admis_sector_of_finrank_le_two` | **Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4 eigvec at ╬╝ Ôłł admissible sector** (Issue #3739, PR #3905): under `finrank ÔëĄ 2` at `╬╝` + admissible eigvec exists, any eigvec ╬Ę at ╬╝ lies in `magSubspaceS ╬Ť N 0` (the `┼ť┬│_tot = 0` admissible sector). Proof: decompose ╬Ę via `eq_sum_magSectorEmbedding_magSectorRestriction`; each summand at non-admissible M is `0` by PR #3904; each summand at admissible M (where `2M = |╬Ť|┬ĚN`) lies in `magSubspaceS ╬Ť N (|╬Ť|┬ĚN/2 - M) = magSubspaceS ╬Ť N 0`; sum of submodule members is in the submodule. Final piece toward the SU(2) symmetric `finrank ÔëĄ 1` argument: combined with within-admissible-sector PF `finrank = 1`, the full Hilbert space finrank at ╬╝ is `ÔëĄ 1`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43-44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem24EigvecInAdmisSector.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_finrank_le_one_from_admis_pf` | **Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4 `finrank ÔëĄ 1` from admis. PF + `finrank ÔëĄ 2`** (Issue #3739, PR #3906): if the anisotropic Hamiltonian eigenspace at ╬╝ has `finrank ÔëĄ 2` AND an admissible eigvec exists at ╬╝ AND the within-admissible-sector eigenspace at ╬╝ has `finrank ÔëĄ 1`, then the full Hilbert space eigenspace at ╬╝ has `finrank ÔëĄ 1`. Proof: by PR #3905, the eigenspace `E` is contained in the admissible submodule `A`, so `E ÔŐô A = E`; the within-admissible hypothesis gives `finrank (E ÔŐô A) ÔëĄ 1`; substitution gives `finrank E ÔëĄ 1`. | `spinSDotXXZ`, `singleIonAnisotropyS`, `anisotropicHeisenbergS` | **Anisotropic (XXZ + single-ion) antiferromagnetic Hamiltonian** (Tasaki (2.5.14), Theorem 2.4 foundation, Issue #3739, PR #3740): `─Ą = ╬ú_{x,y} J_{x,y}(┼ť_xÔüŻ┬╣Ôüż┼ť_yÔüŻ┬╣Ôüż + ┼ť_xÔüŻ┬▓Ôüż┼ť_yÔüŻ┬▓Ôüż + ╬╗ ┼ť_xÔüŻ┬│Ôüż┼ť_yÔüŻ┬│Ôüż) + D ╬ú_x (┼ť_xÔüŻ┬│Ôüż)┬▓` with Ising anisotropy `╬╗` and crystal-field `D`. Hermitian for real `J, ╬╗, D` (`anisotropicHeisenbergS_isHermitian_of_real`); reduces to the isotropic Heisenberg Hamiltonian at `╬╗ = 1, D = 0` (`anisotropicHeisenbergS_one_zero`, in `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergReduction.lean` ÔÇö split out so the core defs do not import the heavy `Heisenberg` module). Each site `┼ť┬│` commutes with `┼ť┬│_tot` (`onSiteS_spinSOp3_commute_totalSpinSOp3`), so the longitudinal `┼ť┬│┼ť┬│` and single-ion terms do; the transverse `┼ť┬╣┼ť┬╣+┼ť┬▓┼ť┬▓` part commutes via `spinSDotXXZ = spinSDot + (╬╗Ôłĺ1)┼ť┬│┼ť┬│` and `spinSDot_commutator_totalSpinSOp3`. This is the symmetry underlying the sector-by-sector MattisÔÇôNishimori argument. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergU1.lean`) |
| `spinSOp1_mul_spinSOp1_add_spinSOp2_mul_spinSOp2`, `spinSOp1_mul_spinSOp1_sub_spinSOp2_mul_spinSOp2` | **Transverse spin operators in ladder form** (Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, eq. 2.5.16, Issue #3739, PR #3742): `┼ť┬╣┼ť┬╣ + ┼ť┬▓┼ť┬▓ = ┬Ż(┼ťÔü║┼ťÔü╗ + ┼ťÔü╗┼ťÔü║)` (longitudinal-preserving) and `┼ť┬╣┼ť┬╣ Ôłĺ ┼ť┬▓┼ť┬▓ = ┬Ż(┼ťÔü║┼ťÔü║ + ┼ťÔü╗┼ťÔü╗)` (longitudinal-flipping by ┬▒2), via `┼ť┬╣ = (┼ťÔü║+┼ťÔü╗)/2`, `┼ť┬▓ = (┼ťÔü║Ôłĺ┼ťÔü╗)/(2i)` and `(1/(2i))┬▓ = Ôłĺ1/4`. These are the single-site identities behind Tasaki's rewriting (2.5.16) of the anisotropic XXZ Hamiltonian into ladder form for the Marshall-sign analysis. | `manyBodyTensorS`, `manyBodyTensorS_mul` | **Many-body tensor (product) operator and its functoriality** (Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3750): `manyBodyTensorS W = ÔŐŚ_x W x` (entry `ÔłĆ_x (W x) (¤â' x) (¤â x)`), with `manyBodyTensorS W * manyBodyTensorS W' = manyBodyTensorS (fun x => W x * W' x)` (a product of sums is a sum of products, `Fintype.prod_sum`) and `manyBodyTensorS (fun _ => 1) = 1`. This is the general single-site-operator lift (generalising the configuration-permutation `manyBodyReversalS` to a non-permutation `W`, e.g. the Wigner `d`-matrix gauge rotation); it gives `╬ś_U (onSiteS z A) ╬ś_UÔü╗┬╣ = onSiteS z (U A UÔü╗┬╣)` for the axis-swap gauge of Theorem 2.4. This is the single-site step that lifts the axis-swap rotation to `╬ś_U ─Ą ╬ś_{UÔü╗┬╣} = ─Ą'`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43ÔÇô44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/ManyBodyTensorConj.lean`) |

Its many-body lift `╬ś_U = ÔŐŚ_x U` satisfies `╬ś_{UÔü╗┬╣} ╬ś_U = 1`, conjugates products (`tensor_conj_mul`) and single-site operators (`tensor_conj_onSiteS`), and carries the anisotropic Hamiltonian (2.5.14) to its axis-swapped image (2.5.15): `╬ś_U ─Ą ╬ś_{UÔü╗┬╣} = ─Ą'` (`tensor_conj_anisotropicHeisenbergS`; the `┼ť┬│ Ôćĺ Ôłĺ┼ť┬▓` sign squares away in the bilinear bond and `(┼ť┬│)┬▓` terms). With the similarity invariant #3746 this transfers the ground-state degeneracy; the explicit `U` (spin-1/2 / Wigner `d`-matrix) instantiates the interface. | `spinReversalS`, `spinReversalS_conj_spinSOp3`, `spinReversalS_conj_spinSOpPlus`, `spinReversalS_conj_spinSOpMinus` | **Single-site spin reversal (¤Ç-rotation about axis 1)** (Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3743): the permutation matrix `F` of `Fin.rev` (`k ÔćŽ NÔłĺk`); conjugation reindexes by `Fin.rev` (`(F┬ĚM┬ĚF) i j = M (rev i) (rev j)`), giving `F ┼ť┬│ F = Ôłĺ┼ť┬│`, `F ┼ťÔü║ F = ┼ťÔü╗`, `F ┼ťÔü╗ F = ┼ťÔü║` (hence `┼ť┬╣ÔćŽ┼ť┬╣`, `┼ť┬▓ÔćŽÔłĺ┼ť┬▓`), and `F` is an involution. The many-site product `╬ś = ÔŐŚ_x F` will give the `M Ôćö ÔłĺM` reflection symmetry `╬ś ┼ť┬│_tot ╬śÔü╗┬╣ = Ôłĺ┼ť┬│_tot`, `╬ś ─Ą ╬śÔü╗┬╣ = ─Ą` used in the MattisÔÇôNishimori uniqueness argument. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43ÔÇô44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SpinSReversal.lean`) |

This is the `M Ôćö ÔłĺM` magnetization reflection used in the MattisÔÇôNishimori uniqueness argument (`E_M = E_{ÔłĺM}` and, with the ground state in `H_0`, `┼ť┬│_tot ╬Ž_GS = 0`). | `parityReachableS_transfer_n_units` | **Iterated `n`-unit cross-sublattice transfer** (Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3801): from `¤â` with `n ÔëĄ ¤â a` and `¤â b + n ÔëĄ N` on a bipartite bond `(a, b)`, the config shifting `n` units from `a` to `b` is `ParityReachableS`-reachable.  Proven by induction on `n` composing single transverse moves at the same bond. | `parityReachableS_drain_a_into_a0` | **Drain `a` into `aÔéÇ` via the iterated shuffle** (Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3802): starting from `¤â`, the config with `¤â' a = 0` and `¤â' aÔéÇ = (¤â aÔéÇ).val + (¤â a).val` (with `b` net unchanged and everything else unchanged) is `ParityReachableS`-reachable provided `(¤â aÔéÇ).val + (¤â a).val ÔëĄ N` and `(¤â b).val + 1 ÔëĄ N`. | `parityReachableS_drainSetInto` | **Multi-site concentration via iterated drain** (Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3803): for a finite set `S` of sites disjoint from `aÔéÇ` and adjacent to `b` (and `aÔéÇ` adjacent to `b`), starting from `¤â` with `(¤â aÔéÇ).val + ÔłĹ_{sÔłłS} (¤â s).val ÔëĄ N` and `(¤â b).val + 1 ÔëĄ N`, the config that drains every `s Ôłł S` (setting `¤â' s = 0`) into `aÔéÇ` (setting `¤â' aÔéÇ = (¤â aÔéÇ).val + ÔłĹ_{sÔłłS} (¤â s).val`) is `ParityReachableS`-reachable. | `parityReachableS_concentrate_A_at_a0` | **A-side concentration at `aÔéÇ`** (Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3804): starting from `¤â` with `aÔéÇ Ôłł A` and a B-site `b Ôłë A`, the config that drains every A-site `a Ôëá aÔéÇ` into `aÔéÇ` is `ParityReachableS`-reachable.  Specialises #3803 with `S = aSitesExcept A aÔéÇ = Finset.univ.filter (fun v => A v = true Ôłž v Ôëá aÔéÇ)`.  Hypotheses: `A aÔéÇ`, `┬Č A b`, `(¤â aÔéÇ).val + ÔłĹ_{s Ôłł aSitesExcept A aÔéÇ} (¤â s).val ÔëĄ N`, `(¤â b).val + 1 ÔëĄ N`. | `parityReachableS_concentrate_B_at_b0` | **B-side concentration at `bÔéÇ`** (Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3805): symmetric to #3804 with A/B swapped. Hypotheses: `┬Č A bÔéÇ`, `A a`, `(¤â bÔéÇ).val + ÔłĹ_{s Ôłł bSitesExcept A bÔéÇ} (¤â s).val ÔëĄ N`, `(¤â a).val + 1 ÔëĄ N`. | `parityReachableS_concentrate_AB` | **Combined A+B concentration to a two-site canonical form** (Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3806): composing #3804 (A-concentration via `bÔéÇ` as temp) with #3805 (B-concentration via the post-A-concentration `aÔéÇ Ôłł A` as temp).  The resulting `¤â_AB` has `¤â_AB aÔéÇ = (¤â aÔéÇ).val + ╬ú_{aSitesExcept} (¤â s).val` (total A-mass), `¤â_AB bÔéÇ = (¤â bÔéÇ).val + ╬ú_{bSitesExcept} (¤â s).val` (total B-mass), and `¤â_AB = 0` on `aSitesExcept Ôł¬ bSitesExcept`. | `parityReachableS_canonical_transfer` | **Canonical-to-canonical reachability at the same total magnetization** (Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3807): from a two-site canonical config (mass concentrated at `(aÔéÇ, bÔéÇ)`), an iterated transverse at the canonical bond `(aÔéÇ, bÔéÇ)` transfers `n` units between `aÔéÇ` and `bÔéÇ`, preserving the canonical shape (`= 0` elsewhere) and the total `m_A + m_B`. Given per-block `ÔëĄ 1` bounds, full dressed eig `ÔëĄ 2`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43ÔÇô44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/DressedAxisSwapDegeneracyBound.lean`) |

| `matrix_similar_eigenspace_inter_finrank_eq` | **(g.3) similarity-invariant intersection eigenspace finrank** (Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3835): generalises `matrix_similar_eigenspace_finrank_eq` (#3746 family) to intersections with a second operator's eigenspace, provided the similarity `U` commutes with that operator `Q`. Given `U * Uinv = 1`, `Uinv * U = 1`, `H' = Uinv * H * U`, AND `Q * U = U * Q`, then `finrank Ôäé (eig H' ╬╝ ÔŐô ker(Q=c)) = finrank Ôäé (eig H ╬╝ ÔŐô ker(Q=c))`. The core technical step for transferring per-parity-block PF bounds from the dressed `─Ą'_dressed` to the bare `─Ą'`, since the Marshall sign diagonal `╬ś_A` commutes with the diagonal `magParityDiagS P`. Key observation: the Marshall sign diagonal `╬ś_A` and the magnetization parity diagonal `P = magParityDiagS` are both diagonal and hence commute, so the intersection `eig ─Ą' ╬╝ ÔŐô ker(P=c)` is preserved by the Marshall similarity dressed Ôćĺ bare. Result for `p < 2`: `Ôłâ ╬Ż : ÔäŁ, finrank Ôäé (eig (axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS J lam D N) (╬Ż : Ôäé) ÔŐô ker(P=(-1)^p)) ÔëĄ 1`. | `anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_of_blocks_le_one` (with `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_of_blocks_le_one`) | **(g.5) bare anisotropic `─Ą` eigenspace `finrank Ôäé ÔëĄ 2` (conditional, via axis-swap)** (Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3837): wraps the conditional `bare ─Ą' eig ÔëĄ 2 from per-block ÔëĄ 1` (`axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_of_blocks_le_one`, #3776 family) with the axis-swap unitary equivalence (`AxisSwapUnitaryS.anisotropic_axisSwapped_eigenspace_finrank_eq`, #3753) to give the same conditional bound for the bare anisotropic Hamiltonian `─Ą`. General `N` version requires an explicit `AxisSwapUnitaryS N` instance; spin-1/2 (`N = 1`) instance via `axisSwapUnitarySpinHalf`. This **closes the bare `─Ą` ÔëĄ 2 link in the (g) chain**, conditional on per-parity-block PF hypotheses (discharged separately per sector by #3836). The remaining gap to unconditional obligation (1) is the ╬Ż-sector matching (PF eigenvalues of even/odd sectors coinciding at the ground-state energy), which is mathematically distinct (Hermitian spectral minimisation) and deferred. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43ÔÇô44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/BareAnisotropicEigLeTwoConditional.lean`) |
| `parity_block_submatrix_full_inter_finrank_eq` (with `parity_block_submatrix_eigenspace_finrank_le_full_inter`, `parityEmbedMap`) | **(h.1) reverse block-diag bridge** (Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3840): for a parity-block-diagonal complex `M`, the parity-`p` submatrix's ╬Ż-eigenspace embeds INJECTIVELY into `eig M ╬Ż ÔŐô ker(P=(-1)^p)` via the zero-extension map (extend by 0 off `parityConfigS p`). Combined with the forward direction (#3832), gives the finrank equality `finrank Ôäé (eig M.submatrix ╬Ż) = finrank Ôäé (eig M ╬Ż ÔŐô ker(P=(-1)^p))` for `p < 2`. The equality unlocks ╬Ż-sector matching arguments: per-block submatrix eig finrank equals the full eig ÔŐô ker(P) finrank, so per-sector PF simplicity (#3834/#3836) directly transfers to constraints on the FULL eigenspace at that energy. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43ÔÇô44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/BlockDiagSubmatrixEmbed.lean`) |
| `eigenspace_finrank_eq_of_commuting_involution` | **(h.2) involution eigenspace decomposition ÔÇö finrank EQUALITY** (Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3841): strengthens `eigenspace_finrank_le_of_commuting_involution` (#3776) from `ÔëĄ` to `=`. For finite-dim `T, P` with `TÔłśP = PÔłśT` and `PÔłśP = id`, the `T`-eigenspace at any `╬╝` splits as a **direct sum** across the two `P`-eigenspaces `┬▒1`. The `ÔëĄ` was tight because the two intersected `┬▒1` eigenspaces of `P` are disjoint (a single eigenvector cannot have two distinct `P`-eigenvalues), so `Submodule.finrank_sup_add_finrank_inf_eq` collapses to a sum (inf = 0). Used by (h.3) to derive the block-sum equality. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43ÔÇô44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/InvolutionEigenspaceDecompEq.lean`) |
| `matrix_eigenspace_finrank_eq_sum_parity_blocks` (with `dressedAxisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_..._sum_parity_blocks`, `axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_..._sum_parity_blocks`) | **(h.3) block-sum finrank equality** (Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3842): combines (h.1) #3840 and (h.2) #3841 to give the block-sum equality for any parity-block-diagonal `M` commuting with `magParityDiagS`: `finrank Ôäé (eig M ╬╝) = finrank Ôäé (eig M.submatrix_0 ╬╝) + finrank Ôäé (eig M.submatrix_1 ╬╝)`. Specialised to the dressed `─Ą'_dressed` and the bare `─Ą'` (both block-diagonal and commuting with `P`). Central tool for unconditional `ÔëĄ 2` at any ╬╝ where both per-block bounds hold simultaneously. Combined with PF (per-sector simplicity at the per-sector spectrum minimum) and a Hermitian spectral identification of the GS energy as `min(╬Ż_0, ╬Ż_1)`, gives the unconditional bare `─Ą'` GS degeneracy `ÔëĄ 2`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43ÔÇô44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/BlockSumFinrankEq.lean`) |

Proof via spectrum-eigenvalues bridge (`HasEigenvalue.mem_spectrum` Ôćĺ `Matrix.spectrum_toLin'` Ôćĺ `spectrum.algebraMap_mem_iff` Ôćĺ `IsHermitian.spectrum_real_eq_range_eigenvalues`). Given per-block bounds `finrank ÔëĄ 1` at the per-block minimum eigenvalues, the full-eigenspace at `min(min_0, min_1)` has `finrank ÔëĄ 2`. Case analysis: `min_0 = min_1` both contribute `ÔëĄ 1`; `min_0 Ôëá min_1` the higher block contributes `0` by (i.4) #3849, the lower `ÔëĄ 1`. Bridge from per-block PF simplicity to the unconditional global ground-state degeneracy `ÔëĄ 2` (modulo identification of PF eigenvalue with `hermitianMinEigenvalue`). Discharges the block-diag (via `axisSwapped_apply_eq_zero_of_magSum_parity_ne`) and commute (via `axisSwapped_commute_magParityDiagS`) hypotheses inline. | `anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_at_min_block_mins` (with `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_at_min_block_mins`) | **(i.8) bare anisotropic `─Ą` eig ÔëĄ 2 at min(per-block mins) via axis-swap** (Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3855): wraps (i.7) #3854 with the axis-swap unitary equivalence (#3753 family `AxisSwapUnitaryS.anisotropic_axisSwapped_eigenspace_finrank_eq`) to lift the bound to the bare anisotropic Hamiltonian `─Ą`. General `N` version requires an `AxisSwapUnitaryS N` instance; spin-1/2 (`N = 1`) instance via `axisSwapUnitarySpinHalf`. **Analogue of (g.5) #3837 with per-block-min hypotheses threading the Hermitian spectral chain ÔÇö the remaining gap is identification of the per-sector PF eigenvalue with `hermitianMinEigenvalue submatrix_p` (which would discharge h0/h1 to yield the unconditional ground-state degeneracy ÔëĄ 2 for bare anisotropic `─Ą`).** Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43ÔÇô44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/BareAnisotropicMinLeTwo.lean`) |

| `axisSwapUnitarySSpinS_anisotropic_axisSwapped_eigenspace_finrank_eq` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_unconditional_general` | **General spin-S axis-swap discharge for Theorem 2.4 obligation (1)** (Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): the concrete `¤Ç/2` rotation about spin-axis 1, `axisSwapUnitarySSpinS N`, now instantiates the earlier `AxisSwapUnitaryS N` interface for every spin parameter `N`. The PR exposes no-argument wrappers for the axis-swap eigenspace-dimension equality, the conditional parity-block and submatrix-block `ÔëĄ 2` bounds, the min-block `ÔëĄ 2` bound, the Hermitian-minimum equality, and the structural general-N capstone `anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_unconditional_general`. This discharges the explicit axis-swap-unitary argument in the strict case-(i) degeneracy chain; the SU(2)-uniqueness target wrappers are handled in the later obligation-(2) row, and Tasaki case (ii) now has conditional, strict-gap, and no-full-finrank bridges recorded in the case-(ii) rows, with the strict-gap derivation still explicit. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43ÔÇô44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AxisSwapUnitarySSpinS.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_balanced_sector_pf` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_groundState_zero_magnetization_of_balanced_sector_pf` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_balanced_sector_pf_at_target` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_balanced_eq_full` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_groundState_zero_magnetization_of_balanced_eq_full` | **General spin-S target conditional bridge with balanced-sector PF discharged** (Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): packages the final linear-algebra step after the general spin-S `finrank ÔëĄ 2` degeneracy capstone. If the target full ground eigenspace has `finrank ÔëĄ 2`, the balanced sector attains the full ground energy, and the balanced-sector ground eigenspace has `finrank ÔëĄ 1`, then the full target ground eigenspace has `finrank ÔëĄ 1`; the companion wrapper then gives zero total `┼ť┬│` magnetization for every non-zero target ground state. The new general spin-S PF wrapper chooses a finite strict diagonal shift and applies the existing anisotropic sector PF/minimum theorem, so the PF-free target wrappers no longer take an explicit balanced-sector PF callback. The direct balanced-sector/full-ground equality input is superseded by the strict-gap wrappers in the next row; for case (ii), the conditional, strict-gap, and no-full-finrank bridges are recorded in the case-(ii) rows and the strict-gap derivation remains explicit. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43ÔÇô44 (files `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSTargetConditionalBridge.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSBalancedSectorPFTarget.lean`) |

These first case-(ii) wrappers keep the remaining target inputs explicit as balanced-sector/full-ground equality plus full `finrank <= 2`; they do not reuse the case-(i) axis-swap sign argument. This is a case-(ii) bridge step, not the final case-(ii) endpoint. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIStrictGapBridge.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_groundState_mem_balanced_sector_of_strict_gap` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_strict_gap_no_full_le_two` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_groundState_zero_magnetization_of_strict_gap_no_full_le_two` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_strict_gap_no_full_le_two` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_strict_gap_no_full_le_two` | **General spin-S target bridge from strict sector gap without the full `finrank <= 2` input** (Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): proves that if the balanced sector attains the full ground energy and every non-balanced non-empty magnetization sector has strictly larger minimum, then every full ground vector lies in the balanced magnetization sector. This uses the sector-projection eigenvector bridge and `hermitian_eigenspace_eq_bot_of_real_lt_min`, not the case-(i) parity-block `finrank <= 2` contradiction. Composing this containment with balanced-sector Perron--Frobenius simplicity gives full target uniqueness and zero total magnetization from strict sector gap alone; the case-(ii)-named wrappers keep only the theorem-facing `1 <= lambda`, `D <= 0` hypotheses plus the strict-gap input. This removes the explicit case-(ii) full `finrank <= 2` input; deriving the case-(ii) strict gap remains future work. If every non-balanced target crossing `E_M <= E_balanced` contradicts the case-(ii) hypotheses `1 <= lambda`, `D <= 0`, then order duality gives the strict gap `E_balanced < E_M` for every non-balanced non-empty sector. The target wrappers feed that strict gap to the no-full-finrank bridge above, so the public endpoint surface no longer exposes either balanced/full equality, full `finrank <= 2`, or strict gap directly; the remaining mathematical task is proving the crossing contradiction callback. For each parity block, if a PF eigenvalue `╬Ż` has `finrank <= 1` and equals that block's Hermitian minimum, then the block has `finrank <= 1` at the full anisotropic ground energy: if the full ground energy equals the block minimum, this is the conditional theorem; if it is lower, `hermitian_eigenspace_eq_bot_of_real_lt_min` makes the block eigenspace zero. The path wrapper supplies both parity blocks along `╬│(t)`, and the target wrappers feed the result to the block-path bridge above. The raw-support abbreviations name the strict, `lambda = 1`, and `D = 0` reachability/PF inputs for one parity block. The one-block selector chooses among the fixed raw-support consumers by the case-(ii) path inequalities and leaves only the corner `(lambda,D)=(1,0)` PF/min callback explicit; the even/odd raw-support wrapper applies this selector to both parity blocks, and the full-min wrapper feeds those callbacks into the block-path PF/min transfer. The raw-support target wrappers derive the support-zero hypothesis from `hJself` and `hJbip`, then expose target uniqueness and zero magnetization directly from the raw-support input surface. The reachability wrappers use finite parity blocks to supply strict diagonal shifts automatically, so the public reachability-level target wrappers require only the even and odd strict, ion-only, and bond-only reachability totality hypotheses plus the two explicit corner callbacks. On a fixed parity block it agrees with Tasaki's `(-1)^((magSumS - p) / 2)` convention up to a constant block sign. The gauge product is `1` when `magSumS` is preserved and `-1` when one configuration has `magSumS` two larger than the other, so it preserves transverse raise-lower signs while flipping the parity-bond and single-ion `┬▒2` signs. The diagonal gauge squares to the identity and is ready to combine with the Marshall diagonal in the next shifted PF matrix layer. The next case-(ii) PF steps are local sign transfer, reachability/irreducibility, and PF/min identification for this shifted matrix. Equal-`magSumS` pairs leave the Marshall-dressed real entry unchanged, while +/-2 pairs negate it; dressed negativity on transverse moves and dressed positivity on parity-bond or single-ion moves therefore become parity-gauged off-diagonal non-positivity. A diagonal shift bound plus this off-diagonal non-positivity gives entrywise non-negativity of `c ÔÇó 1 - R`, and strict dressed signs transfer to strict shifted entries. The next local case-(ii) task is to prove those dressed signs under the strict local hypotheses (`-1 < lambda` for transverse moves, `1 < lambda` for parity-bond moves, and `D < 0` for single-ion moves), then discharge irreducibility and PF/min identification. Transverse raise/lower moves use the positive `(1 + lambda.re) / 4` coefficient and the Marshall sign flip; parity-bond `┬▒2` moves use `1 < lambda.re`, the negative `(1 - lambda.re) / 4` coefficient, and the extra parity gauge; same-site single-ion `┬▒2` moves use `D.re < 0` against the negative `(S^2)^2` off-diagonal entry. The block relation is the reflexive-transitive closure of `ParityStepS` on `parityConfigS`; in the strict interior (`-1 < lambda.re`, `1 < lambda.re`, and `D.re < 0`) every elementary parity step gives a strictly positive shifted block entry. Entrywise non-negativity plus block reachability therefore gives a positive matrix-power entry, and reachability totality plus strict diagonal shift gives `Matrix.IsIrreducible`. | `caseIIParityGaugeSign_eq_ofReal_real` / `caseIICombinedGaugeDiagonalOnParity_map_eq` / `caseIICombinedGaugeDiagonalOnParity_map_mul_self` / `caseIIParityGaugedAxisSwappedReMatrixOnParityBlock_map_eq_combinedGauge_conj_bare` / `axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_submatrix_pf_min_of_caseII_shifted_block` / `axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_submatrix_pf_min_of_caseII_raw_support` / `axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_submatrix_pf_min_of_caseII_raw_support_lambda_one` / `axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_submatrix_pf_min_of_caseII_raw_support_D_zero` | **General spin-S case-(ii) parity-block PF/min bridge** (Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): converts the shifted case-(ii) parity-gauged real block into the bare axis-swapped complex parity-block PF/min callback. The combined parity/Marshall gauge diagonal is complexified and shown to square to the identity; the complexified parity-gauged block is similar to the bare axis-swapped block. Perron--Frobenius gives a one-dimensional positive eigenspace for the shifted non-negative irreducible real block, the shift changes the eigenvalue from `r` to `c - r`, Collatz--Wielandt identifies `c - r` with the Hermitian minimum, and similarity transfers the `finrank <= 1` bound to the bare block. Raw-support consumers cover the strict case-(ii) region, the `lambda = 1` ion-only boundary, and the `D = 0` bond-only boundary. The remaining pathwise task is to select and supply the appropriate strict or zero-coefficient reachability inputs along the case-(ii) deformation path. | `tasaki23_pf_groundState_commuting_eigenvector` / `tasaki23_toy_groundState_joint_casimir_eigenvector` | **The toy-Hamiltonian ground state is a simultaneous Casimir eigenvector**. The first generalises `tasaki23_pf_groundState_casimir_eigenvector` to any operator `B` commuting with `─Ą` and with `┼ť_tot^(3)`: the per-sector Marshall-positive ground state is a `B`-eigenvector (same one-dimensionality argument). Instantiated at the toy Hamiltonian `─Ą_toy = heisenbergHamiltonianS (bipartiteCoupling A)` for `B = (┼ť_tot)┬▓, (┼ť_A)┬▓, (┼ť_┬ČA)┬▓` ÔÇö all of which commute with `─Ą_toy` (it is their linear combination, `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_commute_{totalSpinSSquared,sublatticeSpinSquaredS,_complement}`), unlike with the general Heisenberg `─Ą` ÔÇö the toy ground state is a joint eigenvector of all three. Pinning the three eigenvalues to the predicted (max sublattice, min total) values is the remaining variational obligation. Such a full eigenspace vector is therefore exactly the zero-extension of its sector restriction, letting later component arguments reuse the sector-embedding APIs for vectors obtained from successor `magSubspaceS` support. Consequently `┼ť^-_tot` maps an embedded sector vector supported on `M` to a full vector supported on `M + 1`, while `┼ť^+_tot` maps an embedded sector vector supported on `M + 1` back to sector `M`. These are the contrapositive forms of the ladder-kernel Casimir consequences and are the bridge from the SU(2) obstruction to the sector interval argument: away from the representation endpoint value, the adjacent-sector ladder move cannot vanish. The lowered component is also expanded as a sum of the single-site lowering contributions `ÔłĹ x, ┼ť^-_x ╬Ę_M`; therefore the same package can be invoked from the local site-sum strict positivity hypothesis. This combines non-vanishing from strict Marshall positivity, ladder eigenvalue preservation, the sector-support shift, and the #869 target-sector uniqueness theorem; the remaining critical-path input is to prove the sitewise Marshall-positivity hypothesis for the lowered vector. Positive off-`A` occupation produces an actually lowerable off-`A` witness, and either that witness or the aggregate occupation hypothesis gives strict positivity of the off-`A` filtered sum by `Finset.sum_pos'`. These are decomposition bounds for the remaining strict lowered site-sum positivity proof, not yet the dominance argument for the full sum.
