The dominance wrappers convert strict dominance in either direction between the explicit lowerable coefficient sums and the matching predecessor raising-source sums, so the remaining local comparison can be stated in the coefficient form or the raising-source form as needed by the final and common-energy boundaries. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23LocalCoefficientRaisingSource.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_raising_predecessor_source_summand_pos` / `tasaki23_raising_predecessor_source_attach_sum_nonneg` | **Predecessor raising-source positivity for Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3**: each lowerable predecessor raising-source summand is strictly positive for a strictly positive real source vector, because the real `S^+` coefficient from the lowering predecessor back to the successor is positive. Consequently the attached predecessor raising-source sums used by the final dominance callback are non-negative. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23LocalCoefficientRaisingSourceSum.lean`) |
| `tasaki23RaisingPredecessorSourceCoefficient` / `tasaki23_raising_predecessor_source_attach_sum_eq_boundary_sum` / `tasaki23_raising_predecessor_source_univ_attach_sum_eq_onA_add_offA` | **Predecessor raising-source sum partition for Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3**: the lowerable predecessor raising-source summand is packaged as a boundary-inclusive coefficient that contributes zero at non-lowerable sites. The attached sum over lowerable sites is rewritten as the corresponding ambient finite-set sum, and the total lowerable predecessor raising-source sum is split into the on-`A` and off-`A` attached sums. This supplies the finite-set partition needed before applying the real predecessor source-weight equation to the two sublattice raising-source sums. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23LocalCoefficientRaisingSourceSum.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_raising_predecessor_source_sum_lt_of_offA_sub_onA_pos` / `tasaki23_raising_predecessor_source_sum_lt_callback_of_offA_sub_onA_pos` | **Predecessor raising-source difference dominance for Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3**: positivity of the off-`A` predecessor raising-source attached sum minus the on-`A` attached sum is converted to the strict dominance inequality required by the final raising-source callback. The callback wrapper lifts this pointwise conversion to the quantified local estimate used by the remaining theorem boundary. This states the local comparison in the difference form naturally produced by the real predecessor source-weight identity. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23LocalCoefficientRaisingSourceSum.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_lowerable_positive_source_attach_sum_lt_of_offA_sub_onA_pos` / `tasaki23_lowerable_positive_source_attach_sum_lt_callback_of_offA_sub_onA_pos` / `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_of_left_endpoint_threaded_predictedGS_of_unpacked_reembedded_real_source_weight_predecessor_difference_pos_via_explicit_lowerable_of_outside_sector_ground_energy_lower_bound` | **Explicit lowerable dominance from predecessor differences for Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3**: the off-`A` minus on-`A` predecessor raising-source positive-difference form now also discharges strict dominance of the attached explicit lowerable positive-source coefficient sums, by composing the difference dominance bridge with the lowerable coefficient mirror. The outside-sector final wrapper can therefore run through the explicit-lowerable boundary while accepting the named predecessor-difference callback instead of a separate explicit lowerable local-dominance hypothesis. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (files `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23LocalCoefficientRaisingSourceSum.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23OutsideGroundPredecessorDifference.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_raising_predecessor_source_difference_eq_lowered_marshall_component` / `tasaki23_lowered_marshall_pos_of_raising_predecessor_source_difference_pos` / `tasaki23_lowered_site_sum_pos_of_raising_predecessor_source_difference_pos` / `tasaki23_lowered_marshall_pos_of_unpacked_reembedded_real_source_weight_predecessor_difference_pos` / `tasaki23_lowered_site_sum_pos_of_unpacked_reembedded_real_source_weight_predecessor_difference_pos` / `tasaki23_lowered_site_sum_pos_callback_of_unpacked_reembedded_real_source_weight_predecessor_difference_pos` / `tasaki23_energy_interval_chain_with_predictedGS_of_left_endpoint_predictedGS_of_unpacked_reembedded_real_source_weight_predecessor_difference_pos` / `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_of_left_endpoint_threaded_predictedGS_of_unpacked_reembedded_real_source_weight_predecessor_difference_pos_via_lowered_site_sum_of_sector_minimality` | **Predecessor raising-source difference as lowered Marshall positivity for Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3**: for a Marshall-signed positive source representative, the boundary-sum off-`A` minus on-`A` predecessor raising-source difference is exactly `(marshallSignS A ¤ä).re * Re((S^-_tot ╬Ę) ¤ä)`. The positivity bridges rewrite the lowerable attached-sum difference from the real source-weight callback to this boundary form and therefore prove the lowered-vector Marshall-positive component; the source-form site-sum bridge also turns the same difference directly into the strict single-site lowered sum callback. The named callback adapter exposes this site-sum form as the API consumed by the new interval chain and final theorem wrapper: predicted-GS membership supplies the re-embedded source-weight identity and lowered sublattice-Casimir/support data at each successor step, the predecessor-difference callback is converted to strict lowered site-sum positivity, and the direct site-sum successor chain finishes the common-energy proof without first converting the boundary to the raising-source dominance final callback. The fully threaded callback shape carries predicted-GS, real source-weight, sublattice-Casimir, and successor-support data for both the lowered-vector Marshall component and the site-sum callback. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (files `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23LocalDifference.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23LocalDifferenceUnpacked.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23LocalDifferenceUnpackedSiteSum.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23LocalDifferenceMarshall.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23Interval.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23OutsideGroundPredecessorDifference.lean`) |
| `tasaki23GroundStateSectors_le_card_mul` / `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_of_physical_range_nonempty_threaded_predictedGS_of_unpacked_reembedded_real_source_weight_predecessor_difference_pos_of_outside_sector_ground_energy_lower_bound` | **Physical-range non-empty boundary for Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3**: every admissible sector in `tasaki23GroundStateSectors A N` lies in the full magnetization range `M ÔëĄ Fintype.card V * N`, because the right endpoint is bounded by `(|A| + |┬ČA|) * N = |V| * N`. The final boundary uses this range lemma to replace the interval-specific `magConfigS` non-emptiness callback by a canonical physical-range non-emptiness callback, while keeping the threaded predicted-GS input, local predecessor-difference comparison, and outside-sector ground-representative lower bounds. Internally it now discharges the non-emptiness input and invokes the explicit source common-energy final boundary directly. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42; Seneta, *Non-negative Matrices and Markov Chains*, 3rd ed., Springer 2006, ┬ž1.2, pp. 27ÔÇô28 (files `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23Sectors.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23Final.lean`) |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_mulVec_totalSpinSOpMinus_pow_of_eigenvec` / `heisenbergHamiltonianS_mulVec_totalSpinSOpPlus_pow_of_eigenvec` / `totalSpinSOpMinus_pow_mulVec_mem_magSubspaceS_of_mem` / `totalSpinSOpPlus_pow_mulVec_mem_magSubspaceS_of_mem` / `tasaki23OutsideGroundLeftIteratedLadderFullReachCallback` / `tasaki23OutsideGroundRightIteratedLadderFullReachCallback` / `tasaki23OutsideGroundAdmissibleFullReachCallback_of_iterated_ladder_callbacks` / `tasaki23OutsideGroundEnergyLowerFamilyCallback_of_iterated_ladder_full_reach` / `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_of_threaded_predictedGS_of_unpacked_reembedded_real_source_weight_predecessor_difference_pos_of_iterated_ladder_full_reach_discharge_nonempty` | **Outside-ground iterated total-spin ladder reach for Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3**: the outside reach input is reduced from an arbitrary full-space reached eigenvector to non-zeroness of an iterated total-spin ladder output. The resulting full-space admissible-reach callback feeds the lower-family bridge, and the discharged final boundary passes that lower family directly to the source common-energy final boundary. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42; Seneta, *Non-negative Matrices and Markov Chains*, 3rd ed., Springer 2006, ┬ž1.2, pp. 27ÔÇô28 (files `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23Local.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23OutsideGround.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23Final.lean`) |

The full outside admissible-reach callback follows from separate left and right directional ladder-reach callbacks, exposing the lowering and raising sides of the remaining outside-sector reach task while preserving the public admissible-reach boundary used by the final wrappers. The support lemmas first record that `(┼ť_A)^2` commutes with `┼ť_A^-` and with the complement lowering ladder; the Theorem 2.3 bridges then show `┼ť_A^- ╬Ž` and `┼ť_┬ČA^- ╬Ž` remain in the corresponding maximum sublattice-Casimir eigenspaces. This supplies the component-level Casimir input for the remaining comparison between the two sublattice lowering pieces. The `A`-lowered component `┼ť_A^- ╬Ž` also remains in the maximum `┬ČA`-sublattice Casimir eigenspace, and the `┬ČA`-lowered component `┼ť_┬ČA^- ╬Ž` also remains in the maximum `A`-sublattice Casimir eigenspace. Thus both individual lowering components lie in the joint maximum sublattice-Casimir eigenspace needed for the remaining component comparison. This gives the next comparison step a single structural hypothesis rather than four separate Casimir equalities. This records both the Casimir structure and the sector-support fact needed before applying the raised-lowered cross-ladder identity to the remaining component comparison. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23PredictedLadder.lean`) |

Instead of receiving only joint sublattice-Casimir membership, the callback receives both `┼ť_A^- ╬Ž` and `┼ť_┬ČA^- ╬Ž` as members of `tasaki23LoweredJointMagSubspace A N M`, hence with joint-Casimir structure and successor-sector support at once. The coefficient wrapper rewrites on/off predecessor coefficient dominance into the same strict Marshall-signed component comparison. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (files `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23PredictedLadder.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23IntervalJoint.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23OutsideGroundCrossLadderLoweredJoint.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_energy_interval_chain_with_predictedGS_of_left_endpoint_predictedGS_of_lowered_joint_cross_ladder_component_lt_of_predictedGS` / `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_of_left_endpoint_threaded_predictedGS_of_lowered_joint_cross_ladder_component_lt_of_sector_minimality` / `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_of_left_endpoint_threaded_predictedGS_of_lowered_joint_cross_ladder_coefficient_lt_of_sector_minimality` | **Lowered joint cross-ladder callback threading for Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3**: the predicted-GS interval chain and final wrappers now also pass the raised-lowered cross-ladder identity into the local comparison callback. The callback receives the source predicted-GS membership, the identity applying `┼ť_A^+` and `┼ť_┬ČA^+` to the two lowered components, and both lowered-component memberships in `tasaki23LoweredJointMagSubspace A N M`. This isolates the remaining comparison under exactly the cross-ladder equation and subspace structure already proved for predicted toy ground states. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (files `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23PredictedLadder.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23IntervalJoint.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23OutsideGroundCrossLadderLoweredJointCross.lean`) |

At each successor step the callback receives the propagated `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted A N` membership plus the two facts `┼ť_A^- ╬Ž Ôłł tasaki23JointSublatticeCasimirEigenspace A N` and `┼ť_┬ČA^- ╬Ž Ôłł tasaki23JointSublatticeCasimirEigenspace A N`, then returns the strict pointwise Marshall-signed component inequality. This directly connects the joint-eigenspace bridges to the final local comparison obligation. | `totalSpinSSquared_mulVec_totalSpinSOpMinus_pow_of_eigenvec` / `totalSpinSSquared_mulVec_totalSpinSOpPlus_pow_of_eigenvec` / `totalSpinSOpMinus_pow_mulVec_ne_zero_of_casimir_ne_kernel_values` / `totalSpinSOpPlus_pow_mulVec_ne_zero_of_casimir_ne_kernel_values` | **Iterated total-spin ladder Casimir preservation and non-vanishing for Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3**: the preservation lemmas transport a total-Casimir eigenvalue through an iterated `┼ť_tot^-` or `┼ť_tot^+` ladder. The non-vanishing lemmas then prove that the iterated ladder output remains nonzero whenever every intermediate magnetization avoids the corresponding one-step ladder-kernel Casimir value. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23Local.lean`) |

The complex-form `marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergSector_complexGroundState_full`
is the COMPLEX-Hilbert-space form of Tasaki ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.2 in the
magnetization sector: the ground state of the un-dressed quantum
Heisenberg Hamiltonian restricted to the sector is unique (up to a
positive real scalar in its real part) and has the Marshall sign
structure `╬Ž ¤â := ((sign A ¤â.1).re * v ¤â : Ôäé)` with `v > 0`.

`tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` (PR #3337) is the **final-statement form**
of the `|A| Ôëá |┬ČA|` case. The hypothesis bundle matches
`marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergHamiltonianS_groundState_full`
(#869) exactly. The conclusion bundles, per admissible sector
`M Ôłł tasaki23GroundStateSectors A N`, the Marshall-positive
sector eigenvector existence and within-sector uniqueness from
#869, together with a global energy-minimality clause. The
predicted total spin and predicted degeneracy enter as the
cardinality identity `|tasaki23GroundStateSectors A N| =
tasaki23PredictedDegeneracy A N = 2 S_tot + 1` with
`S_tot = ||A| Ôłĺ |┬ČA||┬ĚN/2 = tasaki23PredictedTotalSpin A N`. The
proof iterates #869 across `tasaki23GroundStateSectors A N`.

References: H. Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, Springer 2020, ┬ž2.5 Theorem 2.2 (pp. 39ÔÇô43), Theorem 2.3
(p. 42); E. Seneta, *Non-negative Matrices and Markov Chains*
(3rd ed.), Springer 2006, ┬ž1.2 (pp. 27ÔÇô28) for the underlying
PerronÔÇôFrobenius theorem.

### Spin-`S` saturated ferromagnetic state (Tasaki ┬ž2.4 generalised)

Generic-spin (`N = 2S`) version of Tasaki ┬ž2.4 P1i for the
**saturated ferromagnet**: the all-aligned (constant-spin) basis
state `|¤â_ÔŐĄÔčę = ÔŐŚ_x |cÔčę` with `¤â x = c` for all `x : V`. The two
extremal weights `c = 0` (highest weight, "all up") and
`c = Fin.last N` (lowest weight, "all down") are the highest- and
lowest-weight vectors of the `J_tot = |V|┬ĚS = |V|┬ĚN/2` irreducible
SU(2) representation in the multi-site Hilbert space. Tracked in
Issue #412; assembled in PRs #875ÔÇô#879. All theorems live in
`Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedState.lean`.

| Lean name | Statement |
|---|---|
| `allAlignedConfigS V N c` | constant configuration `¤â x = c` (PR #875) |
| `allAlignedStateS V N c` | basis state at constant `c`, equal to `basisVecS (allAlignedConfigS V N c)` (PR #875) |
| `magSumS_allAlignedConfigS` | `magSumS = |V|┬Ěc.val` (PR #875) |
| `magEigenvalueS_allAlignedConfigS` | `magEigenvalueS = |V|┬ĚN/2 Ôłĺ |V|┬Ěc` (PR #875) |
| `totalSpinSOp3_mulVec_allAlignedStateS` | `┼ť^z_tot ┬Ě |cÔčę = (|V|┬ĚN/2 Ôłĺ |V|┬Ěc) ┬Ě |cÔčę` for any `c` (PR #875) |
| `magSumS_allAlignedConfigS_zero` | `c = 0` Ôč╣ `magSumS = 0` (PR #875) |
| `magSumS_pos_of_ne_allAlignedConfigS_zero` | the all-up configuration is the **unique** `magSumS = 0` configuration (PR #875) |
| `magSumS_allAlignedConfigS_last` | `c = Fin.last N` Ôč╣ `magSumS = |V|┬ĚN` (PR #876) |
| `magSumS_lt_card_mul_of_ne_allAlignedConfigS_last` | the all-down configuration is the unique configuration with `magSumS = |V|┬ĚN` (PR #876) |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | the **all-up state is a Heisenberg eigenvector for ANY coupling** ÔÇö magnetization conservation `[H, ┼ť^z_tot] = 0` + uniqueness of the M=0 configuration (PR #875) |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero_eigenvalue` | explicit Heisenberg eigenvalue formula on all-up: `╬ú_x J(x,x)┬ĚN(N+2)/4 + ╬ú_{xÔëáy} J(x,y)┬ĚN┬▓/4` (PR #875) |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_last` / `_eigenvalue` | symmetric c = N (all-down) Heisenberg eigenvector + same eigenvalue formula (PR #876) |
| `onSiteS_spinSOpPlus_apply_allAlignedConfigS_zero` | `(onSiteS x ┼ť^+) ¤â' ¤â_ÔŐĄ = 0` (PR #877) |
| `onSiteS_spinSOpPlus_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | `(onSiteS x ┼ť^+).mulVec |¤â_ÔŐĄÔčę = 0` (PR #877) |
| `totalSpinSOpPlus_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | **`┼ť^+_tot ┬Ě |¤â_ÔŐĄÔčę = 0`** (highest-weight annihilation, PR #877) |
| `onSiteS_spinSOpMinus_apply_allAlignedConfigS_last` / `_mulVec_` / `totalSpinSOpMinus_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_last` | symmetric lowest-weight annihilation `┼ť^-_tot ┬Ě |¤â_ÔŐąÔčę = 0` (PR #877) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | the all-up state is a `(┼ť_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector (PR #878) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_apply_diag_allAlignedConfigS_zero` | explicit Casimir diagonal value `|V|┬ĚN(N+2)/4 + (|V|┬▓Ôłĺ|V|)┬ĚN┬▓/4` (PR #878) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero_eigenvalue` | **`(┼ť_tot)┬▓ ┬Ě |¤â_ÔŐĄÔčę = (|V|┬ĚN/2)┬Ě(|V|┬ĚN/2 + 1) ┬Ě |¤â_ÔŐĄÔčę`** ÔÇö operator-level form of "all-up is the highest-weight vector of the J_tot = |V|┬ĚS irreducible SU(2) representation" (PR #878) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_last` / `_apply_diag_` / `_eigenvalue` | symmetric lowest-weight Casimir eigenvalue (same value) (PR #879) |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_commute_totalSpinSOp1` / `_Op2` / `_OpPlus` / `_OpMinus` | `Commute`-form conversions: H commutes with each axis-total operator (PR #881) |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_commute_totalSpinSOpMinus_pow` / `_Plus_pow` | iterated power Commute by induction (PR #881) |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_mulVec_totalSpinSOpMinus_pow_allAlignedStateS_zero` | for any k, `(┼ť^-_tot)^k ┬Ě |¤â_ÔŐĄÔčę` is a Heisenberg eigenvector at the same eigenvalue as `|¤â_ÔŐĄÔčę` (PR #881) |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_mulVec_totalSpinSOpPlus_pow_allAlignedStateS_last` | symmetric for `┼ť^+_tot` on all-down (PR #881) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_commute_totalSpinSOp1` / `_Op2` / `_OpPlus` / `_OpMinus` / `_OpMinus_pow` / `_OpPlus_pow` | Casimir Commute-form analogues (PR #882) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_mulVec_totalSpinSOpMinus_pow_allAlignedStateS_zero` | for any k, `(┼ť^-_tot)^k ┬Ě |¤â_ÔŐĄÔčę` preserves the Casimir eigenvalue `(|V|┬ĚN/2)┬Ě(|V|┬ĚN/2+1)` (PR #882) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_mulVec_totalSpinSOpPlus_pow_allAlignedStateS_last` | symmetric (PR #882) |

The remaining input is the Marshall-positive coefficient expansion that supplies the signed positivity hypothesis. This packages the finite-sum positivity step behind Tasaki's equation (S.23); the remaining input is the concrete Marshall-gauge matrix-entry non-negativity and strict witness for the ground-state vector. For `A x Ôëá A y`, the bipartite gauge sign and Marshall sign product cancel on every non-zero ladder matrix entry, leaving the bare non-negative entry; for `N Ôëą 1` an explicit two-configuration witness gives strict positivity. This feeds PR #4069's finite-sum positivity wrapper for strictly positive Marshall coefficients. Tasaki, Springer 2020, Problem 2.5.d, p. 40 and solution p. 498, equations (S.22)-(S.23) (PR #4071, file `Quantum/SpinS/Problem25dLadderEntrySign.lean`) |
| `twoSpinMinusPlusCorrelationS` / `twoSpinZZCorrelationS` / `twoSpinCorrelationS_eq_ladder_components` / `signed_twoSpinCorrelationS_re_pos_of_ladder_component_equalities` / `twoSpinCorrelationS_bipartite_signed_re_pos_of_marshall_cross_components` | **Problem 2.5.d ladder-to-dot reduction**: lifts `┼ť_x ┬Ě ┼ť_y = 1/2(┼ť_x^+┼ť_y^- + ┼ť_x^-┼ť_y^+) + ┼ť_x^3┼ť_y^3` to the two-spin correlation level and packages the conditional SU(2) sign transfer. If the signed `┼ť_x^-┼ť_y^+` real part matches the signed `┼ť_x^+┼ť_y^-` real part and the signed longitudinal `┼ť_x^3┼ť_y^3` real part is half of it, then signed ladder positivity implies signed dot-product positivity; combined with PR #4071 this gives the Marshall-coefficient cross-sublattice wrapper under those component equalities. Tasaki, Springer 2020, Problem 2.5.d, p. 40 and solution p. 498, equations (S.22)-(S.23) (PR #4072, file `Quantum/SpinS/Problem25dLadderDotReduction.lean`) |
| `dotProduct_star_conjTranspose_mulVec_eq_star` / `twoSpinPlusMinus_ladder_conjTranspose` / `twoSpinMinusPlusCorrelationS_eq_star_twoSpinPlusMinusCorrelationS` / `bipartite_signed_twoSpinMinusPlusCorrelationS_re_eq_plusMinus` / `twoSpinCorrelationS_bipartite_signed_re_pos_of_marshall_cross_ladderAdjoint` | **Problem 2.5.d ladder adjoint equality**: discharges the first component-equality input left by PR #4072. A generic adjoint-expectation bridge shows that the expectation of `MÔÇá` is the complex conjugate of the expectation of `M`; since `(┼ť_x^+┼ť_y^-)ÔÇá = ┼ť_x^-┼ť_y^+` for `x Ôëá y`, the two ladder expectations are complex conjugates, and multiplication by the real cross-sublattice bipartite gauge sign preserves equality of real parts. The resulting wrapper combines PR #4071 and PR #4072 while leaving only the longitudinal `┼ť_x^3┼ť_y^3` equality as the remaining SU(2) input. Tasaki, Springer 2020, Problem 2.5.d, p. 40 and solution p. 498, equations (S.22)-(S.23) (PR #4073, file `Quantum/SpinS/Problem25dLadderAdjointEquality.lean`) |
| `twoSpinProductCorrelationS` / `twoSpinProductCorrelationS_spinSOp3_eq_twoSpinZZCorrelationS` / `twoSpinProductCorrelationS_eq_of_conj_phaseInvariant` / `conj_product_of_conj_factors` / `twoSpinProductCorrelationS_axis3_eq_axis2_of_axisSwap_phase` / `twoSpinProductCorrelationS_axis1_eq_axis2_of_zAxisRot_phase` / `twoSpinProductCorrelationS_axis1_add_axis2_eq_ladder` / `bipartite_signed_twoSpinZZCorrelationS_re_eq_half_plusMinus_of_axis_phases` / `twoSpinCorrelationS_bipartite_signed_re_pos_of_marshall_cross_axis_phases` | **Problem 2.5.d longitudinal component equality**: discharges the remaining longitudinal SU(2) component equality left by PR #4073 under axis-swap and z-axis rotation phase invariance. The two-site phase-invariant bridge gives `C33 = C22` and `C11 = C22`; the `spinSOp3` bridge identifies `C33` with the existing `twoSpinZZCorrelationS`; the transverse ladder identity gives `C11 + C22 = 1/2(C^{+-} + C^{-+})`; PR #4073 gives equality of the signed ladder real parts, hence `Re(g C33) = 1/2 Re(g C^{+-})`. The resulting wrapper removes the final component-equality hypothesis from the signed dot-product positivity transfer. Tasaki, Springer 2020, Problem 2.5.d, p. 40 and solution pp. 498-499, equations (S.22)-(S.23) (PR #4074, file `Quantum/SpinS/Problem25dLongitudinalComponentEquality.lean`) |
| `twoSpinCorrelationS_bipartite_signed_re_pos_of_marshall_cross_eigenphase` / `twoSpinCorrelationS_signed_re_pos_of_tasaki23_balanced_MLM_groundState` | **Problem 2.5.d ground-state phase wrapper**: removes the explicit axis-swap and z-axis rotation phase hypotheses from PR #4074. A normalized non-zero Marshall-positive Heisenberg eigenvector in a one-dimensional eigenspace supplies both unit-modulus phases through the Problem 2.5.c ground-state phase bridges, and the balanced MLM/SU(2) endpoint supplies that one-dimensionality at the Hermitian minimum under the Theorem 2.3 hypotheses. This wrapper is for a full-space coefficient function; the sector-supported Perron-Frobenius ground-state shape is handled separately. Tasaki, Springer 2020, Problem 2.5.d, p. 40 and solution pp. 498-499, equations (S.22)-(S.23), with Theorems 2.3-2.4 context, pp. 42-44 (PR #4075, file `Quantum/SpinS/Problem25dGroundStatePhaseWrapper.lean`) |
| `twoSpinPlusMinusCorrelationS_bipartite_signed_re_pos_of_marshall_sector_coefficients` / `twoSpinPlusMinusCorrelationS_bipartite_signed_re_pos_of_marshall_sector_cross` / `twoSpinCorrelationS_bipartite_signed_re_pos_of_marshall_sector_cross_components` / `twoSpinCorrelationS_bipartite_signed_re_pos_of_marshall_sector_cross_axis_phases` / `twoSpinCorrelationS_bipartite_signed_re_pos_of_marshall_sector_cross_eigenphase` | **Problem 2.5.d sector-supported wrapper**: adapts the PR #4069--#4075 positivity chain to the actual sector-supported Marshall-positive ground-state shape produced by Perron-Frobenius. A sector vector with strictly positive coefficients is zero-extended by `magSectorEmbedding`; off-sector terms in the ladder expectation vanish, on-sector terms are positive-coefficient multiples of the signed matrix entries, and the same axis-phase/eigenspace bridge supplies the signed dot-product positivity conclusion. Tasaki, Springer 2020, Problem 2.5.d, p. 40 and solution pp. 498-499, equations (S.22)-(S.23), with Theorems 2.3-2.4 context, pp. 42-44 (PR #4076, file `Quantum/SpinS/Problem25dSectorSupportedWrapper.lean`) |
| `twoSpinPlusMinus_ladder_signed_entry_re_pos_of_off_two_site_raise_lower` / `exists_twoSpinPlusMinus_ladder_signed_entry_re_pos_of_bipartite_ne_balanced_sector` / `twoSpinCorrelationS_bipartite_signed_re_pos_of_marshall_balanced_sector_cross_eigenphase` | **Problem 2.5.d balanced-sector ladder witness**: supplies the strict sector-local `S_x^+ S_y^-` matrix-entry witness required by PR #4076 at the balanced sector `M0 = |A| * N`. The witness starts from the complement N├ęel configuration, updates the two cross-sublattice sites touched by the ladder operator, and uses `magSumS_configUpdateTwo_eq` plus `magSumS_neelConfigOfS_complement` to keep both configurations in `M0`. The resulting wrapper removes the explicit strict-entry hypothesis from the sector-supported eigenspace-phase theorem at the balanced sector. Tasaki, Springer 2020, Problem 2.5.d, p. 40 and solution pp. 498-499, equations (S.22)-(S.23), with Theorems 2.3-2.4 context, pp. 42-44 (PR #4077, file `Quantum/SpinS/Problem25dBalancedSectorWitness.lean`) |
| `phase_unit_of_unitary_mulVec_eq_smul_of_ne_zero` / `exists_phase_unit_of_finrank_eigenspace_le_one_of_unitary_commute_of_ne_zero` / `twoSpinCorrelationS_bipartite_signed_re_pos_of_tasaki23_balanced_pf_cross` | **Problem 2.5.d balanced Perron-Frobenius endpoint**: extracts the balanced sector PF vector from the Theorem 2.3 common-energy data, rewrites the existing real-sign embedding into the sector-supported Marshall form using `marshallSignS_eq_ofReal_re`, and derives the axis-swap / z-rotation unit-modulus phases without normalizing the vector. The endpoint returns the common Hermitian minimum, the strictly positive balanced coefficients, the non-zero embedded eigenvector, the full eigenspace `finrank ÔëĄ 1` input from the SU(2) endpoint, and the signed cross-sublattice two-spin correlation positivity for that concrete PF vector. Tasaki, Springer 2020, Problem 2.5.d, p. 40 and solution pp. 498-499, equations (S.22)-(S.23), with Theorems 2.3-2.4 context, pp. 42-44 (PR #4078, file `Quantum/SpinS/Problem25dBalancedPFEndpoint.lean`) |
| `twoSpinCorrelationS_sign_cases_of_tasaki23_balanced_pf_cross` | **Problem 2.5.d balanced Perron-Frobenius sign cases**: applies the final bipartite-gauge sign conversion to the concrete balanced PF endpoint from PR #4078. The wrapper preserves the Hermitian minimum, strictly positive balanced coefficients, non-zero embedded eigenvector, and full eigenspace `finrank ÔëĄ 1` package, while replacing the signed-positivity conclusion by the four Boolean same-sublattice positive / cross-sublattice negative sign cases for the original two-spin correlation. Tasaki, Springer 2020, Problem 2.5.d, p. 40 and solution pp. 498-499, equations (S.22)-(S.23), with Theorems 2.3-2.4 context, pp. 42-44 (PR #4079, file `Quantum/SpinS/Problem25dBalancedPFSignCases.lean`) |
| `twoSpinCorrelationS_re_neg_of_tasaki23_balanced_pf_cross` | **Problem 2.5.d balanced Perron-Frobenius cross sign**: extracts the concrete non-vacuous cross-sublattice conclusion from PR #4079. Under `A x Ôëá A y`, the Boolean sign cases collapse to the original two-spin correlation inequality `(twoSpinCorrelationS x y ╬Ž).re < 0` for the same balanced PF vector, while preserving the Hermitian minimum, positive coefficients, non-zero eigenvector, and `finrank ÔëĄ 1` package. Tasaki, Springer 2020, Problem 2.5.d, p. 40 and solution pp. 498-499, equations (S.22)-(S.23), with Theorems 2.3-2.4 context, pp. 42-44 (PR #4080, file `Quantum/SpinS/Problem25dBalancedPFCrossSign.lean`) |

### Single-mode fermion (P2 skeleton)

Phase 2 entry point: the canonical anticommutation algebra of a single
fermion mode acting on `Ôäé┬▓` with computational basis
`|0Ôčę` (vacuum) and `|1Ôčę` (occupied).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `fermionAnnihilation` | `c = !![0, 1; 0, 0] = |0ÔčęÔčĘ1|` | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
| `fermionCreation` | `cÔÇá = !![0, 0; 1, 0] = |1ÔčęÔčĘ0|` | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
| `fermionNumber` | `n = !![0, 0; 0, 1] = |1ÔčęÔčĘ1|` | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
| `fermionNumber_eq_creation_mul_annihilation` | `n = cÔÇá ┬Ě c` | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
| `fermionAnnihilation_sq` | `c┬▓ = 0` | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
| `fermionCreation_sq` | `(cÔÇá)┬▓ = 0` | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |
| `fermionAnticomm_self` | `c ┬Ě cÔÇá + cÔÇá ┬Ě c = 1` (single-mode CAR) | `Fermion/Mode.lean` |

### Multi-mode fermion via JordanÔÇôWigner (P2 backbone)

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `jwString N i` | `ÔłĆ_{j.val < i.val} ¤â^z_j` (noncomm-product, pairwise commutativity from `onSite_mul_onSite_of_ne`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `jwString_zero` | `jwString N 0 = 1` (empty product) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation N i` | `c_i = jwString_i ┬Ě ¤â^+_i` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation N i` | `c_iÔÇá = jwString_i ┬Ě ¤â^-_i` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_zero` | `c_0 = ¤â^+_0` (no JW string at the leftmost site) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_zero` | `c_0ÔÇá = ¤â^-_0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `jwString_commute_onSite` | `Commute (jwString N i) (onSite i A)` (string commutes past same-site operators) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_sq` | `c_i┬▓ = 0` (Pauli exclusion) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_sq` | `(c_iÔÇá)┬▓ = 0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `jwString_isHermitian` | `(jwString N i)ß┤┤ = jwString N i` (product of pairwise-commuting Hermitian ¤â^z is Hermitian) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_conjTranspose` | `(c_i)ß┤┤ = c_iÔÇá` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_conjTranspose` | `(c_iÔÇá)ß┤┤ = c_i` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `jwString_sq` | `(jwString N i)┬▓ = 1` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiNumber N i` | `n_i := c_iÔÇá ┬Ě c_i` (site-occupation number operator) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiNumber_eq_onSite` | `n_i = onSite i (¤â^- ┬Ě ¤â^+)` (JW strings cancel via `J┬▓ = 1`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiNumber_isHermitian` | `n_i` is Hermitian | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiNumber_sq` | `n_i┬▓ = n_i` (idempotent, eigenvalues 0, 1) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnticomm_self` | `c_i ┬Ě c_iÔÇá + c_iÔÇá ┬Ě c_i = 1` (same-site CAR) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiNumber_commute` | `Commute (n_i) (n_j)` for any sites (simultaneously diagonal) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalNumber N` | `N╠é := ╬ú_i n_i` (total particle-number operator) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalNumber_isHermitian` | `N╠é` is Hermitian | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_anticomm_two_site_cross` | simplest nontrivial cross-site CAR on `Fin 2`: `c_0 ┬Ě c_1 + c_1 ┬Ě c_0 = 0` (JW string at site 1 is `¤â^z_0`, combined with `¤â^+ ¤â^z = -¤â^+` and `¤â^z ¤â^+ = ¤â^+`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_anticomm_two_site_cross` | adjoint form: `c_0ÔÇá ┬Ě c_1ÔÇá + c_1ÔÇá ┬Ě c_0ÔÇá = 0` on `Fin 2`, obtained by taking `conjTranspose` of the annihilation version | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_creation_anticomm_two_site_cross` | mixed cross-site: `c_0 ┬Ě c_1ÔÇá + c_1ÔÇá ┬Ě c_0 = 0` on `Fin 2` (same proof template as the annihilation-only version with `¤â^+_1` replaced by `¤â^-_1` at site 1) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_annihilation_anticomm_two_site_cross` | fourth off-diagonal CAR: `c_0ÔÇá ┬Ě c_1 + c_1 ┬Ě c_0ÔÇá = 0` on `Fin 2` (adjoint of the previous; completes the 2-site off-diagonal CAR relations) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_anticomm_zero_one` | generalisation to any chain length: `c_0 ┬Ě c_1 + c_1 ┬Ě c_0 = 0` on `Fin (N+1)` for any `N Ôëą 1` (the JW string at site 1 is uniformly `¤â^z_0` independent of `N`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_anticomm_zero_one` | dual: `c_0ÔÇá ┬Ě c_1ÔÇá + c_1ÔÇá ┬Ě c_0ÔÇá = 0` on `Fin (N+1)`, `N Ôëą 1` (adjoint of the above) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_creation_anticomm_zero_one` | mixed: `c_0 ┬Ě c_1ÔÇá + c_1ÔÇá ┬Ě c_0 = 0` on `Fin (N+1)`, `N Ôëą 1` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_annihilation_anticomm_zero_one` | mixed dual: `c_0ÔÇá ┬Ě c_1 + c_1 ┬Ě c_0ÔÇá = 0` on `Fin (N+1)`, `N Ôëą 1` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `jwString_succ_eq` | recursive factorisation of the JW string: `jwString N ÔčĘi+1, _Ôčę = jwString N i * onSite i pauliZ` (key general lemma for proving jwString at any specific site without raw `Finset.noncommProd` manipulation) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_anticomm_zero_two_fin_three` | first 3-site cross-site CAR: `c_0 ┬Ě c_2 + c_2 ┬Ě c_0 = 0` on `Fin 3` (using `jwString_succ_eq` to factor `jwString 2 2 = ¤â^z_0 ┬Ě ¤â^z_1`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_anticomm_zero_two_fin_three` | dual: `c_0ÔÇá ┬Ě c_2ÔÇá + c_2ÔÇá ┬Ě c_0ÔÇá = 0` on `Fin 3` (adjoint of the previous) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_creation_anticomm_zero_two_fin_three` | mixed: `c_0 ┬Ě c_2ÔÇá + c_2ÔÇá ┬Ě c_0 = 0` on `Fin 3` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_annihilation_anticomm_zero_two_fin_three` | mixed dual: `c_0ÔÇá ┬Ě c_2 + c_2 ┬Ě c_0ÔÇá = 0` on `Fin 3` (adjoint of the previous) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_anticomm_zero_two_general` | generalised to any N Ôëą 2: `c_0 ┬Ě c_2 + c_2 ┬Ě c_0 = 0` on `Fin (N+1)` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_anticomm_zero_two_general` | dual: `c_0ÔÇá ┬Ě c_2ÔÇá + c_2ÔÇá ┬Ě c_0ÔÇá = 0` for any N Ôëą 2 (adjoint) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_creation_anticomm_zero_two_general` | mixed: `c_0 ┬Ě c_2ÔÇá + c_2ÔÇá ┬Ě c_0 = 0` for any N Ôëą 2 | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_annihilation_anticomm_zero_two_general` | mixed dual: `c_0ÔÇá ┬Ě c_2 + c_2 ┬Ě c_0ÔÇá = 0` for any N Ôëą 2 (adjoint) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_anticomm_zero_pos` | **general cross-site CAR `{c_0, c_k} = 0`** for every `k : Fin (N+1)` with `0 < k.val` ÔÇö generalises the `_zero_one` / `_zero_two_general` specialisations. Proof: reduce to the anticommutator `{¤â^+_0, jwString N k}`, which vanishes by induction on the string length (base: `{¤â^+, ¤â^z} = 0` at site 0; step: `¤â^z_{k-1}` at site `k-1 Ôëá 0` commutes past `¤â^+_0`). | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_anticomm_zero_pos` | dual `{c_0ÔÇá, c_kÔÇá} = 0` for every `k : Fin (N+1)` with `0 < k.val` (adjoint of the above) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_creation_anticomm_zero_pos` | mixed `{c_0, c_kÔÇá} = 0` for every `k : Fin (N+1)` with `0 < k.val` ÔÇö same inductive argument on the JW string anticommutator (the site-`k` factor is `¤â^-_k` instead of `¤â^+_k`; JW-string part is unchanged) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_annihilation_anticomm_zero_pos` | mixed dual `{c_0ÔÇá, c_k} = 0` for every `k : Fin (N+1)` with `0 < k.val` (adjoint of the above) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `jwStringExceptAt` / `jwString_eq_onSite_mul_jwStringExceptAt` / `jwStringExceptAt_commute_onSite` | private factorisation helpers for the Jordan-Wigner string at an interior site (#210): for `i.val < j.val`, `jwString N j = onSite i pauliZ * jwStringExceptAt N j i`, and `jwStringExceptAt N j i` commutes with every single-site operator at site `i` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `jwString_anticomm_onSite_pos_spinHalfOpPlus` | operator-level anticommutator `{¤â^+_i, jwString N j} = 0` for every `i j : Fin (N+1)` with `i.val < j.val` ÔÇö generalises `jwString_anticomm_onSite_zero_spinHalfOpPlus` (i = 0 case) to arbitrary interior `i`; building block for the fully general cross-site CAR `{c_i, c_j} = 0` (#210) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `jwString_anticomm_onSite_pos_spinHalfOpMinus` | companion `{¤â^-_i, jwString N j} = 0` for every `i < j` (via `conjTranspose` of the `¤â^+` version) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `jwString_commute_jwString` | any two Jordan-Wigner strings `jwString N i` and `jwString N j` commute (both are noncommutative products of `¤â^z` over distinct sites) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_anticomm_lt` | **fully general cross-site CAR `{c_i, c_j} = 0` for `i < j`** (#210) on `Fin (N + 1)`. Proof: reduce via `jwString_anticomm_onSite_pos_spinHalfOpPlus` to an identity involving `JW_i ┬Ě JW_j = JW_j ┬Ě JW_i` (via `jwString_commute_jwString`), which makes the sum collapse | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_anticomm_lt` | dual `{c_iÔÇá, c_jÔÇá} = 0` for `i < j` (adjoint of the above) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_creation_anticomm_lt` | mixed `{c_i, c_jÔÇá} = 0` for `i < j` ÔÇö same structure as `_anticomm_lt` but with `¤â^-_j` at site `j` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_annihilation_anticomm_lt` | mixed dual `{c_iÔÇá, c_j} = 0` for `i < j` (adjoint of the above) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `spinHalfOpPlus_mul_self` / `spinHalfOpPlus_mul_spinHalfOpMinus_mul_spinHalfOpPlus` | Pauli helper identities `¤â^+ ¤â^+ = 0` and `¤â^+ ¤â^- ¤â^+ = ¤â^+` | `Quantum/SpinHalfBasis.lean` |
| `fermionMultiNumber_commutator_fermionMultiAnnihilation_self` | `[n_i, c_i] = -c_i` (number / annihilation commutator) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiNumber_commutator_fermionMultiCreation_self` | `[n_i, c_iÔÇá] = c_iÔÇá` (number / creation commutator, dual via adjoint) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `spinHalfOpMinus_mul_spinHalfOpPlus_commute_pauliZ` | matrix identity: `Commute (¤â^- ¤â^+) ¤â^z` (both diagonal in the computational basis) | `Quantum/SpinHalfBasis.lean` |
| `fermionMultiNumber_commute_fermionMultiAnnihilation_of_ne` | `Commute (n_i) (c_j)` for `i Ôëá j` ÔÇö the number operator at site `i` commutes with any annihilation at a different site, via the `n ¤â^z = ¤â^z n` matrix commutativity absorbing the JW-string `¤â^z_i` factor | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiNumber_commute_fermionMultiCreation_of_ne` | dual: `Commute (n_i) (c_jÔÇá)` for `i Ôëá j` via adjoint | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalNumber_commutator_fermionMultiAnnihilation` | `[N╠é, c_j] = -c_j` ÔÇö the total particle-number operator shifts annihilation down by one (sum of diagonal `[n_j, c_j] = -c_j` with vanishing off-diagonal terms) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalNumber_commutator_fermionMultiCreation` | dual: `[N╠é, c_jÔÇá] = c_jÔÇá` (via adjoint) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalNumber_commute_hopping` | `Commute N╠é (c_iÔÇá ┬Ě c_j)` ÔÇö the hopping operator preserves total particle number (shifts cancel: `[N╠é, c_iÔÇá] = c_iÔÇá` and `[N╠é, c_j] = -c_j`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiNumber_commute_fermionTotalNumber` | `Commute (n_i) (N╠é)` ÔÇö site occupation commutes with the total particle number (sum of pairwise commuting `[n_i, n_j] = 0`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionDensityDensity_commute_fermionTotalNumber` | `Commute (n_i ┬Ě n_j) (N╠é)` ÔÇö the density-density operator preserves total particle number, foundational for Hubbard-style on-site interactions | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionHopping`, `fermionHopping_commute_fermionTotalNumber` | the general single-particle hopping `H_hop = ╬ú_{i,j} t_{i,j} c_iÔÇá c_j` and the proof that it commutes with `N╠é` (charge conservation of the kinetic Hamiltonian) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionDensityInteraction`, `fermionDensityInteraction_commute_fermionTotalNumber` | the general densityÔÇôdensity interaction `V_int = ╬ú_{i,j} V_{i,j} n_i n_j` and the proof that it commutes with `N╠é` (paired with `H_hop` this gives charge conservation for any Hubbard-type Hamiltonian) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionGenericHamiltonian`, `fermionGenericHamiltonian_commute_fermionTotalNumber` | the canonical charge-conserving fermion Hamiltonian `H = H_hop + V_int` and the proof that `[H, N╠é] = 0`, the unified statement of charge conservation for single-species Hubbard / extended Hubbard models | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiNumber_mul_isHermitian` | `(n_i ┬Ě n_j)` is Hermitian for any sites (commuting Hermitian factors) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionDensityInteraction_isHermitian` | `V_int = ╬ú V_{ij} n_i n_j` is Hermitian when every coupling entry is real (`star V_{ij} = V_{ij}`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionHoppingTerm_conjTranspose` | `(c_iÔÇá ┬Ě c_j)ß┤┤ = c_jÔÇá ┬Ě c_i` (single hopping term) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionHopping_isHermitian` | `H_hop = ╬ú t_{ij} c_iÔÇá c_j` is Hermitian when `t` is Hermitian (`star (t i j) = t j i`); proved via term-wise conjTranspose + `Finset.sum_comm` for the index swap | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionGenericHamiltonian_isHermitian` | `H = H_hop + V_int` is Hermitian when `t` is Hermitian and `V` is entry-wise real; one-line corollary of the two summand Hermiticities via `Matrix.IsHermitian.add` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionGenericGibbsState N ╬▓ t V` | Gibbs state `gibbsState ╬▓ (H_hop + V_int)` for the Hubbard-skeleton Hamiltonian | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionGenericGibbsState_isHermitian` | Hermiticity (when `t` is Hermitian and `V` is real) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionGenericGibbsState_commute_hamiltonian` | `Commute ¤ü_╬▓ H` (always true for the Gibbs state of any operator with itself) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiVacuum N` | the JW vacuum on `Fin (N+1)` modes ÔÇö the all-up many-body basis vector `|ÔćĹÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_mulVec_vacuum` | every annihilation operator kills the vacuum: `(c_i).mulVec (fermionMultiVacuum N) = 0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionMultiNumber_mulVec_vacuum` | each `n_i ┬Ě |vacÔčę = 0` (since `n_i = c_iÔÇá c_i` and `c_i |vacÔčę = 0`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalNumber_mulVec_vacuum` | the vacuum is an `N╠é`-eigenstate of eigenvalue 0 | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionHopping_mulVec_vacuum` | `H_hop ┬Ě |vacÔčę = 0` (each `c_iÔÇá c_j |vacÔčę = c_iÔÇá 0 = 0`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionDensityInteraction_mulVec_vacuum` | `V_int ┬Ě |vacÔčę = 0` (each `n_i n_j |vacÔčę = n_i 0 = 0`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionGenericHamiltonian_mulVec_vacuum` | `H ┬Ě |vacÔčę = 0` for the full Hubbard skeleton (linearity) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalNumber_mulVec_singleParticle` | `c_iÔÇá |vacÔčę` is an `N╠é`-eigenstate of eigenvalue 1 (uses `[N╠é, c_iÔÇá] = c_iÔÇá` and `N╠é |vacÔčę = 0`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalNumber_mulVec_twoParticle` | `c_iÔÇá c_jÔÇá |vacÔčę` is an `N╠é`-eigenstate of eigenvalue 2 (Leibniz on the commutator gives `[N╠é, c_iÔÇá c_jÔÇá] = 2 c_iÔÇá c_jÔÇá`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalNumber_mulVec_eigenstate_of_commute` | generic charge-eigenstate helper: if `[N╠é, X] = ╬▒ X` and `N╠é v = 0` then `N╠é (X v) = ╬▒ (X v)`; abstracts the single- and two-particle constructions | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `spinfulIndex N i ¤â` | bijection `(i, ¤â : Fin 2) ÔćŽ 2 * i + ¤â Ôłł Fin (2*N+2)`, embedding two-species data into a single-species JW chain | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `spinfulIndex_eq_iff`, `exists_spinfulIndex` | shared injectivity (`spinfulIndex N a r = spinfulIndex N b s Ôćö a = b Ôłž r = s`) and decomposition (`Ôłâ a r, k = spinfulIndex N a r`) of the spinful index | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionUpAnnihilation`, `fermionDownAnnihilation`, `fermionUpCreation`, `fermionDownCreation` | spinful annihilation / creation operators as wrappers around the underlying single-species operators at `2i` (up) and `2i+1` (down) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionUpNumber`, `fermionDownNumber` | spinful site-occupation numbers `n_{i,ÔćĹ}`, `n_{i,Ôćô}` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardOnSiteInteraction N U` | the on-site Hubbard interaction `H_int = U ╬ú_i n_{i,ÔćĹ} ┬Ě n_{i,Ôćô}` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardOnSiteInteraction_commute_fermionTotalNumber` | `[H_int, N╠é] = 0` (charge conservation) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardOnSiteInteraction_isHermitian` | `H_int` is Hermitian when `U` is real (`star U = U`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardKinetic N t` | the spinful tight-binding kinetic operator `T = ╬ú_{¤â} ╬ú_{i,j} t_{i,j} c_{i,¤â}ÔÇá c_{j,¤â}` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardKinetic_commute_fermionTotalNumber` | `[T, N╠é] = 0` (charge conservation of the kinetic operator) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardKinetic_isHermitian` | `T` is Hermitian when `t` is a Hermitian matrix (`star (t i j) = t j i`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardHamiltonian N t U` | the canonical (single-band) Hubbard Hamiltonian `H = T + U ╬ú n_{iÔćĹ} n_{iÔćô}` on `Fin (2N+2)` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardHamiltonian_commute_fermionTotalNumber` | `[H, N╠é] = 0` (charge conservation) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardHamiltonian_isHermitian` | `H` is Hermitian when `t` is Hermitian and `U` is real | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardGibbsState N ╬▓ t U` | the Hubbard Gibbs state `gibbsState ╬▓ H_Hubbard` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardGibbsState_isHermitian` | Hermiticity (Hermitian `t`, real `U`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardGibbsState_commute_hamiltonian` | `Commute ¤ü_╬▓ H_Hubbard` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalUpNumber`, `fermionTotalDownNumber` | spinful conserved charges `N_ÔćĹ = ╬ú_i n_{iÔćĹ}`, `N_Ôćô = ╬ú_i n_{iÔćô}` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinZ` | total spin polarisation `S^z_tot = (1/2)(N_ÔćĹ Ôłĺ N_Ôćô)` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalUpNumber_commute_fermionTotalDownNumber` | `[N_ÔćĹ, N_Ôćô] = 0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalUpNumber_commute_fermionTotalNumber` / `fermionTotalDownNumber_commute_fermionTotalNumber` | `[N_ÔćĹ, N╠é] = [N_Ôćô, N╠é] = 0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinZ_commute_fermionTotalNumber` | `[S^z_tot, N╠é] = 0` (spin polarisation commutes with total number) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalUpNumber_commute_hubbardOnSiteInteraction` / `fermionTotalDownNumber_commute_hubbardOnSiteInteraction` | `[N_ÔćĹ, H_int] = [N_Ôćô, H_int] = 0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinZ_commute_hubbardOnSiteInteraction` | `[S^z_tot, H_int] = 0` (free corollary) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionUpAnnihilation_mulVec_vacuum` / `fermionDownAnnihilation_mulVec_vacuum` | every spinful annihilation kills the JW vacuum | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionUpNumber_mulVec_vacuum` / `fermionDownNumber_mulVec_vacuum` | each spinful site number kills the vacuum | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalUpNumber_mulVec_vacuum` / `fermionTotalDownNumber_mulVec_vacuum` | `N_ÔćĹ ┬Ě |vacÔčę = N_Ôćô ┬Ě |vacÔčę = 0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinZ_mulVec_vacuum` | `S^z_tot ┬Ě |vacÔčę = 0` (the vacuum is unpolarised) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardKinetic_mulVec_vacuum` / `hubbardOnSiteInteraction_mulVec_vacuum` / `hubbardHamiltonian_mulVec_vacuum` | each annihilates the vacuum (so `|vacÔčę` is a 0-energy / 0-particle eigenstate) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `spinfulIndex_up_ne_down` | the up-channel position `2 i` is never the down-channel position `2 j + 1` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalDownNumber_commute_fermionUp{Creation,Annihilation,Number}` and the dual `fermionTotalUpNumber_commute_fermionDown{Creation,Annihilation,Number}` | the spinful number on one species commutes with every operator of the other species (different JW positions) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `fermionTotalDownNumber_commute_upHopping` / `fermionTotalUpNumber_commute_downHopping` | the spinful same-¤â hopping term `c_{i¤â}ÔÇá c_{j¤â}` commutes with the opposite-spin total number `N_{¤â'Ôëá¤â}` (cross-spin half of `[H_kinetic, N_¤â] = 0`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |

#### Hubbard spin symmetry ÔÇö full SU(2) invariance (Tasaki ┬ž9.3.3)

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `fermionTotalUpNumber_isHermitian` / `fermionTotalDownNumber_isHermitian` | `N_ÔćĹ` and `N_Ôćô` are Hermitian (sum of Hermitian number operators) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionTotalUpNumber_commutator_fermionUpCreation` | `[N_ÔćĹ, cÔÇá_{i,ÔćĹ}] = cÔÇá_{i,ÔćĹ}` (up-spin sub-chain analogue of `[N╠é, cÔÇá_i] = cÔÇá_i`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionTotalDownNumber_commutator_fermionDownCreation` | `[N_Ôćô, cÔÇá_{i,Ôćô}] = cÔÇá_{i,Ôćô}` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionTotalUpNumber_commute_upHopping` | `[N_ÔćĹ, cÔÇá_{i,ÔćĹ} c_{j,ÔćĹ}] = 0` (same-species hopping preserves spin-up count) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionTotalDownNumber_commute_downHopping` | `[N_Ôćô, cÔÇá_{i,Ôćô} c_{j,Ôćô}] = 0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionTotalUpNumber_commute_hubbardKinetic` / `fermionTotalDownNumber_commute_hubbardKinetic` | `[N_ÔćĹ, H_kin] = [N_Ôćô, H_kin] = 0` (each spin species conserved by kinetic term) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionTotalUpNumber_commute_hubbardHamiltonian` | `[N_ÔćĹ, H] = 0` (Tasaki ┬ž9.3.3, eq. (9.3.35)) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionTotalDownNumber_commute_hubbardHamiltonian` | `[N_Ôćô, H] = 0` (Tasaki ┬ž9.3.3, eq. (9.3.35)) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinZ_commute_hubbardHamiltonian` | `[S^z_tot, H] = 0` (Tasaki ┬ž9.3.3, p. 333) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinPlus` / `fermionTotalSpinMinus` | `┼ť^+_tot = ╬ú_i cÔÇá_{i,ÔćĹ}c_{i,Ôćô}`, `┼ť^-_tot = (┼ť^+_tot)ÔÇá` ÔÇö SU(2) raising/lowering operators (Tasaki ┬ž9.3.3, p. 332) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinPlus_conjTranspose` | `(┼ť^+_tot)ÔÇá = ┼ť^-_tot` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionUpAnnihilation_commutator_fermionTotalSpinPlus` | `[c_{j,ÔćĹ}, ┼ť^+_tot] = c_{j,Ôćô}` (Tasaki ┬ž9.3.3, eq. (9.3.36)) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionDownCreation_commutator_fermionTotalSpinPlus` | `[cÔÇá_{j,Ôćô}, ┼ť^+_tot] = ÔłĺcÔÇá_{j,ÔćĹ}` (Tasaki ┬ž9.3.3, eq. (9.3.36)) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionUpCreation_commute_fermionTotalSpinPlus` / `fermionDownAnnihilation_commute_fermionTotalSpinPlus` | `[cÔÇá_{i,ÔćĹ}, ┼ť^+_tot] = 0` and `[c_{j,Ôćô}, ┼ť^+_tot] = 0` (Tasaki ┬ž9.3.3, eq. (9.3.36)) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinPlus_commute_hubbardHamiltonian` | `[┼ť^+_tot, H] = 0` (Tasaki ┬ž9.3.3, eq. (9.3.35)) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinMinus_commute_hubbardHamiltonian` | `[┼ť^-_tot, H] = 0` (Tasaki ┬ž9.3.3, eq. (9.3.35), proved by adjoint) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SpinSymmetry.lean` |

#### Hubbard all-up-spin state and saturated ferromagnetism (Tasaki ┬ž11.1.1)

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `hubbardAllUpState N` | fully spin-polarised basis vector: all spin-up orbitals occupied, spin-down empty (even JW indices = 1, odd = 0) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/AllUpState.lean` |
| `fermionUpNumber_mulVec_allUpState` | `n_{i,ÔćĹ} ┬Ě |ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę = |ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę` ÔÇö each spin-up number operator acts as identity on the all-up state | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/AllUpState.lean` |
| `fermionDownNumber_mulVec_allUpState` | `n_{i,Ôćô} ┬Ě |ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę = 0` ÔÇö no spin-down electrons; key to the vanishing of `H_int` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/AllUpState.lean` |
| `hubbardOnSiteInteraction_mulVec_allUpState` | `H_int ┬Ě |ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę = 0` ÔÇö no double occupancy in the fully-polarised state (Tasaki ┬ž11.1.1, p. 373; eq. (10.1.5), p. 344) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/AllUpState.lean` |
| `hubbardHamiltonian_mulVec_allUpState` | `H ┬Ě |ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę = H_hop ┬Ě |ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę` ÔÇö the Hubbard model in the all-up sector reduces to a non-interacting hopping problem | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/AllUpState.lean` |
| `fermionDownAnnihilation_mulVec_allUpState` | `c_{i,Ôćô} ┬Ě |ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę = 0` ÔÇö spin-down annihilation kills the all-up state (odd JW index unoccupied, so ¤âÔü║ maps it to 0) (Tasaki ┬ž11.1.1, p. 373) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/AllUpState.lean` |
| `fermionUpCreation_mulVec_allUpState` | `cÔÇá_{i,ÔćĹ} ┬Ě |ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę = 0` ÔÇö spin-up creation kills the all-up state (even JW index already occupied, so ¤âÔü╗ maps it to 0) (Tasaki ┬ž11.1.1, p. 373) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/AllUpState.lean` |
| `hubbardKinetic_mulVec_allUpState` | `H_hop ┬Ě |ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę = (╬ú_i t i i) ÔÇó |ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę` ÔÇö hopping eigenvalue: off-diagonal terms vanish by CAR anticommutation, diagonal terms give 1 each (Tasaki ┬ž11.1.1, p. 373) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/AllUpState.lean` |
| `hubbardHamiltonian_mulVec_allUpState_eigenstate` | `H ┬Ě |ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę = (╬ú_i t i i) ÔÇó |ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę` ÔÇö full Hamiltonian eigenstate: combines `H_hop` eigenvalue and `H_int ┬Ě |ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę = 0` (Tasaki ┬ž11.1.1, p. 373; eq. (10.1.5), p. 344) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/AllUpState.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinSquared` | total-spin Casimir `(┼ť_tot)┬▓ = ┼ťÔü╗┼ťÔü║ + ┼ť_z(┼ť_z+1)` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SaturatedFerromagnetism.lean` |
| `fermionTotalUpNumber_mulVec_allUpState` | `N_ÔćĹ ┬Ě |ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę = (N+1) ÔÇó |ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SaturatedFerromagnetism.lean` |
| `fermionTotalDownNumber_mulVec_allUpState` | `N_Ôćô ┬Ě |ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę = 0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SaturatedFerromagnetism.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinZ_mulVec_allUpState` | `┼ť^z_tot ┬Ě |ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę = ((N+1)/2) ÔÇó |ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SaturatedFerromagnetism.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinPlus_mulVec_allUpState` | `┼ťÔü║_tot ┬Ě |ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę = 0` ÔÇö highest-weight state; no down-spin to raise | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SaturatedFerromagnetism.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinSquared_mulVec_allUpState` | `(┼ť_tot)┬▓ ┬Ě |ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę = S_max(S_max+1) ÔÇó |ÔćĹÔÇŽÔćĹÔčę` where `S_max = (N+1)/2` (Tasaki ┬ž11.1.1, p. 372) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SaturatedFerromagnetism.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinSquared_commute_hubbardHamiltonian` | `[(┼ť_tot)┬▓, H] = 0` ÔÇö Casimir commutes with H (from SU(2) invariance, Tasaki ┬ž9.3.3) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SaturatedFerromagnetism.lean` |
| `isSaturatedFerromagnet` | **Definition 11.1** ÔÇö Lean predicate: there exists a ground-state energy `EÔéÇ` such that every nonzero `H`-eigenvector with eigenvalue `EÔéÇ` is a `(┼ť_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector with eigenvalue `S_max(S_max+1)` (Tasaki ┬ž11.1.1, p. 372) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SaturatedFerromagnetism.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinZ_commutator_fermionTotalSpinMinus` | `[┼ť^z_tot, ┼ť^-_tot] = -┼ť^-_tot` ÔÇö SU(2) algebra relation; follows from site-wise `[┼ť_z, cÔÇá_{i,Ôćô}c_{i,ÔćĹ}] = -(cÔÇá_{i,Ôćô}c_{i,ÔćĹ})` (Tasaki ┬ž9.3.3, p. 332) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SaturatedFerromagnetism.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinMinus_mulVec_preserves_hamiltonian_eigenvalue` | if `H┬Ěv = E┬Ěv` then `H┬Ě(┼ť^-┬Ěv) = E┬Ě(┼ť^-┬Ěv)` ÔÇö applying `┼ť^-` preserves Hamiltonian eigenvalues; follows from `[┼ť^-, H] = 0` (Tasaki ┬ž11.1.1, p. 373) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SaturatedFerromagnetism.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinZ_mulVec_spinMinus_step` | if `┼ť_z┬Ěv = m┬Ěv` then `┼ť_z┬Ě(┼ť^-┬Ěv) = (m-1)┬Ě(┼ť^-┬Ěv)` ÔÇö applying `┼ť^-` decrements `┼ť_z` eigenvalue by 1; follows from `[┼ť^z, ┼ť^-] = -┼ť^-` (Tasaki ┬ž2.4, eq. (2.4.9); ┬ž11.1.1, p. 373) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/SaturatedFerromagnetism.lean` |

#### Hubbard hard-core subspace (Tasaki ┬ž11.2)

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `hubbardDoubleOccupancy N i` | same-site Hubbard double-occupancy operator `n_{i,ÔćĹ} n_{i,Ôćô}` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreSubspace.lean` |
| `hubbardHardcoreSubspace N` | linear subspace of vectors annihilated by every same-site double-occupancy operator, the no-double-occupancy sector used as unnumbered infrastructure for Tasaki Theorems 11.5 and 11.7 (1st ed., ┬ž11.2, pp. 381-388) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreSubspace.lean` |
| `mem_hubbardHardcoreSubspace_iff` | membership in `hubbardHardcoreSubspace` is equivalent to vanishing of all `hubbardDoubleOccupancy N i` actions | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreSubspace.lean` |
| `hubbardDoubleOccupancy_mulVec_eq_zero_of_mem_hardcore` | every `hubbardDoubleOccupancy N i` annihilates each hard-core vector | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreSubspace.lean` |
| `hubbardOnSiteInteraction_mulVec_eq_zero_of_mem_hardcore` / `hubbardOnSiteInteraction_apply_eq_zero_of_mem_hardcore` | the on-site interaction `U ╬ú_i n_{i,ÔćĹ} n_{i,Ôćô}` annihilates every hard-core vector, both as a vector equation and pointwise | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreSubspace.lean` |

#### Hubbard hard-core projection (Tasaki ┬ž11.2)

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `hubbardHardcoreFactor N i` | single-site hard-core factor `1 - n_{i,ÔćĹ} n_{i,Ôćô}` at spinful site `i` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean` |
| `hubbardHardcoreFactor_mul_self` | each hard-core factor is idempotent | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean` |
| `hubbardDoubleOccupancy_mul_hardcoreFactor` | `n_{i,ÔćĹ} n_{i,Ôćô} ┬Ě (1 - n_{i,ÔćĹ} n_{i,Ôćô}) = 0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean` |
| `hubbardHardcoreFactor_commute` | hard-core factors at any two sites commute | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean` |
| `hubbardDoubleOccupancy_isHermitian` / `hubbardHardcoreFactor_isHermitian` | the double-occupancy operator and each hard-core factor are Hermitian | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean` |
| `hubbardHardcoreFactor_mulVec_eq_self_of_mem` | every hard-core factor fixes each hard-core vector | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean` |
| `hubbardHardcoreProjection N` | hard-core projection `P╠é_hc = ÔłĆ_i (1 - n_{i,ÔćĹ} n_{i,Ôćô})`, the non-commutative product of pairwise-commuting hard-core factors, unnumbered infrastructure for Tasaki Theorems 11.5 and 11.7 (1st ed., ┬ž11.2, pp. 381-388) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean` |
| `hubbardHardcoreProjection_mul_self` | the hard-core projection is idempotent | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean` |
| `hubbardHardcoreProjection_isHermitian` | the hard-core projection is Hermitian | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean` |
| `hubbardDoubleOccupancy_mul_hardcoreProjection` | every same-site double-occupancy operator annihilates `P╠é_hc`: `n_{j,ÔćĹ} n_{j,Ôćô} ┬Ě P╠é_hc = 0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean` |
| `hubbardHardcoreProjection_mulVec_eq_self_of_mem` | `P╠é_hc` fixes every hard-core vector | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean` |
| `hubbardHardcoreProjection_mulVec_mem` | `P╠é_hc ┬Ě ¤ł` always lies in `hubbardHardcoreSubspace` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean` |

#### Hubbard one-hole hard-core basis states (Tasaki ┬ž11.2)

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `hubbardOneHoleConfig N x ¤â` | occupation configuration with a hole at site `x` and spin `¤â` (`true = ÔćĹ`) on every other site | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreBasis.lean` |
| `hubbardOneHoleConfig_apply_up` / `hubbardOneHoleConfig_apply_down` | the up- / down-orbital occupation values of that configuration at each site | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreBasis.lean` |
| `hubbardHardcoreBasisState N x ¤â` | one-hole hard-core basis state `\|╬Ž_{x,¤â}Ôčę`, the computational basis vector of `hubbardOneHoleConfig N x ¤â` (Tasaki ┬ž11.2, eq. (11.2.3); 1st ed., pp. 381-388) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreBasis.lean` |
| `hubbardHardcoreBasisState_mem_hardcoreSubspace` | every basis state lies in `hubbardHardcoreSubspace` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreBasis.lean` |
| `hubbardHardcoreProjection_mulVec_basisState` | the hard-core projection fixes every basis state | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreBasis.lean` |
| `hubbardHardcoreBasisState_inner` | orthonormality: `ÔčĘ╬Ž_{x,¤â} \| ╬Ž_{x',¤â'}Ôčę = 1` iff their configurations coincide, else `0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreBasis.lean` |
| `hubbardHardcoreBasisState_self_inner` | each basis state is normalised (self-overlap `1`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreBasis.lean` |

#### JordanÔÇôWigner string action on basis states (Tasaki ┬ž11.2 infrastructure)

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `onSite_pauliZ_mulVec_basisVec` | `¤â^z_j ┬Ě \|cÔčę = (-1)^{c j} \|cÔčę` (single `¤â^z` acts by the parity sign at `j`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/StringBasisVecAction.lean` |
| `jwString_mulVec_basisVec` | `jwString N i ┬Ě \|cÔčę = (ÔłĆ_{j<i} (-1)^{c j}) \|cÔčę` (the JW string acts by the fermion-parity sign of the occupied modes below `i`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/StringBasisVecAction.lean` |
| `jwSign N j c` | the JW string sign `ÔłĆ_{k<j} (-1)^{c k}` of a configuration | `Fermion/JordanWigner/AnnihilationCreationBasisVec.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnnihilation_mulVec_basisVec` | `c_j \|cÔčę = jwSign N j c ÔÇó \|c with jÔćŽ0Ôčę` if `c j = 1`, else `0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/AnnihilationCreationBasisVec.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_mulVec_basisVec` | `cÔÇá_j \|cÔčę = jwSign N j c ÔÇó \|c with jÔćŽ1Ôčę` if `c j = 0`, else `0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/AnnihilationCreationBasisVec.lean` |
| `fermionMultiCreation_mul_Annihilation_mulVec_basisVec` | a single hop `cÔÇá_p c_q \|cÔčę` = `(jwSign┬ĚjwSign) ÔÇó \|c with qÔćŽ0, pÔćŽ1Ôčę` if `c q = 1` and the intermediate config is empty at `p`, else `0` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/HopBasisVec.lean` |
| `jwSign_zero_config` / `fermionMultiCreation_mulVec_vacuum_eq_basisVec` | the string sign of the vacuum is `1`; `cÔÇá_j \|vacÔčę = \|single electron at jÔčę` (base case for the ordered-`cÔÇá` Tasaki basis (11.2.3)) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/VacuumCreationBasisVec.lean` |

#### Span of the one-hole hard-core sector (Tasaki ┬ž11.2, footnote 8)

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `IsOneHoleHardcoreConfig N c` | a configuration is one-hole hard-core: no double occupancy and exactly one empty site (the hole) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreSpan.lean` |
| `hubbardOneHoleConfig_isOneHoleHardcore` | each parametrized configuration `hubbardOneHoleConfig N x ¤â` is one-hole hard-core | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreSpan.lean` |
| `exists_eq_hubbardOneHoleConfig_of_isOneHoleHardcore` | surjectivity: every one-hole hard-core configuration equals `hubbardOneHoleConfig N x ¤â` for some `(x, ¤â)` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreSpan.lean` |
| `hubbardOneHoleHardcoreSector N` | the one-hole hard-core sector `H_hc^N` (span of the one-hole hard-core basis configurations) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreSpan.lean` |

| `hubbardHardcoreBasisState_mem_sector` | each basis state lies in the one-hole hard-core sector | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreSpan.lean` |

#### Hole-filling hop configuration (Tasaki ┬ž11.2, eq. (11.2.4) spatial content)

| `hubbardOneHoleConfig_hop` | filling the hole `x` with an electron of spin `s` hopped from `y` turns the configuration of `\|╬Ž_{x,¤â}Ôčę` into that of `\|╬Ž_{y, ¤â_{yÔćĺx}}Ôčę` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HopConfig.lean` |
| `hubbardHop_mulVec_hardcoreBasisState` | operator content of (11.2.4): `cÔÇá_{(x,s)} c_{(y,s)} \|╬Ž_{x,¤â}Ôčę = (jwSign┬ĚjwSign) ÔÇó \|╬Ž_{y, ¤â_{yÔćĺx}}Ôčę` (create at hole `x`, annihilate at occupied `y`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HopAction.lean` |
| `hubbardHopTerm_inner_hardcoreBasisState` | per-term content of (11.2.5): the matrix element `ÔčĘ╬Ž_{y,¤ä}\| cÔÇá_{(x,s)} c_{(y,s)} \|╬Ž_{x,¤â}Ôčę` is the JW sign product times the indicator `¤ä = ¤â_{yÔćĺx}` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HopMatrixElement.lean` |

#### Hubbard effective Hamiltonian on the hard-core sector (Tasaki ┬ž11.2)

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `hubbardEffectiveHamiltonian N t U` | the hard-core compression `─Ą_eff = P╠é_hc ┬Ě H ┬Ě P╠é_hc` of the full Hubbard Hamiltonian (Tasaki ┬ž11.2; 1st ed., pp. 381-388) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/EffectiveHamiltonian.lean` |

#### Weak Nagaoka spin multiplet (Tasaki ┬ž11.2.1, Theorem 11.5 core)

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `fermionTotalSpinPlus_commutator_fermionTotalSpinMinus` | SU(2) ladder commutator `[┼ť^+_tot, ┼ť^-_tot] = 2 ┼ť^z_tot` (Tasaki ┬ž9.3.3) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/WeakNagaokaTheorem.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinSquared_commute_fermionTotalSpinMinus` | `[(┼ť_tot)┬▓, ┼ť^-_tot] = 0` ÔÇö the Casimir is constant along the spin-lowering tower | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/WeakNagaokaTheorem.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinPlus_mul_fermionTotalSpinMinus` | `┼ť^+_tot ┼ť^-_tot = (┼ť_tot)┬▓ Ôłĺ ┼ť^z_tot(┼ť^z_tot Ôłĺ 1)` ÔÇö raising-after-lowering via the Casimir | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/WeakNagaokaTheorem.lean` |
| `fermionTotalSpinZ_mulVec_spinMinusPow` / `fermionTotalSpinSquared_mulVec_spinMinusPow` | `┼ť^z` / Casimir towers: `(┼ť^-_tot)^k v` has `┼ť^z = N/2 Ôłĺ k` and constant Casimir, for any highest-weight `v` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/WeakNagaokaTheorem.lean` |
| `spinMinusPow_ne_zero` / `spinMinusPow_linearIndependent` | the `N+1` lowered states `(┼ť^-_tot)^k v` (`k ÔëĄ N`) are nonzero (ladder eigenvalue `(k+1)(NÔłĺk) Ôëá 0`) and linearly independent (distinct `┼ť^z`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/WeakNagaokaTheorem.lean` |
| `weakNagaoka_spinMultiplet` | **Theorem 11.5 core**: a nonzero highest-weight `─Ą_eff`-eigenvector `v` (`┼ť^+_tot v = 0`, `┼ť^z_tot v = (N/2) v`) generates `N+1 = 2 S_max + 1` linearly independent `─Ą_eff`-eigenvectors `(┼ť^-_tot)^k v` at the same energy, all with `S_tot = S_max = N/2`. | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/WeakNagaokaTheorem.lean` |
| `tasakiEffMatrix` / `tasakiEffMatrixUp` | the Tasaki matrix `M = Tß┤┤ ─Ą_eff T` of `─Ą_eff` in the one-hole basis (Hermitian, entries `ÔčĘ╬Ž_q\|─Ą_eff\|╬Ž_pÔčę`) and its all-up principal block `M_ÔćĹ` (Hermitian); `─Ą_eff` preserves the all-up sector | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/WeakNagaokaGroundState.lean` |
| `hubbardEffectiveHamiltonian_mulVec_tasakiState` / `_tasakiExpansion` | operator lift: `─Ą_eff ╬Ž_p = ╬ú_q ÔčĘ╬Ž_q\|─Ą_eff\|╬Ž_pÔčę ╬Ž_q` and `─Ą_eff (╬ú c_q ╬Ž_q) = ╬ú_q (M c)_q ╬Ž_q` (via particle-number conservation, sector completeness `tasaki_completeness`) | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/WeakNagaokaGroundState.lean` |
| `weakNagaoka_theorem_11_5` | **Tasaki Theorem 11.5 (weak Nagaoka, effective one-hole sector)**: there exist `N+1 = 2 S_max+1` linearly independent `─Ą_eff`-eigenvectors at the maximal-spin sector minimum energy, all with `S_tot = S_max = N/2`; the ground state is the all-up state from a minimum eigenvector of the all-up block `M_ÔćĹ` (no PerronÔÇôFrobenius). | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/WeakNagaokaGroundState.lean` |
| `weakNagaoka_theorem_11_5_global` | **Tasaki Theorem 11.5 (global form)**: for `t Ôëą 0` symmetric, the all-up minimum equals the global one-hole minimum (`hermitianMinEigenvalue M_ÔćĹ = hermitianMinEigenvalue M`, via the Schwarz bound (11.2.9) ferromagnetization on the real Tasaki matrix), so the `N+1` degenerate eigenvectors are genuine **ground states** with `S_tot = S_max` | `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/WeakNagaokaGlobalMin.lean` |
| `mulVec_eq_smul_of_rayleighOnVec_eq_min` | Hermitian variational equality: a nonzero vector attaining the minimum-eigenvalue Rayleigh bound is a minimum eigenvector (companion to the variational lower bound) | `Quantum/SpinS/HermitianVariationalEquality.lean` |
